Results
The status of the imported .zip file, along with the name and description, if any, of the upload, is displayed in the
Bulk Import Contracts Status  page. Y ou can click the View Details  link next to a file to open the Details of Bulk
Contracts Import  page and view the details of the bulk import operation.
The contract record mapping details are reflected  in the Integrated Systems  section in the respective contract
workspace. T o verify the mapping details, in the Contract Workspace (Procurement)  page, in the Overview  tab, go
to the Integrated Systems  section and view the following information:
•System Name : The integrated system name is displayed. For example, Icertis.
•Type : The type of document in the external system is displayed. Possible values: Agreement, Amendment.
•External ID : The Agreement ID of the integrated system is displayed as a hyperlink. Click the hyperlink to
navigate to the agreement in Icertis.
•Status : The status of the Icertis agreement.

Deleting or Deactivating Line Items in a Contract
Line Items Document Using Excel Import
Use this procedure to delete or deactivate line items in a Contract Line Items Document (CLID) using the Excel
Import functionality.
Prerequisites
•Y our administrator must enable the Allow Line Item Deletion Through Excel Import
(Application.ACM.AllowLineItemDeletionThroughExcelImport) ICM parameter. For information about this
parameter, see Allow Deleting Line Items Through Excel Import .
•Y ou must have atleast one line item in the CLID that you want to modify.
•(Optional) Y our site must have SAP Ariba Contracts integrated with ICI. For information about integrating SAP
Ariba Contracts with ICI, see Integrating SAP Ariba Contracts with Icertis Contract Intelligence for SAP Ariba
Solutions [page 4] .
Context
As a buyer, to delete or deactivate line items present in the CLID of a contract workspace, you can create an Excel
file that contains the required information and import the data using the Excel Import  functionality.
Procedure
1. Open the contract workspace that contains the CLID you want to modify.
Integrating SAP Ariba Contracts with Icertis Contract Intelligence for SAP Ariba Solutions
Integrating SAP Ariba Contracts with Icertis Contract Intelligence for SAP Ariba
Solutions PUBLIC 31

2. Open the CLID.
The CLID details page is displayed.
3. Edit the Excel version of the CLID to prepare the data to import.
For information about editing the Excel version of a CLID, see Editing Excel Versions of Contract Line Items
Documents .
In the Contract Item Information  worksheet, enter a specific  value in the Item Status  column for the line
items that you want to delete or deactivate. T o delete a line item, enter HardDeleted . T o deactivate a line item,
enter Deleted .
 Note
For hierarchical line items that have child line items, make note of the following:
Item Status (Parent Line Item) Item Status (Child Line Item) Result
Deleted Deleted  or no entry The parent and child line items are de-
activated.
HardDeleted HardDeleted  or no entry The parent and child line items are de-
leted.
Deleted HardDeleted The parent line item is deactivated
and child line item is deleted.
HardDeleted Deleted The parent and child line items are de-
leted.
4. Return to the Import Content from Excel  page and select Update Document  under Select how you would
like to import the content .
5. Click Choose file and select the updated Excel file.
6. Click Import .
Next Steps
On the CLID details page, verify that the updates to the line items are as expected.
 Note
Deleted line items are not shown in the line items table on the CLID details page. Deactivated line items are
highlighted in grey in the line items table and are not editable.

 Award
 Award scenarios

Ok to proceed with the contract creation. Another popup appears with a disclaimer message.
4. Click Ok on the popup.
Y ou are redirected to the ICI user interface to create an agreement. The Create Agreement  page appears for
the first  step of the agreement creation process.

3
Procurement has to do more with less
At a global level, procurement leaders have navigated challenge after challenge over the last few years. From supply chain 
disruption to inflation, working with suppliers and finding the best pricing has been tough. 
There’s still a lot of work to be done — and you might not have the staff to do it. Thirty-eight percent (38%) of leaders in our 
most recent Voices of Sourcing report are dealing with a declining or flat workforce. It’s a worrisome trend: Gartner research 
showed that while 43% of procurement leaders thought they had adequate talent available to meet their needs today, only 
14% thought they had the talent to meet their future needs.The opportunity procurement 
leaders have been waiting for
Are Procurement Teams Built for the Future?Leaders who create a vision outperform 
their peers in other organizations by 25%
Source: 2023 Gartner Procurement CompetenciesLeading the charge within their organizations for more 
efficiency and recognizing the importance of digital 
transformation to achieve large-scale business goals.
If you want to be one of the leaders with a seat at the 
table, you must rethink how to deliver value to the entire 
organization. Automation, once considered a nice-to-have, 
has become a must-have — and the only way for you to 
meet your efficiency and strategic goals. 
Your procurement team has to become more efficient with 
sourcing to help control your organization’s spending and 
create more time for high-value strategic work. To gain 
this efficiency, you have to embrace automation, establish 
sourcing best practices within your team, and then scale 
those practices to the rest of your organization.For too long, procurement teams have been seen as the 
penny pinchers or the slow-things-downers of the enterprise. 
But today, procurement leaders are finally starting to play a 
more strategic role in company leadership and can collaborate 
with their peers to create bigger wins for their businesses.
 
A recent Deloitte report describes procurement leaders who 
create a vision for their role in the business and inspire others 
to join in.

4
A talent shortage means an ever-growing mountain 
of work for existing procurement teams. It’s no surprise, 
then, that 68% of respondents also said they plan to 
focus on cost management and operational efficiency 
to manage these workloads. 
Fifty-nine percent (59%) of leaders told us they’re looking for advanced tech, such as automation, to drive the efficiency 
they’re looking for. If you want to stay ahead of the competition, you’ll have to find opportunities for change on an enterprise 
level, not just within your procurement team. Autonomous sourcing can deliver the efficiency you need throughout your 
organization — and a whole lot more. 
What autonomous sourcing is — and 
what it means to your business
You might feel bombarded by discussions of autonomous sourcing — and still a little confused about what exactly it can do 
for your team. Many companies are using the term to refer to various levels of automation in different parts of the sourcing 
process, but it’s much more than that. 
  
Applying autonomous sourcing to even the most complex sourcing event will free up your procurement professionals’ time 
for higher-value tasks, like cultivating stronger supplier relationships for better terms and pricing down the line.
You can balance dozens of factors automatically and create clearer options that let you make the most informed decisions. 
For instance, autonomous sourcing can present separate scenarios that optimize for different factors like location, pricing, 
or speed, and the user can decide which is most important for that event before awarding the bid. 
63% of procurement leaders in our 
study said increasing workloads were 
their biggest concern.
Autonomous sourcing helps organizations automate the sourcing process from start to finish while still 
allowing precise control and human decision-making where necessary. It lets sourcing teams easily 
design hands-free automated workflows (executed by bots) that remove the manual, time-consuming 
steps of the sourcing process and reduce it down to just the time it takes suppliers to submit bids.

5
Autonomous sourcing will automatically  
consider factors like:  
• Supplier diversity
• Environmental, social, and corporate governance
• Sustainability
• Risk mitigation 
• Nearshoring
• On-time delivery performance
• Load acceptance percentage
For tactical and tail spend, you can realize tremendous savings by automating processes that have previously been unmanaged 
or under-managed. Business users will get the best pricing from the right suppliers without being burdened by the ins and outs 
of procurement.
The benefits of autonomous sourcing
The bottom line in autonomous sourcing is efficiency. Business users no longer need to wait for procurement’s help or stumble 
through a sourcing event on their own. Every purchase is simple, seamless, and successful. You’ll be able to:
• Free up time for more strategic work for your procurement team by automating 100% of the sourcing process.
• Work with a single source of truth for every sourcing event, building a more powerful data engine for future events with 
every use.
• Automate the simple — empower business users to source on their own with best practices built right in.
• Uncover significant savings by implementing a competitive bidding process across all spending.
• Make faster decisions throughout your business with quicker procurement processes. 
Your procurement team will feel more satisfied in their roles because they’re doing fewer mundane, manual tasks and can 
do more fulfilling work instead. They also won’t spend their time getting pulled in different directions — they’ll be able to 
proactively get ahead of issues and work in a more productive, focused way.

6
Traditional MethodsAutonomous sourcing vs. traditional sourcing
Traditional sourcing is reactive and labor-intensive, leaving little time for more strategic work like building supplier relationships, 
partnering with other departments for more cohesive decision-making, and mitigating risk.
The steps of a complex sourcing event that can take months using manual methods can be set up in minutes instead with 
autonomous sourcing.  It’s as simple as filling out a short form describing your needs and letting automation do the rest, such 
as pick the right suppliers, contact them, gather their bids, analyze them, and present options to satisfy specific needs, like 
cost or risk reduction.
For even more productivity and efficiency, the right solution won’t even need a human to start a sourcing event — it can be 
set up to automatically trigger based on certain cues or signals. For example, in a warehouse where inventory dips below 
a predetermined level, a bot can automatically launch and run a successful sourcing event through an API with no human 
intervention at all.

7
Autonomous sourcing success: Days-long processes shrink to just hours with automation
Mars Inc. is experiencing the benefits of digital transformation — taking their process to build an RFP from four days down to 
just four hours with Keelvar solutions. Efficiency has skyrocketed and, by giving their team more time to spend on strategic 
work, Mars has seen increased employee satisfaction and retention.
The chef analogy: Orchestrating effective autonomous sourcing
Autonomous sourcing brings efficient sourcing to everyday users without the learning 
curve. Think of it like this: Each automated workflow built in autonomous sourcing is like 
a recipe, which is then cooked (executed) by a bot.
When a chef (procurement professional aka bot architect) creates and writes a recipe 
(automated workflow) for the bot to execute, they’re giving it the guidelines to ensure 
a delicious dish every time. When someone in your organization uses an automated 
workflow to run a sourcing event, you’re ensuring they meet sourcing best practices 
every time.When to use autonomous sourcing
Autonomous sourcing is best for simple, repetitive, ad-hoc, or urgent events. This lets business users who need guidance on 
finding the right supplier for their needs do their best work while your procurement team focuses on theirs. And the best part 
is that procurement has governance and visibility into all spend, without having to do the work.The recipe for autonomous 
sourcing success
Autonomous sourcing is the best option for:
• Sourcing spend with minimal human effort 
• Launching RFPs quickly 
• Ensuring competition to get the best price 
• Improving visibility and governance over lower-value buying
• Empowering buyers in the organization with best practices 
— and with minimal trainingWe’ve got this awesome Optimizer that we use to run large-scale, complex 
sourcing events. But then we’ve also got the autonomous sourcing piece of 
it now, which is built onto the Optimizer, so you don’t even have to train the teams to 
use it. You now have a bot do all the work of building and creating the events for you. 
 
— Sam de Frates, Vice President, Commercial – Europe, CIS & Turkey and Analytics“

8
What is a sourcing bot?
Imagine sourcing bots like your sourcing co-pilot or 
personal assistant. Procurement professionals create the 
automated workflow (recipe) and the bot executes it.
These workflows can either be automatically triggered 
into action by the bot listening for certain signals — such 
as inventory going below a certain point — or by a human 
requesting to purchase or source something. Then the 
bot does the rest, freeing teams from the manual rigors 
of sourcing.
Like a recipe includes all the ingredients and instructions needed for a finished dish, so do the workflows your procurement 
team builds. Building a workflow starts with intake requirements (the ingredients) and moves through the process  
(the recipe’s preparation), including:
  Intake: What is being sourced and what informxation does a supplier need to bid? 
  Supplier selection:  Which suppliers should be included and can bid?
  Approval flows:  Do you need approval steps in the workflow or can it be fully automated? 
  Event management: Who should be notified of progress along the way internally?
  Evaluations:  Are there other factors than the lowest cost that should be considered when evaluating bids?
  Award: Which supplier will be selected?
  Export:  Do you want this data to update another system?
And just like a cookbook full of recipes, you build these workflows once so they can be used again and again. Your 
procurement team can even automate the process so thoroughly that the business user needs only to decide what they 
want to purchase or source — and intelligent sourcing bots execute the workflow from there until human approval is needed.

Managing Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
This guide is for SAP Ariba buyer users seeking information on how to manage their suppliers, including common
supplier information, supplier creation and onboarding, and supplier lifecycle processes.
Buyers manage common supplier information, on boarding, and supplier lifecycle processes to add new suppliers,
collect and maintain supplier profile  information, and decide which suppliers are qualified  or preferred in specific
domains.
This guide applies to:
•SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and Performance
•SAP Ariba Supplier Information and Performance Management (new architecture)
•SAP Ariba Supplier Risk

Common Supplier Information
About the Supplier Management  Dashboard
The Supplier Management  dashboard shows all of the activities and suppliers that you manage directly. Y ou also
search for suppliers there.
Use the Supplier Management  dashboard to:
•Search for suppliers.
•Monitor your supplier management activities, such as the supplier requests you've created or the registrations
you've started.
•Monitor the suppliers to which you've been assigned through buyer category assignments (the user matrix),
including their qualifications  and preferred status levels (SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and Performance only).
On the Supplier Management  dashboard, choose any supplier's name to see their profile.  A supplier's profile
includes all of the information your organization has gathered about them and all of their activities, supplier
management projects, and lifecycle processes.
The Supplier Management  dashboard is available in sites that include SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and
Performance or SAP Ariba Supplier Information and Performance Management (new architecture).
Related Information
My Activities on the Supplier Management Dashboard [page 11]
My Suppliers on the Supplier Management Dashboard [page 13]
Searching for Suppliers [page 60]
My Activities  on the Supplier Management  Dashboard
The My Activities  area of the Supplier Management  dashboard shows information about the supplier
management projects you've created so that you can monitor their progress and navigate to them easily.
Y ou only see this area if you have permission to create a supplier management project (such as requesting a
new supplier or inviting a supplier to register) and have created at least one project. In sites where the process
project feature (SM-16798) is enabled, you also see this area if you're a decision-maker for at least one qualification
process project.
The area shows a tile for each supplier lifecycle stage with the number of projects you've created or are a decision-
maker for. Choose a tile to see a list of projects with the following information:
•The supplier name, which you can choose to open their profile.
Managing Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Common Supplier Information PUBLIC 11

•The projects that you created. Lifecycle tiles in the My Activities  area are locale-specific,  meaning that they
only show the projects that you created in the current locale setting in your user preferences, and don't show
projects that you created in any other locales. For example, if you create a supplier request with your locale set
to English-United States , and then change your default locale to Spanish-Mexico  and create another request,
you only see the one request you created in the Spanish-Mexico  locale.
•The current status of the project. If the project is still in approval, the Status  column shows the number of days
it has been in approval and the names of the outstanding approvers. Y ou can use this information to monitor
the approval's progress and send reminders as needed. Choose the filter  icon ( ) to filter  the activity list by
status.
•For qualification,  disqualification,  and preferred supplier management projects (SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle
and Performance only), the commodities and regions associated with the project.
•A View  button, which you can choose to open the relevant area of the supplier's profile  and review
questionnaires, issue reinvitations, or complete tasks.
The My Activities  area only shows the supplier projects that you created manually and that require approval:
•If a supplier request you created is automatically approved, it doesn't show on the Requests  tile.
•Supplier registrations created through automatic invitation or mass invitation don't show on the Registration
tile.
 Note
In sites where the process project feature (SM-16798) is enabled:
•The Qualifications  tile shows qualifications  regardless of project type. It shows any legacy qualification
projects you've created. It also shows any process qualification  projects that you've created or are a
decision-maker for.
•In addition to legacy disqualification  projects, the Disqualification  tile also shows discontinuation process
projects created for qualification  processes.

My Suppliers  on the Supplier Management  Dashboard
The My Suppliers  area of the Supplier Management  dashboard shows you the most recent activities for the
suppliers to whom you've been assigned and who have at least one qualification,  disqualification,  or preferred
supplier project.
The My Suppliers  area is only available in SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and Performance solutions.
T o see the My Suppliers  area, you must be assigned to least one supplier qualification,  disqualification,  or
preferred supplier project or process based on a commodity and region combination using buyer category
assignments (the user matrix).
The area shows the following information for each supplier you manage:
•Supplier name and ID. Choose a supplier's name to open their profile.
•Registration status.
 Tip
An administrator can designate suppliers as qualified  or preferred by importing data into your site. In this
case, it's possible for suppliers to be qualified  or preferred without having registered first,  and their profile
information can be incomplete as a result. Y ou can use this registration status information to identify those
suppliers and invite them to register and complete their profiles.
•The most recent activity, for example, if the supplier was recently qualified  or disqualified,  or achieved a
preferred status level.
•The supplier's preferred category status levels, if any, which show as color-coded labels by status level. The
preferred status labels include the number of categories for which the supplier has that status level. Choose
the Details  link to see the list of categories and regions for which the supplier has that preferred category
status level.
The My Suppliers  area includes a number of filters  that you can use to show only the suppliers that meet specific
criteria:
•Preferred status filters , which are links at the top of the area. There's an All Status  link, and a link for each
preferred level your organization uses. T o apply one of these filters,  choose the status level link. Each link shows
the number of suppliers in the current list who have achieved that level for at least one commodity category
and region. These numbers can change as you apply or clear other filters  to change the current supplier list.
T o clear the filters,  choose the All Status  link.
•Category and region filters , which are dropdown menus. These filters

filters,  choose the status level link. Each link shows
the number of suppliers in the current list who have achieved that level for at least one commodity category
and region. These numbers can change as you apply or clear other filters  to change the current supplier list.
T o clear the filters,  choose the All Status  link.
•Category and region filters , which are dropdown menus. These filters  apply to both preferred and qualified
categories and regions. T o apply them, select categories or regions on the dropdown menu, then choose Done .
If you've applied one of these filters,  the dropdown menu shows the number of filtered  values present in the
current supplier list. For example, if you selected five commodity categories, but the current list only includes
suppliers who are preferred or qualified  for two of those categories, the dropdown menu shows a (2). This
number can change as you apply or clear other filters  to change the current supplier list.
T o clear this filter,  choose the dropdown menu, then choose Clear .
•Registration and qualification  status filters , which are dropdown menus. T o apply these filters,  select
statuses on the dropdown menu, then choose Done . If you've applied one of these filters,  the dropdown menu
shows the number of filter  values present in the current supplier list. This number can change as you apply or
clear other filters  to change the current supplier list.
T o clear this filter,  choose the dropdown menu, then choose Clear .
•A primary supplier manager filter . The supplier list shows the suppliers to which you've been assigned based
on commodity and region combinations using buyer category assignments (the user matrix). The primary
Managing Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Common Supplier Information PUBLIC 13

supplier manager filter  allows you to narrow the list to suppliers for which you've also been specified  as primary
supplier manager either in the supplier's profile  or in a supplier data import.
Select this filter  to apply it, and clear it to remove it.
These filters  are additive. Applying the first  filter  narrows the list to those suppliers who meet those initial criteria,
and applying the second filter  narrows the list down further to the list of suppliers that meet both criteria. In
addition, the filter  options that are available depend on the characteristics of the suppliers in the current list.
Therefore, the filters  you apply affect  the options in the other filters.  For example, if you filter  by commodity
category, the region filter  only shows the regions that match the previously filtered  categories; the registration
status filter  only shows the statuses for suppliers with the matching commodity categories; and so on.

Supplier Profile  Summary Navigation [page 24]
Supplier Profile  Summary Navigation
Y ou can use breadcrumbs and useful links to navigate between the supplier profile  summary and profile  detail
pages, where you view and manage supplier information and lifecycle activities.
Navigation to and from the Profile  Summary
Y ou can use breadcrumbs to navigate between the supplier profile  summary, the profile  detail pages you open from
the summary, and your previous location.
Choosing a supplier's name on the Supplier Management  dashboard opens their profile  summary. From there, you
can use breadcrumbs to navigate back to a previous page. For example, you can search for Supplier ABC  and
open their profile  summary from search results. The resulting breadcrumbs in the banner of the profile  summary
show the navigation path, which you can use to return to search results:
In the supplier profile  summary, you can choose links or cards to navigate to profile  detail pages. Y ou can then
use breadcrumbs on profile  detail pages to navigate back to the profile  summary or to your previous location.
For example, once you've searched for Supplier ABC  and opened their profile  summary, you can choose the
Contacts  panel to view their contact details. The resulting breadcrumb in the Contacts  detail area shows the
complete navigation path from search:
24 PUBLICManaging Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Common Supplier Information

The Search  breadcrumb represents the supplier search page on the Supplier Management  dashboard.
In sites that include SAP Ariba Supplier Risk, a Risk Information  breadcrumb represents navigation steps for risk-
related pages, including supplier search and other areas of the Supplier Risk  dashboard as well as engagement
detail pages. Choose the Risk Information  breadcrumb to return to the original risk-related page in the navigation
path.
 Tip
For reliable navigation, always use breadcrumbs rather than your browser's back button or the back arrow ( Go
to Dashboard ) in the site banner.
Navigation to and from Guided Sourcing Pages
Choosing a supplier name in the following areas opens the supplier's profile  summary:
•The Invite suppliers  page in the creation workflow  for guided sourcing events
•On the For Y ou  dashboard:
•Draft events with invited suppliers
•Events awaiting publication approval with invited suppliers
•Pending selection and awarded events with participating suppliers
•Event lists opened from the RFx  link in the Quick Links  panel
When navigating froma guided sourcing page to a supplier's profile  summary, a Sourcing  breadcrumb returns you
to your original location in guided sourcing.
Navigation Between Profile  Detail Pages
On profile  detail pages, Useful Links  to the most commonly used profile  detail areas allow you to navigate directly
between those areas without returning to the supplier profile  summary. Depending on your organization's solution
package and your site's configuration,  the Useful Links  list can include the following links:

About the Classic Supplier 360° Profile
The classic supplier 360° profile  shows all the information that your organization has collected about a supplier in
separate areas, and provides tools for managing common supplier information, onboarding, and lifecycle activities.
Depending on how your site is set up, your use either the supplier profile  summary or the classic supplier 360°
profile  to view and manage suppliers. The classic supplier 360° profile  opens on a Summary  area by default and
includes a navigation panel with the supplier's name. If your site uses the profile  summary instead, most of the
same information is available there in a different  layout. For more information, see About the Supplier Profile
Summary [page 14] .

associated projects and events. The classic supplier 360° profile  includes different  areas for specific  activities and
types of information. The areas you see vary depending on your site's solution package and feature enablement. In
the navigation bar, choose the link for an area to view it.

About the Activity log  Detail Area of the Supplier Profile
The Activity log  detail area in supplier profiles  shows a record of project activities, including project activities that
are automated.
Use the Activity log  to view the Date , User , Process Type , Activity Type , Title , and Details  of activities in supplier
management projects.
The Activity log  differs  from the advanced view History  tab in multiple ways. The Activity log  aggregates and
displays activity from multiple project types, whereas the History  tab displays an audit trail of changes and activity
in a specific  project. Y ou can't export data from the Activity log , but you can from the History  tab. Also, the Activity
log displays only dates, while the History  tab displays timestamps.
Y ou can search the Activity log  for keyword data from the Title  and Details  columns. Y ou can also filter  the log by
project type or activity type.
•Project types:
•Supplier Request
•Supplier Registration
•Supplier Qualification  (SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and Performance only)
•Supplier Disqualification  (SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and Performance only)
•Preferred Supplier  (SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and Performance only)
•Modular Questionnaire

Supplier Contact Management
Supplier contacts are people in the external supplier organization who can respond to questionnaires and perform
other activities on behalf of the supplier. Managing these contacts makes the appropriate supplier organization
representatives available for questionnaire invitations. Suppliers can also have an internal primary supplier
manager, who is a person in your organization who either interacts with or can answer questions about the supplier.

About the Contacts  Detail Area of the Supplier Profile
Supplier contacts are the external people that you interact with while doing business with a supplier. A primary
supplier manager is a person at your company who is familiar with the supplier. Y ou view and manage these
external and internal contacts in the Contacts  detail area of the supplier profile.
Contacts
Supplier contacts are external to your organization, but they can sign in to SAP Business Network to fill out
questionnaires, participate in events, negotiate contracts, and perform other tasks in your site.
Approved supplier contacts are added to a supplier when:
•An administrator imports supplier contact data in your site.
•An approver approves a supplier request, which always includes an initial contact for the supplier.
•Someone in your organization who has appropriate permissions manually adds a contact to the supplier.
•Someone in the supplier organization creates a user in the supplier's SAP Business Network account and your
site is configured  to automatically synchronize them to the supplier's contacts in your site. Y our site can be
configured  to auto-approve these contacts.
The Contacts  detail area of the supplier profile  shows the following information for all the contacts that have been
added to the supplier in your site:
Column Description
Name The contact's first  and last name.

Common Supplier Information PUBLIC 47

Column Description
Contact The contact's email address, which is also their unique user
name.
Type The type of the contact, if any (for example, technical, ac-
counts receivable, or legal).
AN StatusAn indicator of whether or not the supplier contact is a known
user who can sign in to the supplier's SAP Business Network
account. A contact is a known user in the supplier's SAP
Business Network account when they've responded to a ques-
tionnaire invitation or been synchronized from SAP Business
Network. Y ou can add contacts that don't have SAP Business
Network users yet, but those contacts must create them to an-
swer questionnaires. The links in notification  emails to supplier
contacts prompt them to either sign in or create an account on
SAP Business Network.

umn only shows in sites where departments are enabled.

Approval , or Denied .
Y ou can click any of these column headings to sort the supplier contact list by the information in the column.
The current primary supplier contact is starred.
Depending on your permissions, in the Contacts  area, you can:
•View details for any contact.
•Add or edit a contact.
•Delete any contact other than the primary contact.
•Approve or deny a contact from SAP Business Network.
Once a supplier contact is synchronized with SAP Business Network, the contact maintains their own first  name,
last name, email address, and phone number data. Any edits you make to that information are overwritten by the
information from SAP Business Network. Any updates the supplier contact makes to those fields  automatically
show in their contact information in your site.
Supplier Manager
A primary supplier manager is internal to your organization, and is typically a person who is familiar with or
interacts with the supplier. Each supplier can have one primary supplier manager. A primary supplier manager is
added to a supplier when:
•An administrator imports primary supplier manager data in your site.
•Someone in your organization who has the required permissions manually adds a primary supplier manager to
the supplier.

Supplier Contact Synchronization with SAP Business
Network
Depending on your site's configuration,  new users in the supplier's SAP Business Network account can be
automatically added as contacts in your site and can either be automatically approved or require manual approval.
Suppliers fill out and submit external questionnaires in their SAP Business Network accounts. If a supplier hasn't
responded to any questionnaire or event invitations from you before, your site doesn't have a link to their SAP
Business Network account and they're private. Once they respond to an invitation, your site is linked to their SAP
Business Network account and they're public.
Supplier contacts created in your site are private at first.  They're not synchronized to the supplier's SAP Business
Network account and exist only in your site until you invite them to a sourcing event or an external questionnaire.
If you do invite a private supplier contact to an event and that contact doesn't exist in the supplier's SAP Business
Network account, the supplier account administrator must approve the contact before they can participate in the
event. The supplier contact then maintains their own profile  information on SAP Business Network, including name
and contact information.
Once a supplier contact is public, their first  name, last name, email address, and phone number are no longer
editable in your site. The information in those fields  is synchronized from SAP Business Network, where the
supplier contact maintains it. Any updates the supplier contact makes to those fields  in SAP Business Network
show in your site. Y ou continue to maintain the other information associated with the supplier contact,

their first  name, last name, email address, and phone number are no longer
editable in your site. The information in those fields  is synchronized from SAP Business Network, where the
supplier contact maintains it. Any updates the supplier contact makes to those fields  in SAP Business Network
show in your site. Y ou continue to maintain the other information associated with the supplier contact, such as
type, whether they're the primary contact, and associated commodities, regions, and departments.
Information you maintain about supplier contacts in your site, including question answers mapped to contact
fields,  is never synchronized to the supplier's SAP Business Network account. Suppliers and supplier contacts
always maintain their own information about themselves on SAP Business Network.
Managing Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Common Supplier Information PUBLIC 49

On SAP Business Network, a supplier user can request the removal of their personal information from the
supplier's SAP Business Network account. An administrator in the supplier SAP Business Network account
approves the request. At that point, if the supplier user is a public supplier contact in your site, the contact
is removed from the supplier's profile.  They no longer have access to any of your supplier management
questionnaires to which they were previously assigned.

Adding or Editing a Supplier Contact
Use these steps to add or edit a supplier contact in the supplier's profile  so that they're available for event or
questionnaire invitations and have the most current information. Supplier contacts are the people in the supplier
organization who interact with your organization to register, fill out questionnaires, bid on events, and so forth.
Prerequisites
T o add, edit, or delete a supplier contact in a supplier's profile  or when inviting a supplier to a questionnaire, you
must be a member of one of the following groups:
•Supplier Registration Manager
•SM Ops Administrator
•Customer Administrator
•Category Manager
•Commodity Manager
•Event Administrator
•Limited Event Administrator
•Sourcing Project Administrator
•Supplier Project Administrator
•Supplier/Customer Manager
•Supplier/Customer Agent
Members of the Supplier Qualification  Manager  group can add, but not edit or delete, supplier contacts in the
supplier's profile  and when inviting a supplier to a questionnaire.
50 PUBLICManaging Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Common Supplier Information

Context
If you're adding the first  contact to a supplier's profile,  that contact automatically becomes the primary supplier
contact.
 Tip
Since a supplier must always have a primary contact, you can't toggle Primary Contact  off when editing the
current primary contact for the supplier. Instead, create or edit another supplier contact and toggle Primary
Contact  on for them. Doing so transfers primary status from the previous primary contact to the new or edited
contact.
Y ou can't edit an unapproved supplier contact.
Once a supplier contact is linked to an SAP Business Network account, the contact maintains their own first
name, last name, email address, and phone number data, and those fields  are no longer editable in your site. Any
updates the supplier contact makes to those fields  in their SAP Business Network profile  automatically show in
their contact information in your site.
Y ou can create two different  supplier contacts with the same email address for a supplier because
the email addresses aren't case-sensitive. For example: you can create emailaddress@company.com  and
Emailaddress@company.com .
 Note
Although there's no limit to the number of contacts you can add to a supplier, a maximum of 1000 active
contacts per supplier is made available for searching, in no particular order or priority and with no guarantee
that one of those 1000 contacts is the primary contact. Only contacts that are made available for searching
show in lists of available contacts during questionnaire or sourcing event invitations and other workflows  that
involve contact selection or can be used in supplier searches.
Procedure
1. Perform one of the following actions:
•In the supplier's profile  summary, choose the Supplier Contacts  panel.
•In the classic supplier 360° profile,  choose Contacts  in the left-hand navigation bar
The Contacts  detail area of the supplier's profile  opens.
2. Perform one of the following actions:
•T o add a new contact, in the upper right corner of the contacts table, choose Add  ( ).
•T o edit an existing contact, choose  (options) > Edit   ).
3. Enter or edit information for the supplier contact, including the required first  and last name and email address.
Managing Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Common Supplier Information PUBLIC 51

 Tip
The default time zone for supplier contacts is Pacific  Standard Time. If you know the supplier contact's
time zone, you can set it here. Differences  between the default time zone and the actual time zone of the
new contact can prevent them from accessing an event or activity with an impending closing date.
4.Optional: T oggle Primary Contact  on to make the contact the primary contact for the supplier.
A message indicates the name of the supplier contact that you're replacing with the new contact as primary
contact for the supplier. The primary contact is the default recipient of all new external questionnaire
invitations.
They show in the header and Supplier Contacts  panel of the supplier profile  summary or the Summary  area of
the classic supplier 360° profile.
5.Optional: Choose the commodities, regions, and departments for which the supplier contact is responsible.
6.Optional: Choose a type for the contact from the Contact Type  dropdown menu.

If you created a new supplier contact, it's automatically approved and is added to the supplier. The new contact

Supplier contacts receive an email notification  when they’re added. However, the system doesn't verify if the

Use these steps to add a supplier contact if you're inviting or reinviting a supplier to fill out questionnaires and you
don't see the person that you want to invite listed as an available supplier contact.
Prerequisites
T o add, edit, or delete a supplier contact in a supplier's profile  or when inviting a supplier to a questionnaire, you
must be a member of one of the following groups:
•Supplier Registration Manager
•SM Ops Administrator
•Customer Administrator
•Category Manager
•Commodity Manager
•Event Administrator
•Limited Event Administrator
•Sourcing Project Administrator
•Supplier Project Administrator
•Supplier/Customer Manager
•Supplier/Customer Agent
Members of the Supplier Qualification  Manager  group can add, but not edit or delete, supplier contacts in the
supplier's profile  and when inviting a supplier to a questionnaire.
Y ou must also have permission to send the external questionnaire:
•For registration questionnaires, you must be a member of the Supplier Registration Manager  group.
•For standalone modular questionnaires, you must be a member of the SM Modular Questionnaire Manager
group.
•For qualification  questionnaires in qualification  projects, you must be a member of the Supplier Qualification
Manager  group (SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and Performance only).
•For modular questionnaires included in qualification  or other process projects, you must be a member of the
SM Modular Questionnaire Manager  global user group and a project group with the Process Initiator  role
(SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and Performance only, in sites where the process project feature (SM-16798) is
enabled).
Procedure
1. Perform one of the following actions in the list of available recipients on the send questionnaires page:
•T o add a new supplier contact, in the upper right corner of the recipients table, choose the add icon (  ).
•T o edit an existing supplier contact, choose   (more options) >   Edit .

and is the default recipient for all external questionnaires.

Approving or Denying a New Supplier Contact from SAP
Business Network
If your site is set up to add new, unapproved supplier contacts automatically from the supplier's SAP Business
Network account, use these steps to approve or deny them. Unapproved contacts can't be invited to participate in
sourcing events or fill out some types of supplier management questionnaires.
Prerequisites
Y ou must be a member of the Supplier Registration Manager  or SM Ops Administrator  to approve or deny
supplier contacts from SAP Business Network in supplier profiles.
Context
Unapproved supplier contacts show in the Contacts  details area of supplier profiles  with the status Pending
Approval . Y ou can approve contacts that were previously either unapproved or denied. Once you approve a contact,
that approval is final.
Unapproved supplier contacts can't be invited to participate in sourcing events or to fill out external registration
and qualification  questionnaires. However, they can be added to process projects and invited to external modular
questionnaires.
Procedure
1. Perform one of the following actions:
•In the supplier's profile  summary, choose the Supplier Contacts  panel.
•In the classic supplier 360° profile,  choose Contacts  in the left-hand navigation bar
The Contacts  detail area of the supplier's profile  opens.
2. Perform one of the following actions:
•Choose   (options) > Approve    to approve the contact.
•Choose   (options) > Deny    to deny the contact.
Results
The supplier contact now shows a status of either Approved  or Denied .
Managing Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Common Supplier Information PUBLIC 57

Next Steps
Once the supplier contact is approved, you can edit it to add more information such as commodities, regions,
departments, contact type, and so forth. Y ou can also make the approved contact the recipient of supplier
management questionnaires.
Once the supplier contact is either approved or denied, you can delete it. Y ou can't delete unapproved supplier
contacts from the supplier's contacts list.

Assigning, Reassigning, or Removing a Primary Supplier
Manager
Use these steps to assign, reassign, or remove a primary supplier manager in the supplier's profile.  A primary
supplier manager is a person at your company who either interacts with or can answer questions about the
supplier.
Prerequisites
Y ou must be a member of the Supplier Registration Manager , Supplier Qualification  Manager , Preferred
Supplier Manager , SM ERP Administrator , or SM Ops Administrator  group to add or edit contacts in the supplier
profile.
Context
Each supplier can have one assigned primary supplier manager. In sites that use modular supplier management
questionnaires, the primary supplier manager can receive notifications  of expiring or expired questionnaires.
Y ou can assign any person in your organization who has a user account in your site as the primary supplier
manager. Customer administrators in your organization can also manage primary supplier manager assignments
by importing them into your site in a CSV data file. The most recent assignment, whether made manually in the
supplier profile  or through data import, is always the current assignment. People who are added or removed as
primary supplier manager through data import don't receive email notifications  of the assignment or removal.
58 PUBLICManaging Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Common Supplier Information

In sites that include the Supplier Management  dashboard, you can filter  both supplier searches and the
information in the My Suppliers  dashboard area by primary supplier manager. The My Suppliers  area is only
available in solutions that include SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and Performance.
Procedure
1. Perform one of the following actions:
•In the supplier's profile  summary, choose the Supplier Contacts  panel.
•In the classic supplier 360° profile,  choose Contacts  in the left-hand navigation bar
The Contacts  detail area of the supplier's profile  opens.
2. Perform one of the following actions in the Supplier Manager  area:
•Choose Assign supplier manager  if the supplier doesn't currently have a primary supplier manager and
you want to assign one. Search for or navigate to the name of the person you want to assign and select
them.
•Choose   (options) > Edit  ( ) to change the currently assigned primary supplier manager. Search for or
navigate to the name of the person you want to assign and select them.
•Choose   (options) > Delete  ( ) to remove the primary supplier manager without replacing them.
Results
People who are assigned to or removed from suppliers as primary supplier manager in supplier profiles  receive
notifications  informing them of the change.

Supplier and Supplier Management Project Search
Y ou can search for suppliers on the Supplier Management  dashboard and for supplier management projects on
the Home  dashboard.
Supplier search includes filters  to help you locate suppliers by registration or qualification  status, commodities,
integration status, and other useful characteristics.
Managing Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Common Supplier Information PUBLIC 59

Project search includes filters  to help you locate supplier management projects by status, created date, owner,
and other useful characteristics. It's useful for locating unapproved or denied supplier requests, since they're not
included in supplier search.
The Supplier Management  dashboard and its supplier search are available in sites that include SAP Ariba Supplier
Lifecycle and Performance or SAP Ariba Supplier Information and Performance Management (new architecture).
If your site includes SAP Ariba Supplier Risk, the Supplier Risk  dashboard includes a separate supplier search. For
details, refer to Supplier Risk Dashboard .

Searching for Suppliers
Use these steps to view detailed information about your organization's suppliers by searching for them on the
Supplier Management  dashboard.
Prerequisites
The Category  search filter  is only available if there's at least one question of type Commodity  in an external
(supplier-facing) registration questionnaire, a qualification  questionnaire, a preferred level questionnaire, or a
modular questionnaire. The same is true of the Region  filter  and a question of type Region , and the Department
filter  and a question of type Department .
Qualification  status  and Category status  filters  are only available in sites that includeSAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle
and Performance.
Context
 Note
•Combining different  types search terms such as name and country code in a single supplier search can
produce unexpected results.
•If an administrator in your site updates master data such as commodities, regions, and departments, there
can be a short delay before those updates are reflected  in the filters  in the Refine  results  panel.
•When you choose multiple registration statuses and either commodities, department, or region, the search
doesn't show any results.
•If your site uses internal registrations, when filtering  on registration status, there's no way to distinguish
between internal and external registrations.
60 PUBLICManaging Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Common Supplier Information

The supplier search page includes filters  for commodity categories, regions, ERP integration status, primary
supplier manager, certificates,  and, if your organization uses them, departments. It also includes filters  for the
supplier's status in various supplier management processes such as registration.
If you run a search based on search terms, the list of available search filters  narrows to the options that apply to
the suppliers in the current search results. For example, if you search for plastics  and none of the suppliers that
match that term are registered, the registration status filter  doesn't include the Registered  status. T o update the
list of available search filters,  clear the current filters  and run either a new search based on different  search terms
or an empty search.
If your questionnaires use search terms, you can search for suppliers based on answers to questions that have
been enabled for search. The options for these filters  vary depending on how the questions were set up, and can
involve selecting one or more values from a list; entering a range of dates or numbers; entering one or more words
to search for text answers that include them; or choosing between yes and no answers. There can be a short delay
between when an answer is updated in a questionnaire and when it shows up in search.
Y ou can apply filters  to search results in any order.
 Tip
Y ou can't use supplier search to find  these types of information:
•Suppliers with requests that have been submitted but haven't been approved yet.
•The type of supplier request used to create a supplier (internal or external).
•Suppliers invited to register through mass registration invitations (legacy suppliers) versus suppliers
invited through other means (nonlegacy suppliers).
However, you can search for these things using project search on the Home  dashboard. Y ou can also use
project search quickly find  specific  supplier registration, qualification,  disqualification,  preferred supplier
management, and process projects.
Procedure
1. On the dashboard, click the Supplier Management  tab.
2. Perform one of the following actions:
•T o perform a targeted search, enter a search term in the search bar on the dashboard and press Enter  or
click the search icon (  ).
•T o filter  the entire supplier search list using search filters,  leave the search bar on the dashboard empty

Saving a Supplier Search and Running It Later
Use these steps to save any set of search filters  you use frequently so that you can and run the search again quickly
instead of having to manually apply the same filters  each time.
Context
Saving a search saves a specific  set of filters  so that you can apply them again in one step.
Saved searches are always based on permanent filters  such as registration status, commodity, and region. They
can't include search terms such as supplier name or country/region code, or any custom search filters  based on
answers to questionnaires.
Y ou can maintain up to 10 saved searches at a time.
Procedure
1. On the search results page, apply the default filters  you want to save in the search.
2. In the area above the search results, choose Save Search .
3. Enter a name for the search and choose Save .
Results
The filters  you applied are now saved under the name you specified  and the saved search is applied to the current
search results. The search fields  on both the Supplier Management  dashboard and the search results page show a
Saved Searches  link that indicates the current number of searches you've saved in parentheses.
Next Steps
T o clear a saved search, remove its filters  from the filter  list. Once you've removed the last filter,  you've cleared the
saved search.
T o run a saved search, choose Saved Search , then choose the name of your saved search. If you already had
a search open, your previous search results are automatically cleared and replaced by the results of the saved
search.
Managing Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Common Supplier Information PUBLIC 65

Modifying or Deleting a Saved Supplier Search
Use these steps to update a current search, save changes to filters  as a new saved search, or delete a saved search
you no longer need.
Context
Saved searches are always based only on default filters  such as registration status, commodity, and region (the
permanent filters  on the left side of the search page). Saved searches can't include search terms (such as supplier
name or contact information), or any custom search filters  based on answers to supplier management forms and
questionnaires.
Y ou can only maintain 10 saved searches at a time.
Procedure
•T o modify a current saved search, perform the following actions:
a. On the Supplier Management  dashboard or the search results page, run the saved search by choosing
Saved Searches , then choosing the name of the saved search in the dropdown menu.
b. Modify the search by adding or removing filters.
c. Save your changes by performing one of the following actions:
•T o update the current saved search with your changes, choose the name of the saved search, then
choose Update Search .
•T o save your changes as a new saved search, choose Save new Search , enter a name for the new
search, and choose Save .
Y our new or updated search is applied to the current search results. Y ou can rerun it at any time.
•T o delete a current saved search, perform one of the following actions:
•On the Supplier Management  dashboard or on the search results page, to the right of the search field,
choose Saved Searches , then choose the delete icon (  ). Choose OK to confirm  that you want to delete
the search.
•T o delete the currently applied saved search on the search results page, click the name of the saved search
and choose Delete Search . Choose OK to confirm  that you want to delete the search.
If you deleted the currently applied saved search, its filters  are applied to the current search results, but the
saved search itself is now deleted. Y ou can remove the filters  from the filter  list.

Exporting Supplier Search Results for Reporting and Other
Uses
Use these steps to export supplier search results to a comma-separated value (CSV) file allows. Y ou can use the
exported file as a snapshot of specific  supplier data to your computer to work with offline  in a spreadsheet program
such as Microsoft Excel or import it into a third-party reporting tool.
Prerequisites
Y ou can export any supplier search results as long as you’ve applied at least one search filter  to them. Y ou can't
export an empty supplier search, which includes all of the suppliers in your site.
T o export search results, you must be a member of one of the following groups:
•SM Ops Administrator
•Customer Administrator
•Supplier Request Manager
•Supplier Registration Manager
•Supplier Qualification  Manager
•Preferred Supplier Manager
•Mobile Supplier Manager
Context
When you export supplier search results, you can choose the character that separates values in the export file
and the export file encoding. By default, exported files  use a comma separator (,) and UTF-8 encoding. Regardless
of the separator you choose, the export generates a file with a CSV (comma-separated value) extension. Y ou can
change the extension when you save the file to your computer.
The exported file includes data for the suppliers in the current search results based on both the search terms and
filters  you've used (including custom filters  based on questionnaire answers) and contains the following columns:
•Supplier Name
•ERP Vendor ID
•SM Vendor ID
Managing Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Common Supplier Information PUBLIC 67

•An Id
•Registration Status
•Integrated to ERP
•Primary contact first  name
•Primary contact middle name
•Primary contact last name
•Primary contact email
•Address - Line1
•Address - Line2
•Address - Line3
•Address - City
•Address - Country Code
•Address - Region Code
•Address - PO Box
•Address - Postal Code
•Qualification  Status
•Preferred Status
•Category
•Region
•Business Unit
A single supplier can have multiple rows of data in the exported file. If the supplier's preferred or qualified  or
multiple combinations of category, region, and department, the exported file contains a separate row for each
unique status and combination. .
 Note
While there's no explicit limit on the amount of supplier data you can export in search results, the larger the
data set, the longer the export takes.
Procedure
1. Perform a supplier search, using search terms and applying filters  to return the results you want to capture in
the exported file.
2. At the top of the search results list, click Export .
3. (Optional) Expand Export settings  and perform any of the following actions:
•T o use a separator other than a comma (,), choose a separator from the Delimiter  dropdown menu.
•T o use an encoding method other than UTF-8, choose an encoding method from the Encoding format
dropdown menu.
4. Click Export .
5. Save the exported file to the location of your choice.

Searching for Unapproved or External Supplier Requests
and Other Supplier Management Projects
Use these steps to search for supplier management projects, including supplier requests that are in approval or
denied (and therefore not visible in supplier searches) or requests by external or internal request type.
Context
Y ou can use project search on the Home  dashboard to search for supplier management projects. Project search
allows you to create search filters  using certain project fields  that aren't available as filters  in supplier search on
the Supplier Management  dashboard. Since project search returns results for all projects, it also includes supplier
requests that are in approval or denied, and that therefore haven't resulted in the creation of a supplier.
For complete details on using project search, refer to Searching for Projects or Documents using the Search Page .
 Tip
•The names of supplier management projects always include the supplier's name. Y ou can use supplier
names as project search terms if you want to look for the projects associated with a specific  supplier.
•T o search for external supplier requests, use the Supplier Request Project  content type and filter  the
search by the Is Self Registration  field.
Supplier registration project search includes a filter  for the Is Legacy  field,  but searching using that filter  currently
isn't supported.
Procedure
1. Choose the Home  dashboard.
2. On the dashboard search bar, choose the content type menu to the left of the search field  and select the type of
content you want to search for, such as Supplier Request Project .
3. Enter a search term, such as the name of the supplier.
4. Press Enter  or choose the search icon (  ) to start the search.
5. T o search for matching text in text-based project fields  (such as the Title ), enter text in the unlabeled text box.
6. T o search by project status or another project field  that doesn't currently show in the Search Filter  area,
perform the following steps:
a. Choose 
 Options

 Others
 .

8. Choose Search .

Reports on Supplier Management Projects and
Activities
Y ou can use either supplier data snapshots or analytical reports to analyze and download data on various aspects
of your organization's suppliers and supplier management activities.
There are two methods for reporting on supplier management projects and activities:
•Supplier data snapshots , which allow you to export preconfigured  sets of data in specific  areas such as
onboarding progress or certificates  in CSV files.
•Analytical reports , which allow you to aggregate, drill down, and filter  on supplier, project, and task data along
different  dimensions, including:
•Commodities, regions, and departments
•Project type and owner
•Key dates such as project start and questionnaire submission dates
•T ask owner and status
With analytical reports, you can create custom reports or use prepackaged reports designed for the most
common types of queries.

Use these steps to export a snapshot of supplier onboarding activity or certificates  in a CSV file.
Prerequisites
T o create a supplier data snapshot, you must be a member of one of the following groups:
•Supplier Registration Manager
•Supplier Qualification  Manager
•Preferred Supplier Manager
•SM Modular Questionnaire Manager
•SM Process Manager
•SM ERP Administrator
•SM Ops Administrator
Supplier data snapshots are only available in sites that include either SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and
Performance or SAP Ariba Supplier Information and Performance Management (new architecture).
Context
Y ou can create the following supplier data snapshots:
Snapshot Report Description
Supplier Onboarding Progress  [page 73] This snapshot includes data about supplier onboarding prog-
ress, from the time the supplier request was submitted to the
time the registration was approved to the time the supplier
was successfully integrated. This report is useful for tracking
how long various supplier onboarding milestones have been
pending so that you can identify and address issues and bottle-
necks.
Supplier Certificate  Data [page 74] This snapshot report includes the supplier's certificates,  which
can be collected across multiple questionnaires. The report

 Note
•Supplier data snapshot CSV files  opened in Microsoft Excel only show Latin characters. T o see non-Latin
characters in a supplier data snapshot, open the CSV file in a text editor.
•Data about inactive suppliers isn't available in supplier data snapshots.
•The snapshot report headers are not localized and are always exported in English regardless of the user's

Supplier Onboarding Progress  Data Snapshot Reference
The Supplier Onboarding Progress  data snapshot is a CSV file that includes data about supplier onboarding
progress, from submission of a supplier request to registration to successful integration. It shows how long various
supplier onboarding milestones have been pending so that you can identify and address issues and bottlenecks.
Time period  Filters
A supplier is included in a Supplier Onboarding Progress  data snapshot if any one of the following dates falls
within the date range specified  in the Time period  filter:
•The submission date of the supplier request that created the supplier.
•The supplier's registration invitation date
•The date when the registration was created, if the supplier has a registration status other than Not Invited  but
there's no registration invitation date.

Keep in mind the following tips when running or creating analytical reports on supplier management projects:
•Every analytical report must include at least one measure (data field),  which you add in the first  step of the
reporting wizard.
•The supplier management project facts include pre-defined  measures (data fields).  Y ou can also create custom
measures (user-defined  fields)  that contain the results of calculations based on data in other fields  in the fact.
For example, you can identify bottlenecks in your organization's approval process based on the amount of
time between questionnaire submission and final  project approval by adding a user-defined  field  with a formula
of IF(sum(ApprovedDate.Day-QuestionnaireSubmissionDate.Day)>0, (sum(ApprovedDate.Day-
QuestionnaireSubmissionDate.Day)), 0) . Y ou can also identify aging requests or registrations based
on the amount of time between when the project was created and the current date by adding a user-defined
field  with a formula such as sum(CURRDATE-BeginDate.Day)  and then using filters  on status fields;  for
example, you can filter

.Day-QuestionnaireSubmissionDate.Day)>0, (sum(ApprovedDate.Day-
QuestionnaireSubmissionDate.Day)), 0) . Y ou can also identify aging requests or registrations based
on the amount of time between when the project was created and the current date by adding a user-defined
field  with a formula such as sum(CURRDATE-BeginDate.Day)  and then using filters  on status fields;  for
example, you can filter  registration projects by Invited  status to identify aging registrations to which the
supplier hasn't responded.
•If you want a report that simply lists all of the data in rows, add fields  to the Detail Fields  area of the pivot
layout and check Show detail fields  in report . On the report pivot table, you can also switch between detail and
aggregate views by clicking the data menu.

•The Status  field  in the Project  hierarchy shows a generic project status rather than supplier management
project statuses. Use the Registration Status , Registration Update Status , and Request Status  to see
statuses for those specific  types of projects.
•The Process  and Process Status  fields  show generic project values and aren't related to process projects.
 Note
Some data fields  may appear in analytical reports but are not available for use in reporting. For example,
the Process Status  field  might be displayed, but cannot be included in reports.
•In supplier management projects, Start Date  typically shows when the project is created. However, in
registration and modular questionnaire project, Start Date  is reset every time the project is upgraded to the
latest template version. This behavior can result in reports where project Start Date  is after the dates of other
activities.
•Y ou can use the Project Type  field  to filter  SM Project  reports based on project type.
•The second step of the report wizard shows available hierarchy fields  by default. T o see all available fields,
including flat (nonhierarchical) fields,  click Available Hierarchies  and choose Available Fields . Some fields
that are important for reports on supplier management projects, such as Is Legacy , Is Self Registration ,
Registration Status , Registration Update Status , Request Status , and various supplier IDs only show on the
Available Fields  list.
•If you're not sure which fields  you want to add to a report, add them as page fields.  Once you've run the
report, you can move page fields  into the report from the Field Browser , as well as moving fields  between rows,
columns, and the Field Browser .
•The Supplier/Customer  fact includes data on the suppliers in your site. However, many of its hierarchies
and fields  pull data from legacy supplier workspace projects and the supplier profile  questionnaire, which
aren't applicable in SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and Performance and SAP Ariba Supplier Information and
Performance Management (new architecture) solutions. T o see Supplier/Customer  data alongside supplier
management project data in an analytical report, create a multi-fact report that includes both the Supplier/
Customer  fact and one or more of the supplier management project facts.
•Every analytical report includes at least one date filter.  T

Performance and SAP Ariba Supplier Information and
Performance Management (new architecture) solutions. T o see Supplier/Customer  data alongside supplier
management project data in an analytical report, create a multi-fact report that includes both the Supplier/
Customer  fact and one or more of the supplier management project facts.
•Every analytical report includes at least one date filter.  T ypically, the larger the date range you choose, the
larger the report.
•Dates related to project activity are calendar dates that are relative to the server time zone. Dates related to
task activity use a fixed  timestamp based on Greenwich Mean Time (GMT). This difference  can cause some
discrepancies between project and task duration calculations in analytical reports.

Joule Capabilities in Supplier Management
SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and Performance is integrated with Joule, SAP’s generative AI copilot.
Users in SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and Performance can ask Joule questions about your organization's suppliers
and receive immediate and accurate answers through generative AI.
 Caution
Joule must not be used in shared desktop environments where different  users share the same Web browser
(so-called “kiosk mode”). T o prevent unauthorized access to conversations, for example, if a session is not
terminated properly, each user must use a separate session at the operating system level. We strongly
recommend not relying on browser sign-out only.
Prerequisites
•Joule must be integrated with SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and Performance. For details, see Integrating Joule
with SAP Ariba Solutions .
•An administrator must enable the configurable  field  Last Status Change Date  in vendor data export.
For more information about enabling this field,  refer to Use of the Supplier Data API with Pagination
vendorDataRequests Endpoint .
•T o navigate to supplier profiles  from Joule, including Open in App  navigation, users in your SAP Ariba site must
be members of the Joule Supplier Profile  User  group.
•Users must have the appropriate permissions and project team roles for the specific  scenarios.
•For navigation to supplier management project pages, the features and projects associated with the specific
scenarios must be enabled and configured.

WalkMe in SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and
Performance
WalkMe Digital Adoption Platform is available in SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and Performance.
WalkMe is an AI-powered digital adoption solution that enables teams to create guidance and automation in the
flow  of work, while giving business and technology leaders insights into how people are using the software and
where users can be more self-sufficient.  This enables organizations to accelerate user adoption of technology,
boost productivity, and maximize the value of enterprise software investments.
 Note
The following features and functionalities provided by SAP as part of SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and
Performance including automation and transaction processing may be continuously improved on behalf of
the customer. SAP may use Customer Data in order to achieve such improvement.

The SAP Ariba Procurement mobile app gives you mobile access to your company's SAP Ariba strategic sourcing

User Experience Feedback Surveys for Supplier
Management
A feedback survey about user experience is available on the Supplier Management  dashboard, the supplier search
page, and profile  detail pages such as the Contact  page. Y ou can use it to provide feedback about your experience
of supplier management functionality to SAP Ariba.
When you navigate to an applicable supplier management page, a popup asks you to help make SAP software
better with your feedback:
Clicking Y es opens one of two variations of the Overall Product Satisfaction Survey  randomly. One variation asks
you to select agreement with two statements about the software's usability from Strongly disagree  to Strongly
Managing Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Common Supplier Information PUBLIC 89

agree . The other variation asks you to select their overall satisfaction with the software from Very dissatisfied  to
Very satisfied :
Both variations also ask you to provide written feedback regarding their experience with the software.
If you submit a response, you don't see the survey prompt again for 90 days. If you close the survey without
submitting a response, you're prompted again after 48 hours. If you click Ask me later , the survey closes and
you're prompted again in 25 hours.
These surveys are run by Qualtrics
 . All responses are transmitted directly to Qualtrics, which is hosted in the
European Union, and are governed by the Qualtrics terms of service
 . SAP Ariba has access to those responses
for internal use only.
 Note
Feedback surveys don't collect personal data in predefined  questions and answers, only data about SAP Ariba
applications. Don't enter any personal data in the comments.
If the supplier profile  summary (NGSM-2959) is enabled in your site, the feedback surveys aren't available there.

•CA-FLP-EXT-FX

Accessibility in Supplier Management
The supplier management application is enhanced to provide accessibility to people with special needs.
SAP Ariba has enhanced the accessibility of supplier management with the goal of eventually reaching adherence
with the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG). The supplier management user interface supports screen
readers to guide people with special needs through the application.
These accessibility enhancements, include the following changes:
•User interface elements are accurately labeled to ensure the users know what information can be entered in
the fields.
•User interface pages have titles that describe the purpose of the page/topic, helping the users to navigate
through the application.
•T ooltips are available for the user interface elements such as icons and graphics.
•Y ou can use keyboard navigation to access some of the user interface elements. Y ou can see a visible outline
around the user interface element that has the current keyboard focus. For more information see, Keyboard
Navigation .
Screen Readers
Y ou can use screens readers to read the user interface elements. SAP Ariba supports the following screen reader
programs.
•JAWS, from Freedom Scientific  Inc.
•VoiceOver from Apple Inc.
 Note
Other screen reader programs can work but aren't officially  tested by SAP Ariba.
Pages
SAP Ariba continues to enhance the accessibility in supplier management with the goal of reaching adherence
with the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG). Accessibility enhancements are available in Request ,
Registration  workflows,  supplier search page, supplier 360 profile,  and SM Administration .

Supplier Creation and Onboarding
Supplier Request Creation and Management
Approved supplier requests create new suppliers in your site. Depending on your role, you can request a new
supplier or approve a supplier request created by someone else in your organization.
There are two types of supplier requests:
•Internal supplier requests, where someone in your organization requests a new supplier. Depending on your
site's configuration,  this internal request can be the default request, or it can use a Dun & Bradstreet lookup to
add certain information about the supplier to the request automatically.
•External supplier requests, where a supplier requests to be added to your site. External supplier requests are
an optional feature and their availability depends on your site's configuration.
