Internal Supplier Requests
Internal supplier request projects allow internal users to request to work with new suppliers. When a supplier
request is approved, the supplier is created in your site.
Procurement agents, functional buyers, people running sourcing events, and others in your company who want to
work with new suppliers can request a new supplier by filling  out and submitting an internal supplier request form.
Note that there's a separate project for suppliers to create an external supplier request [page 94] .
The internal supplier request project template determines:
•What information users need to provide when submitting the request.
•Whether the request is automatically approved on submission or must be manually approved.
•If the request requires manual approval, which category or supplier managers approve the request, and
whether other stakeholders at your company must also approve the request.
Requesters create internal supplier requests from the Create  menu on the dashboard and monitor their progress in
the My Activities  area of the Supplier Management  dashboard. As the requester enters information in an

ved.
•If the request requires manual approval, which category or supplier managers approve the request, and
whether other stakeholders at your company must also approve the request.
Requesters create internal supplier requests from the Create  menu on the dashboard and monitor their progress in
the My Activities  area of the Supplier Management  dashboard. As the requester enters information in an internal
Managing Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Supplier Creation and Onboarding PUBLIC 93

supplier request, an automatic duplicate check presents them with possible matches to suppliers that already
exist in the database. The requester then has the option of canceling the request if they see a valid match or of
submitting the request if they don't believe any of the potential matches are duplicates.
If your site is integrated with Dun & Bradstreet (D&B), the supplier request workflow  also includes a step that looks
up the supplier in the Dun & Bradstreet database. If if the supplier isn't found, this workflow  can also include an
exception process for requesting a non-D&B supplier.
Notifications  inform approvers that the request requires approval. They can also see outstanding requests by
choosing 
 Manage
 My Tasks
  on the dashboard. Notifications  also inform the requester when the request has
been approved or denied, and show the final  approver so that the requester can have further discussions about
denied requests. Approvers also see a list of potential duplicate suppliers so that they can deny duplicate requests
and approve only requests for genuinely new suppliers.
When a supplier request is approved, either automatically or manually, the supplier is created in your site's supplier
database. The information in the approved external supplier request shows in the Supplier request  detail area of
the supplier's profile.  If your site is integrated with Dun & Bradstreet, the new supplier profile  includes data from
D&B.
If your site is integrated with SAP Master Data Governance for Suppliers (MDG-S), the supplier request workflow
can also include a final  step for validating approved supplier requests in SAP MDG-S before creating suppliers in
the database. If MDG-S identifies  the supplier as a duplicate, SAP Ariba marks the approved request as a duplicate,
hides it, and redirects the requester to the existing, matched supplier instead. This option is only supported if your
supplier requests don't use Dun & Bradstreet integration.
Newly created suppliers are eligible for registration, which gathers detailed profile  information about them. Y our
site's configuration  determines whether the supplier is automatically invited to register or a registration manager
can manually start an internal or external registration. If your site is integrated with SAP ERP , SAP S/4HANA,
or SAP MDG-S, your site's integration settings determine whether the

suppliers are eligible for registration, which gathers detailed profile  information about them. Y our
site's configuration  determines whether the supplier is automatically invited to register or a registration manager
can manually start an internal or external registration. If your site is integrated with SAP ERP , SAP S/4HANA,
or SAP MDG-S, your site's integration settings determine whether the new supplier is automatically or manually
synchronized with the integrated system's vendor master list either at this point or during supplier registration.

External Supplier Requests
External supplier request projects allow suppliers who want to do business with your organization to introduce
themselves. When an external request is approved, the supplier is created in your site.
External supplier request projects aren't available by default and are only available in sites where the feature is
enabled. As part of external supplier request enablement, SAP Ariba Support provides your organization with

request project for internal users at your company to request a new supplier [page 93] . External supplier requests

Requesting a New Supplier
Use these steps to create a supplier request when you want to work with a supplier that is not already in your
organization's vendor database.
Prerequisites
Y ou must also be a member of the Supplier Request Manager  or SM Ops Administrator  group to create supplier
requests.
Context
Creating a supplier request starts your organization's process for registering and managing the supplier.
While you're creating a supplier request, SAP Ariba searches the database to see if there are any existing suppliers
or previously denied requests with the same information and identifies  potential duplicates. If the supplier you're
requesting already exists in your site, or was previously denied by approvers, you can cancel the request.
Y ou can save a supplier requests as a draft so that you can exit the form without losing your current answers.
Y ou can complete and submit a draft supplier request later, or you can discard it. The draft supplier request isn't
validated until you submit it.
Once you have submitted a supplier request, you cannot delete it.
If your site is integrated with Dun & Bradstreet, refer to Requesting a New Supplier Using Dun & Bradstreet Lookup
[page 97] .
 Note
•For supplier request questionnaires that include Extended Address  questions, an answer in the Region
field  is required.
•Attachments in supplier management questionnaires can't include embedded documents.
Procedure
1. On the dashboard, choose 
 Create
 Supplier Request
 .
2. Enter information in the supplier request form.
3. If SAP Ariba identifies  any existing suppliers or previously denied requests that match the information in your
request, perform the following actions:
a. Choose See duplicates .
b. Review potential duplicate suppliers. If you agree with a suggested match, choose Cancel Request . If you
don't think any of the potential matches is the same supplier, choose Close .
96 PUBLICManaging Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Supplier Creation and Onboarding

4. Perform one of the following actions:
•T o submit the request for approval, choose Submit .
•T o save the request as a draft to finalize  and submit later, choose Save .
Results
If you submitted the supplier request form, it's sent for approval. Y ou receive a notification  when the request is
approved or denied. If your request is approved, the new supplier is added to the supplier database. If the request is
denied, it's not.
If you saved a draft of the supplier request, it's available on the Supplier Request  tile of the Supplier Management
dashboard. Y ou can also view the draft supplier requests you created in the My Documents  content item on the
Home  dashboard.

Requesting a New Supplier Using Dun & Bradstreet Lookup
When you want to work with a supplier that isn't already in your site's vendor database, use these steps to create
an internal supplier request. Y our organization requires that you try to match potential new suppliers to the Dun &
Bradstreet (D&B) supplier database before creating the request.
Prerequisites
The supplier request with Dun & Bradstreet lookup is only available in sites with the D&B integration feature
enabled.
T o create a supplier request, you must be a member of the Supplier Request Manager  or SM Ops Administrator
group.
If you request a D&B supplier, answers that are automatically filled  in by D&B data aren't editable by default, even
if there's no D&B data available for the supplier you selected. If the self-service configuration  parameter Enable
Extended Address editing in supplier requests using Dun & Bradstreet lookup  is enabled in your site, you can
edit supplier address answers before submitting the request.
Managing Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Supplier Creation and Onboarding PUBLIC 97

Context
Creating a supplier request starts your organization's process for registering and managing the supplier.
T o create a supplier request using Dun & Bradstreet lookup, you search for the supplier you want to request in the
D&B database. If the supplier is in the D&B database, you select it, then fill out the request form. D&B information
for supplier name, address, and Dun & Bradstreet ID (D-U-N-S number) are automatically added to the request
form.
If you don't see the supplier you want to request in the D&B database, you can perform one of the following actions:
•Ask the supplier to register with D&B. Once they've done so, you can create the request by selecting the
supplier in the D&B search.
•Fill out the request even though you can't find  the supplier in the D&B database. In this case, the supplier
probably isn't registered with D&B, and the supplier request includes questions about why you need an
exception to your organization's general requirement that all suppliers have D-U-N-S numbers. Provide the
supplier name and address information that would otherwise automatically be added to the request by D&B.
Depending on your organization's processes, approval of requests for suppliers that aren't registered with D&B
can be different.
While you're creating a supplier request, SAP Ariba searches the database to see if there are any existing suppliers
or previously denied requests with the same information and identifies  potential duplicates. If the supplier you're
requesting already exists in your site, or was previously denied by approvers, you can cancel the request.
Y ou can save a supplier requests as a draft so that you can exit the form without losing your current answers.
Y ou can complete and submit a draft supplier request later, or you can discard it. The draft supplier request isn't
validated until you submit it.
 Note
•Lookups through the Dun & Bradstreet Data Integration T oolkit are in principle free of charge unless there's
a misuse of the current customer agreement.
•Attachments in supplier management questionnaires can't include embedded documents.

 Create

 .
2. On the Search Supplier  by dropdown menu, choose one of the following options:
•D-U-N-S Number
•Phone number
•Name
•Business ID (T ax ID, VAT ID, other ID)
•Email or website
3. Enter search criteria

Resubmitting a Supplier Request When an Approver Asks
for Additional Information
If an approver asks for additional information about a supplier request you created, use these steps to edit the
request, make the necessary changes, and resubmit them for approval.
Context
If an approver asks for additional information about a supplier request that you created, it moves from In Approval
status to Pending Resubmit  status. Edit the request to provide the requested information and resubmit it so that it
can be approved.
The email notification  you receive includes comments from the approver detailing the additional information
they're asking for.
Procedure
1. Perform one of the following actions:
•Choose the link in the notification  email to reopen the request.
•In the My Activities  area of the Supplier Management  dashboard tab, choose the Supplier Requests  tile,
locate the request you need to resubmit, and choose View .
2. Choose Edit .
3. Make the requested changes.
4. Choose Save .
Results
The supplier request moves back from Pending Resubmit  status to In Approval  status and the approval flow
restarts.

Approving or Denying a Supplier Request
If you're in the approval flow  for a supplier request, use these steps to approve or deny it. Approving a supplier
request creates the supplier in your site.
Prerequisites
T o edit a supplier request while it's in approval:
•If the self-service configuration  parameter Application.SM.SupplierRequest.RestrictRequestEdit  is enabled
in SM Administration , you must be a member of the Supplier Request Manager  group who is also either the
project owner (the person who created the supplier request project) or a currently active approver.
•If the parameter is disabled, you must be a member of the Supplier Request Manager  group.
If your organization uses the supplier request with Dun & Bradstreet lookup and the requester has selected a D&B
supplier, certain questions are automatically filled  in with D&B data where it's available, including address answers.
These answers aren't editable by default, even if there's no data from D&B. If the self-service configuration
parameter Enable Extended Address editing in supplier requests using Dun & Bradstreet lookup  is enabled in
Intelligent Configuration  Manager  and you otherwise have permission to edit the request during approval, you can
also edit address answers.
Context
People in your organization submit internal supplier requests to add suppliers they want to work with to your site.
If the external supplier request feature (SM-2861) is enabled in your site, suppliers outside of your organization can
also submit supplier requests on their own behalf to introduce themselves.
Approvers can edit supplier requests to add missing information, correct mistakes, or supply information that can
only be added by approvers, before approving the request and creating the supplier.
When a requester submits a supplier request, SAP Ariba searches the database to see if there are any existing
suppliers or previously denied requests with the same information and identifies  potential duplicates. If you believe
that the requested supplier already exists in your site, you can prevent duplicate supplier records by denying the
request.

4. If the information in the request matches any existing suppliers or previously denied requests, choose See

5. Perform one of the following actions:

How Duplicate Checks Work in Submitted Supplier
Requests
Duplicate checks help prevent the creation of duplicate records for the same supplier in the database. After a
requester submits an internal supplier request, or a supplier submits an external supplier request, a duplicate
check matches its information to existing suppliers to identify potential duplicates.
The information in the request that contributes to the duplicate check depends on your site's configuration  and
the setup of your organization's supplier requests. External supplier requests and duplicate check based on custom
questions are both optional features.
The supplier request duplicate check searches for existing suppliers in your site's vendor database, by a
combination of:
•Exact match to Dun & Bradstreet ID (D-U-N-S Number).
•Exact match to country/region-specific  tax IDs in internal supplier requests only, if your organization uses
default duplicate check based on tax IDs.
•Strict match to the full supplier name, based on the following conditions:
•Company extensions such as LLC, INC, Incorporated, are truncated when performing a duplicate check.
For example, if your site has an existing supplier with the value "First Light Diagnostics LLC", the duplicate
check removes "LLC" from the supplier name and performs a search only for "First Light Diagnostics".
Note that the supplier full names support the following company extensions but truncate them during
duplicate checks: “co” , “company” , “corp” , “corporation” , “incorporated” , “inc” , “llc” , “llp” , “sa” , “sl” , “srl” ,
“ag” , “ltd” , “gmbh” .
•When searching, if the supplier name matches any of these vendor database fields  in your site: name1,
name2, name3, or name4. For example, a supplier in your site has “First” , “Light” , “Medical” , and
“Diagnostics” as values for name1, name2, name3, and name4 respectively. Consequently, a search for
"Medical" returns every supplier with the value “Medical” in name1, name2, name3, or name4. Therefore,
“SMR Medical” , “Medical Diagnostics” ,

ny of these vendor database fields  in your site: name1,
name2, name3, or name4. For example, a supplier in your site has “First” , “Light” , “Medical” , and
“Diagnostics” as values for name1, name2, name3, and name4 respectively. Consequently, a search for
"Medical" returns every supplier with the value “Medical” in name1, name2, name3, or name4. Therefore,
“SMR Medical” , “Medical Diagnostics” , “Health and Medical” and so on are all potential matches for the
search.
•In a supplier search, the exact full match is shown regardless of the supplier's status, whether approved,
registered, draft, and so on.
•At least a partial match to supplier name followed by a partial or full address match. At least a partial name
match must occur before address matching is attempted.
•If your site uses custom duplicate check, partial matches to answers are also used for custom duplication
check. For custom duplicate check, the answer must match starting with the first  character. If the answer
contains any spaces, each space-separated word or group of characters is matched separately, and each must
either fully or partially match starting with the first  character.
Managing Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Supplier Creation and Onboarding PUBLIC 105

For example, if your site has an existing supplier with the value "Green Energy Corporation" in a field  used for
a custom duplicate check, "Green," Energy," and "Corporation" are all indexed for matching separately. Any
corresponding supplier request answer that starts with "G," "E," or "C" is considered for matching, and answers
such as "Gre," "Energ," and "Corp" are matched. Answers such as "Greed," "Reen," and "Gre Energion" aren't
matched.
Matching is case insensitive. The duplicate check scores different  matches based on predefined  logic. The score
is expressed as a percentage between 0 and 100, with scores of 70% and above considered strong matches and
scores of less than 70% considered weak matches.
In match scoring, a D-U-N-S Number match scores either 100% for an exact match or 0% for no match. Strict
name matches score 100%, while partial name matches can vary between 0% and 100%. For matches on country/
region, the duplicate check assigns maximum scores to exact matches but also accepts synonyms, ignoring case,
and scores synonym matches based on degree of similarity to the request answer, with an exact string match
scoring 100%. The duplicate check assigns a total matching score to existing, potentially matching suppliers based
on all matching fields.
When the requester submits the supplier request (for internal requests only) and during supplier request approval
(for both internal and external supplier requests), the duplicate check displays the top 20 matching suppliers,
based on match score, in the following order:
•Exact matches (score of 100%) first.
•Partial matches next, in descending order based on score.
The duplicate check popup includes information on which answers in the current supplier request match to existing
suppliers.
 Restriction
•For default duplicate check on supplier D-U-N-S Number, name, and address, the duplicate check cleanses
the values in those fields  and replaces newlines, white spaces, and ASCII punctuation with a single space
before matching values to existing suppliers. Because of this cleansing, a new supplier request with a
supplier name of "Supplier A, Ltd." matches an existing supplier name "Supplier A Ltd" but not an existing
supplier named "Supplier A, Ltd.".
•Default duplicate checks match against existing suppliers in the database regardless of how the
matching supplier information was added. Default

gle space
before matching values to existing suppliers. Because of this cleansing, a new supplier request with a
supplier name of "Supplier A, Ltd." matches an existing supplier name "Supplier A Ltd" but not an existing
supplier named "Supplier A, Ltd.".
•Default duplicate checks match against existing suppliers in the database regardless of how the
matching supplier information was added. Default duplicate check against tax IDs in supplier registration
questionnaires with the required configuration  is only supported for answers added by questionnaire
recipients. It's not supported for answers added by registration questionnaire imports.
Custom duplicate checks match answers in internal or external supplier requests against mapped
answers from existing submitted supplier requests. They don't match against mapped data that originates

from sources outside of supplier requests, such as data import, ERP integration, or other types of
questionnaires.
•Duplicate checks don't match against denied supplier requests or inactivated suppliers.

Supplier Registration Management
Supplier registrations collect and maintain detailed profile  information about your organization's suppliers.
Depending on your role, you can start a supplier registration, fill out registration questionnaires, approve or deny
registration questionnaires, or cancel an in-progress registration.
Supplier registrations typically collect detailed profile  information about the supplier, often information that's
required before your organization can start transacting with them. For example, registration questionnaires can
collect essential bank and tax information. Because this profile  information can change over time, supplier
registrations have two stages:
Managing Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Supplier Creation and Onboarding PUBLIC 107

•New registration,  where a registration manager starts a registration, questionnaire recipients provide initial
profile  information, and approvers vet this information and approve or deny the registration.
•Registration updates,  where questionnaire recipients update previous answers where necessary after the
supplier is registered.

New Supplier Registrations
Starting a new supplier registration creates a registration project with questionnaires to gather detailed profile
information about a supplier.
After a new supplier is created, a registration manager starts a supplier registration by sending one or more
external registration questionnaires to recipients. Registration projects can also include internal questionnaires,
and starting the registration makes them available for editing. Approvers review the submitted questionnaires and
approve or deny them. If the new registration is approved, the registration project then supports ongoing updates
to both external and internal questionnaires.
There are two types of supplier registrations:

The registration process typically asks for detailed profile  information about the supplier, such as the commodities
they supply, the regions in which they operate, what standards they adhere to or agree with, financial  details, and
so forth. Depending on the way registration is set up in your site, it can include one or more external registration
questionnaires to gather this information.
Supplier registration projects can also include internal questionnaires that allow internal stakeholders to answer
questions about the supplier based on your organization's internal processes and to add internally important
information to the supplier's profile.
It's possible to cancel a registration while it's in progress. Canceling is the only way to convert an external
registration to an internal one after the supplier has been invited, and is useful if an external registration is stalled
and you want to complete it internally instead.
The supplier registration project template determines:
•The number of external registration questionnaires and the number of internal questionnaires (if any), and
their content.
•Registration approvers, and whether or not there are different  approval flows  and other tasks for new
registrations versus questionnaire updates.
•Whether other tasks are part of the registration workflow.
•Whether or not denial of an internal questionnaire affects  the supplier's registration status in a new
registration.
•How long external questionnaires remain open so that suppliers can submit their answers.
Users with the appropriate permissions start the registration, send one or more external questionnaires to
recipients, fill out any internal questionnaires, review the questionnaire responses, request more information,
complete T o Do tasks, approve or deny the registration, and view its progress and status in the Registration  detail
area of the supplier's profile.
In external registrations, the supplier submits external registration questionnaires in their SAP Business Network
profile.  T o do so, they must create an SAP Business Network account if they don't already have one. The supplier's
profile  is automatically shared between SAP Business Network and SAP Ariba Supplier Management solutions.
Once registration is complete, the supplier is public, meaning that they have a linked SAP Business Network
account in your site.
In internal

ires in their SAP Business Network
profile.  T o do so, they must create an SAP Business Network account if they don't already have one. The supplier's
profile  is automatically shared between SAP Business Network and SAP Ariba Supplier Management solutions.
Once registration is complete, the supplier is public, meaning that they have a linked SAP Business Network
account in your site.
In internal registrations, internal recipients in your organization submit external registration questionnaires in your
site and the supplier doesn't participate at all. Internal registrations don't establish communications with the
supplier through SAP Business Network. Unless you've interacted with the supplier through SAP Business Network
for another activity, such as a sourcing event or an external modular questionnaire, they remain private, meaning
that they don't have a linked SAP Business Network account in your site.
In registrations that are done using the Set Registered  option, registration managers choose this option in the
supplier's profile  and the supplier doesn't participate at all. The Set Registered  option is available only after the
supplier request is approved. When registration managers choose this option, the system immediately sets the
status of the supplier to Registered . In addition, the system synchronizes supplier data with your ERP systems
automatically or with manual intervention depending on how your site is configured.  This data then becomes
available for supplier qualifications  or for use in other SAP Ariba procurement applications. After setting a supplier
as Registered , you can still start an internal or external registration project. However, if you do so, the registration
process starts and the Set Registered  option is only available again if you cancel the newly started registration.
Notifications  inform:
•Questionnaire recipients that they've been invited to register, including a link to the external questionnaires.
•The requester for the supplier that the registration has started.
110 PUBLICManaging Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Supplier Creation and Onboarding

•Approvers that they need to approve a new or updated registration questionnaire.
•Internal stakeholders that they need to complete other tasks.
•The supplier that the registration has been approved, denied, or requires more information (in external
registrations only).
If a registration invitation includes multiple external questionnaires, questionnaire recipients receive a separate
notification  for each questionnaire. If the registration has been canceled, notifications  inform registration managers
of the cancellation.
As your organization's registration process evolves over time, an administrator can upgrade existing registration
projects to the latest version of the template. An upgrade can include changes to internal or external
questionnaires, tasks, and approval flows.  The administrator can opt to sent notifications  about the upgrade
to supplier contacts so that they can update affected  questionnaires. Internal users don't receive upgrade
notifications.
If your site is integrated with an SAP ERP system, your site's integration settings determine whether a new
supplier is automatically or manually synchronized with your ERP vendor master list. Many organizations require
registration before synchronizing a new supplier to the ERP system.
For suppliers with external registrations, additional settings allow registration managers to establish a fulfillment
relationship with the supplier as they register, at the same time as the sourcing relationship is established.
In solutions that include SAP Ariba Sourcing, event templates can specify that suppliers must be registered to
either participate in or be awarded events. There's also a configuration  option for requiring a minimum registration
status for participation in sourcing events.

Supplier Registration Updates
After a supplier is registered, external questionnaire recipients and people in your organization can update
questionnaires to keep profile  information for the supplier current.
Supplier registration projects can be set up to support different  approval flows  for new registrations and for
updates to individual questionnaires.
When a new registration starts, external registration questionnaires are open for the amount of time defined  in
the template questionnaire's timing rules. Once the recipient submits the questionnaire the first  time, it closes
automatically. After all of the tasks in a new registration are completed and the supplier has Registered  status,
previously closed external questionnaires automatically reopen for an amount of time defined  by your site's
configuration  and configurations  in individual questionnaires. The default amount of time a questionnaire remains
open is 365 days from the approval. The recipient assigned to the external questionnaires in a registration can
navigate to them and submit updates at any time when they're not in approval. External questionnaires can be set
up to generate periodic reminders asking suppliers for updates.
Internal registration questionnaires are also always open for update when they're not in approval.
In a new registration, once the recipient invited to the registration opens at least one questionnaire, only they
can access the external questionnaires in the registration. In internal registrations, the registration project owner
can also access external questionnaires. After the new registration is approved, you can reassign external
questionnaires to a different  recipient at any time when no external questionnaire updates are in approval.
Only the assigned recipient or, in internal registrations, the registration project owner, can update the external
questionnaires.
If a registration starts as an internal registration, meaning that people in your organization submit questionnaires
on behalf of the supplier, internal recipients can continue to update those questionnaires. A registration manager
can invite the supplier to an internal registration, transferring ownership of external questionnaires to a supplier
contact. When a supplier contact submits external questionnaires from a registration that was previously internal
for the first  time, that initial

ubmit questionnaires
on behalf of the supplier, internal recipients can continue to update those questionnaires. A registration manager
can invite the supplier to an internal registration, transferring ownership of external questionnaires to a supplier
contact. When a supplier contact submits external questionnaires from a registration that was previously internal
for the first  time, that initial submission is also treated as an updated.
As your organization's registration process evolves over time, an administrator in your organization can upgrade
existing registration projects to the latest version of the template. An upgrade can include changes to internal
or external questionnaires, tasks, and approval flows.  The administrator can opt to sent notifications  about the
upgrade to supplier contacts so that they can update affected  questionnaires. Internal users don't receive upgrade
notifications.  Suppliers or internal recipients can also choose to update registrations for other reasons.
When a recipient submits an update to an external registration questionnaire, the supplier remains in Registered
registration status, but has a separate Pending Approval  registration update status.
The new registration workflow  includes all of the tasks associated with all of the questionnaires in a new
registration. If your registration projects include one or more registration update phases to define  tasks for
registration updates, each questionnaire update is handled individually. When a respondent submits an update
to an external or internal registration questionnaire, only the tasks associated with that questionnaire start, and
task owners or approvers can complete the tasks related to the questionnaire being updated. When there are no
remaining tasks in progress for the questionnaire that is being approved and no tasks in progress for the other
questionnaires in the update phase, the update status is Approved .
If the registration update isn't set to auto-approve, approvers approve or request more information for registration
updates. If the updates are approved, they're added to the supplier's profile.  The supplier remains in Registered
registration status, but has Approved  registration update status. The registration update status is always based on
the entire workflow  defined  by all of the tasks in registration update phases.

The registration status reflects  the supplier's progress through the full initial registration process where your
organization collects important profile  information from them. Registration update status is useful mainly for
tracking individual updates, which typically involve small changes to that profile  information, and can be used
to create conditions in the registration project. Registration update status does't show in the supplier's profile.
However, you can search for registration projects using the search bar on the Home  dashboard and filter  the results
by registration update status. If you have permission to access the advanced view of a registration project, you
can see also its registration update status on the Overview  tab. T o see the advanced of a registration project,
the Advanced View  link for that project type must be enabled in your site and you must have the appropriate
permissions.

About the Registration  Detail Area in the Supplier Profile
Y ou invite a supplier to register and view and manage their registration, including internal profile  questionnaires, in
the Registration  detail area of a supplier's profile.
Every supplier profile  includes a Registration  detail area, whether or not the supplier is registered.
114 PUBLICManaging Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Supplier Creation and Onboarding

Once a supplier is invited to register, the Registration  area includes the following information:
•A summary of the registration, including who invited the supplier to register and on what date, the invited
supplier contact, and their email address.
•A process flow,  which shows the steps of the registration project workflow.  The currently active step is
highlighted in blue, while completed steps are highlighted in green. Once registration tasks have started, they
show in the process flow,  with the names of the people or groups responsible for completing them enclosed by
brackets ([ ]). Y ou can choose a task node to see a complete list of approvers or task owners, including a list of
group members when the approver or task is a project group.
For new registrations, the process flow  shows all tasks associated with the registration. For registration
updates, if your site uses tasks in one or more registration update phases, the process flow  shows only the
steps associated with the most recently updated questionnaire.
•All internal and external questionnaires that are included in the registration.
•All T o Do and approval tasks associated with the registration.
•In sites that include approval tasks for registration updates, an alert message that includes the name of any
updated questionnaires that have pending approvals.
If you have permission, you can do the following things in the Registration  detail area:
•Invite [page 116]  or reinvite [page 120]  a new supplier to register.
•Start an internal registration [page 122]  on behalf of a supplier.

•If the status tracker for email notifications  feature (SM-5116) is enabled in your site, see whether registration

contacts in external registrations. It doesn't track notifications  to internal recipients of external questionnaires
in internal registrations.
 Note
If this feature is enabled, the date of reinvitation doesn't update once the supplier is public.
•T rack the progress of the registration in the process flow  graph.
•View the answers to both internal and external registration questionnaires in the Questionnaires  table.
•Fill out and submit internal registration questionnaires [page 128]  in the Questionnaires  table if you have the
appropriate permissions.
•View all tasks associated with the registration in the Tasks  table and view the details of completed tasks.

Inviting a Supplier to Register
Use these steps to invite a supplier to register. Supplier registration gathers detailed profile  information about a
supplier so that your organization can assess them. Inviting a supplier to register creates a supplier registration
project for them, invites them to fill out external registration questionnaires, and makes internal registration
questionnaires available for editing.
116 PUBLICManaging Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Supplier Creation and Onboarding

Prerequisites
T o invite a supplier to register, you must be a member of the Supplier Registration Manager  or SM Ops
Administrator  group.
T o specify a requester for the registration during invitation, the requester selection feature (SM-20904) must be
enabled in your site.
Context
Inviting a supplier to register asks them to participate in their registration from the beginning by filling  out
external questionnaires. If you want to register a supplier without asking them to fill out external questionnaires,
you can start an internal registration instead. In an internal registration, recipients in your organization submit
questionnaires on the supplier's behalf instead.
Y our organization's registration projects can have one or more external (supplier-facing) registration
questionnaires. If there are multiple external questionnaires, some of them can be optional. Y ou can choose which
optional external questionnaires to send with the registration invitation. Each external registration questionnaire
generates a separate email invitation to the supplier contact.
If the requester selection during registration invitation feature is enabled in your site, you can also choose the
requester for invitations to fill out registration questionnaires. Choosing the requester allows you to identify
someone in your organization with whom the supplier is familiar as the sender of those invitations. The name
of the requester you choose shows in the "From" field  in registration invitations to the supplier. Depending on how
your organization has set up registration invitations, the requester's name can also show in other places in those
emails as well. For suppliers created from approved supplier requests, the default registration requester is the
owner of the supplier request project, which can be a person with whom the supplier already has a relationship. If
you don't choose a registration requester, registration invitations use whatever default sender is set up for them,
which can be a generic system alias, a custom alias, or you as the registration project owner.
Procedure
1. Locate the supplier on the Supplier Management  dashboard.
 Tip
In supplier search results, you can filter  by the registration status Not Invited  to show new suppliers that
haven't yet been invited to register.
2. Choose the supplier's name to open their profile.
3. Perform one of the following

can be a generic system alias, a custom alias, or you as the registration project owner.
Procedure
1. Locate the supplier on the Supplier Management  dashboard.
 Tip
In supplier search results, you can filter  by the registration status Not Invited  to show new suppliers that
haven't yet been invited to register.
2. Choose the supplier's name to open their profile.
3. Perform one of the following actions:
•In the supplier's profile  summary, choose the registration status link in the Supplier Status  panel.
•In the classic supplier 360° profile,  choose Registration  in the left-hand navigation bar.
The Registration  detail area of the supplier's profile  opens.
4. Choose Invite .
Managing Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Supplier Creation and Onboarding PUBLIC 117

5. Select the optional questionnaires that you want to send to the supplier if the registration project includes
optional questionnaires. If there's only one questionnaire, or if a questionnaire is required, it automatically
selected and you can't deselect it.
T o preview a questionnaire, choose  (view).
6. Choose the contact to whom you want to send the registration invitation. If the supplier has only one contact,
it's automatically selected. If the contact you want to invite isn't in the list of available contacts and you have
the appropriate permissions, you can add them now.
7 .Optional: If the select requester feature is available in your site, perform one of the following actions:
•If there's no requester set for the registration and you want to make an individual person in your
organization the requester, choose Select requester , select that person, and choose Confirm .
•If there's already a requester for the registration and you want to select a different  requester, choose
Change requester , select a different  person in your organization, and choose Confirm .
8. Choose Send invite .
Results
The supplier now has the status Invited . The supplier contact that you selected receives an invitation for each
external questionnaire you sent. The supplier contact can use the links in the invitation emails to sign into an
existing account or create a new account on SAP Business Network and fill out and submit the questionnaires.
The Registration  detail area of the supplier's profile  now shows the external questionnaires that you sent and
the supplier contact you sent them to. It also shows any internal questionnaires that your organization uses for
registration and all tasks for the registration. T o view a questionnaire or the details for a completed task, choose
View  ( ).
If the status tracker for supplier management email notifications  feature is enabled in your site, the Registration
area also shows Email Status  and Email Status Date . The Email Status  lets you know if the registration invitation
email has been Delivered , Opened , or Bounced.
118 PUBLICManaging Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Supplier Creation and Onboarding

T echnical changes have been made to the email status tracker for supplier registration invitations. As a result, data
from June 2023 onwards is accessible. This transition keeps all customer data intact.
If you chose a registration requester, the invitation email notifications  to the supplier show the requester's name in
the "From" field.  Otherwise, the name and email address in those fields  is determined by how your organization has
set up each registration questionnaire.
 Note
On SAP Business Network, suppliers see an option to decline to participate in a supplier management
questionnaire in the same way they can decline to participate in a sourcing event. If the supplier contact
you invited to register declined to participate in a registration questionnaire, they can no longer access the
registration questionnaire, but the supplier remains in Invited  status.
Next Steps
While the supplier is in Invited  status but before the invited contact has responded to your invitation, you can
cancel the registration if necessary. For example, if the supplier contact hasn't responded and you need the
supplier to achieve Registered  status for business continuity or other reasons, you can cancel the external
registration and start an internal one. After the supplier contact has responded to the invitation, you can only
cancel the registration under certain limited circumstances.
External registration questionnaires close after an amount of time defined  in your site's questionnaire setup. After
a questionnaire has closed, the supplier contact can no longer edit it to fill it out. If a supplier hasn't responded, or
if the invitation has bounced, reinviting the supplier to register reopens any closed questionnaires and allows you
to choose a different  recipient if necessary. By default, questionnaires close 30 days after the invitation is sent, but
your organization can specify a different  amount of time in its questionnaire setup.
Y ou can track registration progress using the Supplier Onboarding Progress  data snapshot or analytical reports.

Reinviting a Supplier to Register
Use these steps to remind a supplier to respond to an initial registration invitation or to change the invited supplier
contact.
Prerequisites
T o reinvite a supplier to register, you must be a member of the Supplier Registration Manager  or SM Ops
Administrator  group.
T o add or edit a supplier contact on the send questionnaires page, you must be a member of the Supplier
Registration Manager  or SM Ops Administrator  group.
Y ou can only reinvite a supplier to register if their registration is in Invited  status. Once the supplier has opened
and viewed an external questionnaire and the registration has moved to In Registration  status, you can no longer
reinvite them. If the supplier doesn't submit the questionnaire, you must use another method to remind them.
T o specify a requester for the registration during invitation, the requester selection feature (SM-20904) must be
enabled in your site.
Context
Reinviting a supplier is useful if:
•The previously invited contact is the right person to complete the registration but they need a reminder.
Reinviting the same contact sends them another invitation as a reminder.
•The previously invited contact isn't the right person to complete the registration.  Y ou can choose a
different  contact when reinviting the supplier.
•External questionnaires have closed before the invited contact responded.  External questionnaires close
after an amount of time defined  in your site's questionnaire setup. By default, that amount is 30 days, but
your organization can specify a different  amount of time in its questionnaire setup. After a questionnaire has
closed, the supplier contact can no longer edit it to fill it out. Reinviting the supplier reopens the external
questionnaires.
•The initial invitation didn't specify a requester or a different  person in your organization is a more suitable
requester : The requester you choose shows in the "From" field  in registration invitations to the supplier, and
depending on how your organization has set up registration invitations, can also show in other places in those
emails as well. Reinviting the supplier and resends questionnaire invitation emails with the requester you
choose.
Y ou can't change which external questionnaires are included when you reinvite a supplier to register. The
registration always includes the external questionnaires

plier, and
depending on how your organization has set up registration invitations, can also show in other places in those
emails as well. Reinviting the supplier and resends questionnaire invitation emails with the requester you
choose.
Y ou can't change which external questionnaires are included when you reinvite a supplier to register. The
registration always includes the external questionnaires selected during the original invitation.

Supplier Creation and Onboarding

 Tip
In supplier search results, you can filter  by the registration status Invited  to show new suppliers who have
been invited to register but haven't responded yet.
2. Choose the supplier's name to open their profile.
3. Navigate to the Registrations  detail area of the profile.
4. In the Questionnaires  table, for any external registration questionnaire, choose   (more options) > Reinvite
 .
5.Optional: T o change the questionnaire recipient, choose a different  contact. If the supplier only has one
contact, that contact is automatically selected and you can't uncheck it. If you have the appropriate
permissions, you can add or edit a contact now [page 54]  to make sure you're sending the invitation to the
recipient you want at the correct email address.
6.Optional: If the select requester feature is available in your site, perform one of the following actions:
•If there'sn't a requester set for the registration and you want to make an individual person in your

•If there's already a requester for the registration and you want to select a different  requester, choose
Change requester , select a different  person in your organization, and chose Confirm .
7 . Choose Send .
Results
The supplier contact you selected receives a new invitation for each external questionnaire in the registration with
the requester you selected. The supplier contact can use the links in the emails to create an account and fill out and
submit the questionnaires.
If you added or edited a supplier contact while reinviting the supplier, those changes show in the Contacts  detail

Starting an Internal Supplier Registration on Behalf of a
Supplier
Use these steps to start an internal registration and invite other people in your organization to fill out external
questionnaires on behalf of a supplier. An internal registration doesn't require the supplier to sign into SAP
Business Network to fill out and submit the questionnaires. Supplier registration gathers detailed profile
information about a supplier.
Prerequisites
Y ou must be a member of the Supplier Registration Manager  or SM Ops Administrator  group to start an internal
registration on behalf of a supplier.
Context
In an internal registration, people in your organization submit external registration questionnaires on behalf of
a supplier. The supplier doesn't participate in the registration. Y our organization's supplier registration projects
can have one or more external registration questionnaires, some of which can be optional. Y ou can choose which
optional questionnaires to send to recipients. Y ou choose one internal recipient for each external questionnaire, and
you can choose different  recipients for different  questionnaires. A recipient can be any internal user in your site.
Each external registration questionnaire generates a separate email notification  to the recipient.
Y ou can start an internal registration for a supplier if they have a registration status of:
•Not Invited  because no one has started a registration for them before.
•Registered  because of an internal supplier registration data import. In this case, the data import sets the
supplier's registration status but doesn't create a registration project with tasks and questionnaires. Starting
an internal registration creates the registration project so that recipients in your organization can fill out
questionnaires and approvers and task owners can complete tasks in the registration workflow.
 Note
After the internal registration is completed, registration managers have the option of inviting the supplier
to participate in the registration and fill out the external questionnaires. There's currently no way to change
which external questionnaires are included in a registration after it's first  started. Therefore, when you start an
internal registration and choose the questionnaires to include in it, keep in mind that the supplier can eventually
participate in the registration.
Procedure
1. Locate the

o participate in the registration and fill out the external questionnaires. There's currently no way to change
which external questionnaires are included in a registration after it's first  started. Therefore, when you start an
internal registration and choose the questionnaires to include in it, keep in mind that the supplier can eventually
participate in the registration.
Procedure
1. Locate the supplier that you want to register on the Supplier Management  dashboard.
122 PUBLICManaging Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Supplier Creation and Onboarding

 Tip
In supplier search results, you can filter  by the registration status Not Invited  to show new suppliers who
haven't yet been invited to register. However, there's no way to distinguish suppliers who have a status of
Registered  through an internal registration data import, and for whom you can start an internal registration
manually, in supplier searches.
2. Choose the supplier's name to open their profile.
3. Perform one of the following actions:
•In the supplier's profile  summary, choose the registration status link in the Supplier Status  panel.
•In the classic supplier 360° profile,  choose Registration  in the left-hand navigation bar.
The Registration  detail area of the supplier's profile  opens.
4. Choose Start internal registration .
5. On the confirmation  popup, choose Start registration  to confirm  that you want to start the internal
registration.
6. Select any optional questionnaires that you want to include in the registration. If there's only one questionnaire,
or if a questionnaire is required, it's automatically selected and you can't uncheck it.
7 . For each questionnaire included in the registration, perform the following steps:
a. Choose Choose recipient (  ).
b. Search for and select the person in your organization who you want to make the recipient of the
questionnaire.
c. Choose Confirm .
8. Choose Send .
Managing Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Supplier Creation and Onboarding PUBLIC 123

Results
The supplier now has the status Invited . The recipients you selected receive notifications  for each external
questionnaire you included in the registration. They can use links in the notifications  or navigate to the supplier's
360° profile  to edit and submit the external questionnaires to which they're assigned.
The Registration  detail area of the supplier's profile  now shows the external questionnaires that you included in the
registration, any internal questionnaires your organization uses for registration, and all registration tasks. T o view
questionnaire or task details, choose View  (  ). The Supplier recipient  field  shows the name and email address of
the internal recipient for the first  listed questionnaire. The questionnaire details page for each external registration
questionnaire shows the name of its individual internal recipient.
Next Steps
In internal registrations, external questionnaires remain open regardless of due date settings in the questionnaire
setup. However, if a recipient hasn't responded, reinviting the recipient sends a new email invitation or allows you to
choose a different  recipient if necessary.
After the internal registration is approved, if you later decide you want to establish communications with the
supplier and have them provide registration information, you can invite them to participate and convert the internal
registration to an external registration.

Setting a Supplier as Registered Using the Set Registered
Option
Use these steps to set a supplier as Registered  without having to go through an internal or external registration
process.
Prerequisites
Y ou must be a member of the Supplier Registration Manager  or SM Ops Administrator  group to set a supplier's
status as Registered .
Context
In registrations that are done using the Set Registered  option, internal users in your organization choose this
option in the supplier's profile  and the supplier doesn't participate at all. The Set Registered  option is available only
after the supplier request is approved and the supplier is in Not Invited  status.
Procedure
1. Locate the supplier you want to register on the Supplier Management  dashboard.
 Tip
In supplier search results, you can filter  by the registration status Not Invited  to show new suppliers who
haven't yet been invited to register.
2. Choose the supplier's name to open their profile.
3. Perform one of the following actions:
•In the supplier's profile  summary, choose the registration status link in the Supplier Status  panel.
•In the classic supplier 360° profile,  choose Registration  in the left-hand navigation bar.
The Registration  detail area of the supplier's profile  opens.
4. Choose Set Registered .
Results
When you choose this option, the system immediately sets the status of the supplier to Registered . In addition, the
system synchronizes supplier data with your ERP systems automatically or with manual intervention depending on
how your site is configured.  This data then becomes available for supplier qualifications  or for use in other SAP
Ariba procurement applications.
Managing Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Supplier Creation and Onboarding PUBLIC 125

Next Steps
After setting a supplier as Registered , you can still start an internal or external registration. However, if you do so,
the registration process starts and the Set Registered  option won't be available again unless you cancel the newly
started registration.

Reinviting Internal Recipients to an Internal Supplier
Registration
Use these steps to reinvite internal recipients to an internal registration if they haven't responded to a previous
invitation and you want to prompt them to do so, or if you want to change a questionnaire's recipient.
Prerequisites
T o reinvite internal recipients to an internal registration, you must be a member of the Supplier Registration
Manager  or SM Ops Administrator  group.
Y ou can only reinvite recipients to an internal registration, or change the internal recipients of external registration
questionnaires, if the registration is in Invited  or Registration Denied  status. For registrations in Invited  status,
once an internal recipient has opened and viewed an external questionnaire and the registration has moved to In
Registration  status, you can no longer reinvite them.
Context
In an internal registration, people in your organization submit external registration questionnaires on behalf of a
supplier. Y ou can choose different  recipients for different  questionnaires. A recipient can be any internal user in
your site. Each external registration questionnaire generates a separate email notification  to the recipient.
Reinviting internal recipients to an internal registration is useful if the previously invited recipient:
•Is the right person to complete the external registration questionnaire but hasn't responded to the earlier
invitation. Reinviting the same recipient sends them another invitation as a reminder.
•Isn't the right person to complete the registration questionnaire. Y ou can choose a different  recipient when
reinviting them.
126 PUBLICManaging Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Supplier Creation and Onboarding

Y ou can't change which external questionnaires are included in the registration when you reinvite recipients to
an internal registration. The registration always includes the external questionnaires selected during the original

1. On the Supplier Management  dashboard, locate the supplier with the internal registration for whom you want
to reinvite internal recipients.
 Tip
In supplier search, you can filter  by the registration status Invited  to show suppliers for whom a registration
has been started where no recipients have submitted external questionnaires. However, there's no way to
distinguish suppliers who have a status of Invited  through an internal registration in supplier searches.
2. Choose the supplier's name to open their 360° profile.
3. Perform one of the following actions:
•In the supplier's profile  summary, choose the registration status link in the Supplier Status  panel.
•In the classic supplier 360° profile,  choose Registration  in the left-hand navigation bar.
The Registration  detail area of the supplier's profile  opens.
4. In the Questionnaires  table, for any external registration questionnaire, choose   (more options) > Reinvite
( ).
5. (Optional) T o change the recipients for a questionnaire, perform the following steps:
a. Choose Choose recipient  (  ).
b. Search for and select the user in your organization who you want to make the recipient of the
questionnaire.
c. Choose Confirm .
6. Choose Send .
Results
The selected recipients receive new invitations for each external questionnaire in the registration. They can use the
links in the invitation emails to sign into your site and fill out and submit their assigned questionnaires.

After a new internal registration is approved, you can also reassign the internal recipients of its external

Filling Out an Internal Registration Questionnaire
Use these steps if your organization's registration projects include one or more internal questionnaires and you're a
questionnaire editor. Filling out internal questionnaires can be a required or optional step in supplier registration.
Prerequisites
T o edit an internal registration questionnaire, you must be a member of the Supplier Registration Manager ,
Supplier Internal Questionnaire Editor , SM Ops Administrator , or SM ERP Administrator  group.
If the questionnaire is associated with a T o Do task, you must also be a task owner to edit the questionnaire while
completing the T o Do task.
Context
Internal supplier registration questionnaires typically collect detailed profile  information about the supplier that
only people in your organization can provide, such as internal supplier categorizations.
Internal questionnaires can be associated with T o Do tasks. If there's no T o Do task associated with an internal
questionnaire, or if it's associated with an optional T o Do task, filling  out the questionnaire is optional. If there's a
required T o Do task associated with an internal questionnaire and you're a task owner, you must submit the internal
questionnaire to complete the task.
Internal registration questionnaires are always editable unless they're in approval. Y ou can update them at any time,
even after registering the suppliers.
If the owner of a T o Do task on an internal questionnaire is a project group, all members of that project group who
have the appropriate permissions can edit the internal questionnaire. The first  member to submit the questionnaire
becomes the owner of the completed task. In registration projects, if the T o Do task has restarted because
of a registration update, task ownership reverts to the entire project group so that all members who have the
appropriate permission can again edit the internal questionnaire.
 Note
Attachments in supplier

ns can edit the internal questionnaire. The first  member to submit the questionnaire
becomes the owner of the completed task. In registration projects, if the T o Do task has restarted because
of a registration update, task ownership reverts to the entire project group so that all members who have the
appropriate permission can again edit the internal questionnaire.
 Note
Attachments in supplier management questionnaires can't include embedded documents.

Procedure
1. Perform one of the following actions:

navigate to the Registration  detail area.
•If the questionnaire is associated with an active T o Do task and you're a task owner, choose the link in the

2. Perform one of the following actions:
•Locate the questionnaire in the Questionnaires  table and choose View  ( ) to open the questionnaire

the Tasks  table and choose   (take action) to open the task details page, where the questionnaire is
automatically editable.
3. Fill out the questionnaire as needed.
4.Optional: T o save your draft edits of the questionnaire, choose Save .
Y ou can continue editing the questionnaire later by opening it and choosing Edit  again.
5. Perform one of the following actions:
•On the questionnaire details page, click Submit .
•On the task details page, click Mark Complete .
If the questionnaire is associated with a T o Do task, either of these actions completes the task.
Results
If the questionnaire has an approval task, that task starts and approvers approve or deny your answers. For
internal registration questionnaires, depending on your site's configuration,  your answers can be added to mapped
database fields  and synchronized to an integrated ERP system immediately after you submit the questionnaire or
only after approval.
If you're the first  user to submit the questionnaire, your name is added to the Owner  field  and is always displayed
even if other users edit the questionnaire subsequently. If you have access to the Advanced View , you can choose
Advanced View  (  ) in the top right corner of the questionnaire and view the questionnaire's activity history in the

Filling Out an External Registration Questionnaire on Behalf
of a Supplier in an Internal Registration
Use these steps if a registration manager has made you the recipient of one or more external registration
questionnaires in an internal supplier registration so that you can fill them out on behalf of the supplier. Suppliers
don't participate in internal registrations.
Prerequisites
T o edit and submit an external registration questionnaire on behalf of a supplier in an internal registration, you must
be the registration project owner or the questionnaire recipient.
If you're not a member of the Sensitive Data Access  group, to see and edit otherwise masked answers, the
self-service configuration  parameter Enable questionnaire recipients in internal registrations to see and edit
otherwise masked answers  must be enabled in your site.
Context
Attachments in supplier management questionnaires can't include embedded documents.
Procedure
1. Perform one of the following actions:
•Choose the link in the notification  email and sign into your site.
•Navigate to the Registration  detail area of the supplier's profile,  and choose   (more options) > View
  for the questionnaire.
2. On the questionnaire details page, choose Edit .
 Tip
In some cases, external registration questionnaires can include questions that already have existing
answers. These answers can come from an approved supplier request, a data import, or an integrated
ERP system. When you first  open the questionnaire, it takes a few moments for the system to load these
130 PUBLICManaging Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Supplier Creation and Onboarding

answers, and during that time the Edit  button is temporarily disabled. If this situation occurs, refresh the
page or navigate away and back again to see the updated questionnaire and active Edit  button.
3. Fill out the questionnaire as needed. If the questionnaire includes existing answers, you can edit them or leave
them as is.
4. Choose Submit .
Results
If the questionnaire has an approval task, that task starts and approvers approve or deny your answers. Y our
answers aren't added to mapped database fields  or synchronized to an integrated ERP system until the registration
is finally  approved.
Next Steps
If the internal supplier registration is approved and the supplier has Registered  status, you can update the
questionnaires to which you've been assigned as needed.
If a registration manager invites the supplier to participate in the registration, access to the external questionnaires
to which you've been assigned transfers to the invited supplier contact and you can no longer edit them after that
point.

Approving or Denying a Supplier Registration Questionnaire
Use these steps to approve or deny a supplier registration questionnaire if you're in its approval flow.  Questionnaire
approval is part of the overall approval process for both new supplier registrations and registration updates.
Context
Supplier registration questionnaires can be internal, meaning that they're designed to collect information from
people in your organization, or external, meaning that they're designed to collect information from suppliers. The
Managing Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Supplier Creation and Onboarding PUBLIC 131

type and number of questionnaires included in a supplier registration depend on the setup of your organization's
registration projects. In external registrations, the supplier fills out and submits external questionnaires. In internal
registrations, people in your organization fill out and submit external questionnaires on behalf of the supplier,
without the supplier's direct participation.
For a new registration, external questionnaire recipients fill out and submit registration questionnaires once.
Approvals for external registration questionnaires directly affect  supplier registration status. When a recipient
submits the first  external questionnaire for a new registration, the supplier moves to Pending Approval  registration
status. They can't move to Registered  status until all external registration questionnaires are finally  approved and
any other tasks in the new registration workflow  are completed. If one approver denies one external registration
questionnaire, the supplier's registration status moves to Registration Denied .
The role of internal questionnaires in the registration workflow  varies depending on their setup. Internal registration
questionnaires are typically editable anytime they aren’t in approval. Depending on the setup, approval of an
internal questionnaire can be a required or optional part of a new registration or registration update workflow.
If the internal questionnaire is set up as a profile  questionnaire, its approval or denial has no impact on the
supplier's registration or registration update status. Approval of nonprofile  internal questionnaires directly affects
the supplier's registration or registration update status.
Internal and external questionnaires can have different  approval flows  depending on whether they're in a new
registration or are being updated after the new registration is completed. After a recipient has submitted an
update to a registration questionnaire, the supplier remains in Registered  status. If the updated questionnaire
is either an external questionnaire or an internal questionnaire that affects  registration status, and the update
requires an approval, the supplier has a separate Pending Approval  registration update status. When there are no
remaining tasks in progress for the questionnaire that is in approval, the update status is Approved . Submitting an
update only starts the tasks

the updated questionnaire

requires an approval, the supplier has a separate Pending Approval  registration update status. When there are no
remaining tasks in progress for the questionnaire that is in approval, the update status is Approved . Submitting an
update only starts the tasks that are associated with the updated questionnaire, and the process flow  graph in the
Registration  detail area of the supplier's profile  now shows the tasks associated with the current update:
For either internal or external questionnaire updates, or if you've requested more information from the recipient
during an approval, the approval task details page shows the answers in both the latest, unapproved update and the
previous versions. Y ou can view and compare different  questionnaire versions.
Procedure
1. Perform one of the following actions:
•Navigate to the Registration  detail area of the supplier's profile.  Choose   (take action) in the Tasks  table.
•Choose the link in the approval notification  email.
The approval task details page opens.
2. Review the answers to the questionnaire.
3.Optional: Perform any of the following actions if you're reviewing a questionnaire update:
•T o display only the questions with updated answers, choose Updated .

•T o display all questions regardless of whether or not they've updated answers, choose All.
•T o compare the current answers with versions of the questionnaire that the respondent submitted before
the previous version, choose the version number in the Previous Version  column and select a version. The
Latest Version  column always shows answers from the current update.
4. Perform one of the following actions:
•T o approve the questionnaire, choose Approve , enter an optional comment, and choose Approve .
•T o deny the questionnaire, choose Deny , enter an explanatory comment, and choose Deny . This option is
only available for new registrations. It's not available for updates.
•T o request additional information from the respondent, choose Request Additional Info , enter a comment
explaining what information you want, and choose Request Additional Info .
Results
For new registrations:
If you've approved the questionnaire and you're the final  approver in the registration, the supplier is now in
Registered  status. After approval, answers in both internal and external registration questionnaires are added
to mapped database fields  and initially synchronized to an integrated system using either manual or automatic
synchronization, depending on your site's integration setup. External questionnaires and internal questionnaires
that affect  registration status automatically reopen for further updates.
If you approved or denied an internal profile  questionnaire that doesn't affect  registration status, the registration
status remains unchanged and the questionnaire automatically reopens for further updates.
If you denied the questionnaire and it is either an external questionnaire or an internal questionnaire that
affects  registration status, the registration is now in Registration Denied  status. Answers in denied external
questionnaires and denied internal questionnaires that are a required part of registration are saved and show on
questionnaire detail pages, but aren't added to mapped database fields.  Suppliers with denied registrations aren't
synchronized to an integrated system. Both external and internal questionnaires automatically close to further
updates.
For registration updates:
If you're the final  approver for an external or internal questionnaire update and you approved it, the approved
update shows as the latest version and the questionnaire

aren't added to mapped database fields.  Suppliers with denied registrations aren't
synchronized to an integrated system. Both external and internal questionnaires automatically close to further
updates.
For registration updates:
If you're the final  approver for an external or internal questionnaire update and you approved it, the approved
update shows as the latest version and the questionnaire reopens for further updates.
Depending on your site's configuration,  updates to approved internal registration questionnaires can be added
to mapped database fields  and synchronized to an integrated system immediately after the questionnaire is
submitted or only after approval.
Approved updates to an external registration questionnaire are always added to mapped database fields  and
synchronized to an integrated system after approval. If your registration project includes tasks in one or more
update phases, updates to an external registration questionnaire are added to mapped database fields  and
synchronized to an integrated ERP system after all tasks associated with the questionnaire are completed,
regardless of the status of the overall update phase or of other questionnaire tasks in it.
If you requested additional information rather than approving or denying the questionnaire, the questionnaire
reopens so that the recipient can provide the requested information. By default, the current approval flow  for
the questionnaire resets and the process flow  graph for the registration reverts to the previously completed new
registration workflow.  After the recipient resubmits the questionnaire, the approval task becomes active again,

the approval flow  restarts from the beginning, and the process flow  graph now shows the questionnaire update
workflow  again. If the enhancements to the workflow  for requesting additional information feature (SM-3492) is
enabled in your site, requesting additional information restarts the current node of the approval flow  rather than
resetting the entire approval flow.
The supplier remains in Registered  status. After all tasks associated with the current update are completed, the
supplier also has a registration update status of Approved  or Denied .
For both new registrations and registration updates:
Any comments that you or other approvers added during questionnaire approval show on both the questionnaire
and approval task details pages, which you can see by viewing the questionnaire or task. Questionnaire details
pages show all comments for all tasks in the project, and you can filter  them to just show the comments for the
current questionnaire. T ask details pages show only the comments associated with the current task. Depending on
how your site's notifications  are set up, approval comments can also be included in approval or denial notifications
to suppliers.
If your organization uses different  phases to manage tasks for new questionnaires and questionnaire updates, the
questionnaire details page only shows comments for tasks in the current phase. When the questionnaire is new,
comments for tasks in the new questionnaire phase show. After a recipient submits the first  questionnaire update,
only comments for tasks in the update phase show, and the comment history shows comments for all updates. If
the advanced view for the project is enabled and you have permission to view it, you can see comments for tasks in
the new phase by viewing task details on the Tasks  tab of the advanced project view.
If you requested additional information during approval of an external questionnaire and entered a comment, the
supplier sees the most recent approval comment when they revise their response to the questionnaire.
Next Steps
If you or another approver denied a questionnaire in a new registration, you can start a new round of the approval
task to reapprove it. A member of the Supplier Registration Manager  or SM Ops Administrator  groups can also
restart the entire registration project.
