Starting a New Approval Round for a Denied Supplier
Registration Questionnaire
Use these steps to start a new round of the approval task if an approver has denied a supplier registration
questionnaire in error. Starting a new round of the approval task removes the denial and restarts the approval task
so that approvers can reapprove the questionnaire.
Prerequisites
T o start a new approval round for a denied supplier registration questionnaire, you must be a member of the
Supplier Registration Manager  or SM Ops Administrator  group.
Y ou can only start new rounds for denied approval tasks on external registration questionnaires and internal
registration questionnaires that affect  the supplier's registration status. If an internal questionnaire is a profile
questionnaire, its approval or denial doesn't affect  supplier registration status. Instead of starting a new approval
round for a profile  questionnaire, you can edit it to start a new approval for it.
Context
The option to start a new approval round is only available in new registrations. Once an approver denies an
external questionnaire, or an internal questionnaire that's not a profile  questionnaire, in a new registration,
the entire registration is denied. Starting a new round of a denied approval task allows the approvers for that
task to re-evaluate the currently submitted questionnaire answers and reapprove them so that the supplier can
complete registration. For registration updates, respondents can always submit updates to denied questionnaires
for reapproval.
Starting a new approval round is useful when not much time has elapsed since the questionnaire denial or
when you're otherwise confident  that the currently submitted answers are the appropriate basis for new approval
decisions. Starting a new approval round doesn't require any further action by the questionnaire recipients and
only restarts the approval tasks on the denied questionnaire. If time has elapsed since the original denial or you
otherwise want the recipient to submit updates before approving the registration, you can restart the registration
instead.
Procedure
1. In the Registration  detail area of the supplier's profile,  locate the denied approval task and choose View  ( ).
2. In the upper right corner of the approval task details page, choose New Round .
3. Choose Confirm .
Managing Suppliers and Supplier

otherwise want the recipient to submit updates before approving the registration, you can restart the registration
instead.

2. In the upper right corner of the approval task details page, choose New Round .
3. Choose Confirm .

in the approval flow  for the task receive email notifications  letting them know that they need to reapprove the
questionnaire.
The approval task details page shows comments for all rounds of the approval task.
If an approver denies the questionnaire again, the registration moves back to Denied  registration status and for
external registrations, the supplier receives a second email notification  for the registration denial.
If approvers approve the questionnaire, any subsequent tasks in the registration workflow  start.

Restarting a Denied Supplier Registration
Use these steps to reinvite questionnaire recipients to a denied supplier registration and restarts the entire
registration workflow  so that they can update questionnaires and approvers can reapprove the registration based
on those updates.
Prerequisites
T o restart a denied registration, you must be the project owner or a member of the Supplier Registration Manager
or SM Ops Administrator  group.
Context
Once an approver denies an external questionnaire, or internal registration questionnaire that's not a profile
questionnaire, in a new registration, the entire registration is denied. Reinviting a supplier (for external
registrations) or internal recipients (for internal registrations) to a denied registration allows you to restart the
registration workflow  so that the registration can be completed.
Restarting denied registration is useful when time has elapsed since the original denial or when you otherwise want
to completely restart the registration process from the beginning. When you restart the registration, recipients
136 PUBLICManaging Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Supplier Creation and Onboarding

must resubmit all external questionnaires and task owners must recomplete all tasks related to those external
questionnaires in the registration workflow.
Y ou can't change which external questionnaires are included when restarting a denied registration. The registration
always includes the external questionnaires selected during the original registration invitation.
 Note
Currently, once a supplier contact opens an external questionnaire, no other supplier contact can access it.
Although it's technically possible to change the contact when reinviting a supplier to a denied registration,
always make sure that you reinvite the contact who originally completed the denied registration so that they
can update the questionnaires.
If you want to complete the registration by reapproving existing questionnaire answers rather than requiring
recipients to resubmit all external questionnaires and task owners to recomplete all required tasks, you can start a
new round of approvals for the denied questionnaire instead.
Restarting a denied registration doesn't affect  approvals for internal questionnaires.
Procedure
1. Locate the supplier that you want to reinvite on the Supplier Management  dashboard.
 Tip
In supplier search, you can filter  by the registration status Denied  to show suppliers with denied
registrations.
2. Choose the supplier's name to open their profile.
3. Perform one of the following actions:
•In the supplier's profile  summary, choose the registration status link in the Supplier Status  panel.
•In the classic supplier 360° profile,  choose Registration  in the left-hand navigation bar.
The Registration  detail area of the supplier's profile  opens.
4. For any external registration questionnaire, choose   (more options) > Reinvite   .
5. Choose Send .
Results
The supplier moves from Denied  registration status back to Invited  registration status. Recipients receive a new
invitation for each external questionnaire in the registration. Recipients can use the links in the emails to update the
questionnaires. Recipients must resubmit every external questionnaire in the registration, even if they don't change
their answers, to move the registration back to Pending Approval  status.
After recipients have resubmitted all external questionnaires, task owners recomplete any previously completed
tasks and approvers reapprove previously approved or denied

questionnaires. Recipients must resubmit every external questionnaire in the registration, even if they don't change
their answers, to move the registration back to Pending Approval  status.
After recipients have resubmitted all external questionnaires, task owners recomplete any previously completed
tasks and approvers reapprove previously approved or denied questionnaires. If an approver denies a registration
questionnaire in an external registration, the supplier receives a second email notification  of the denial. If approvers

Inviting a Supplier to Participate in an Internal Registration
Use these steps to invite a supplier to participate in an internal registration for the first  time by signing into SAP
Business Network and updating external questionnaires, making the registration external. In internal registrations,
people in your organization complete a registration on behalf of the supplier.
Prerequisites
T o invite a supplier to participate in an internal registration, you must be a member of the Supplier Registration
Manager  or SM Ops Administrator  group.
The supplier must have one of the following registration statuses:
•Registered , through either an internal supplier registration data import or an approved internal registration
project.
•Registration Denied , through a denied internal registration project.
Y ou can't invite a supplier to participate in an internal registration if they have an internal registration project that is
in progress or a questionnaire update submitted by an internal recipient is in progress.
The supplier must have at least one contact. T o add or edit a supplier contact on the send questionnaires page, you
must be a member of the Supplier Registration Manager  or SM Ops Administrator  group.
T o specify a requester for the registration during invitation, the requester selection feature (SM-20904) must be
enabled.
138 PUBLICManaging Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Supplier Creation and Onboarding

Context
Inviting a supplier to an internal registration transfers access to external questionnaires from assigned internal
recipients to the invited supplier contact. The supplier must now create an account or sign into an existing account
on SAP Business Network to fill out the external questionnaires and submit them. When you first  invite a supplier
to an internal registration, you send them the external questionnaires for the first  time, and those questionnaires
close after the amount of time defined  for new questionnaires in your site's questionnaire setup.
If the supplier was originally registered through an approved internal registration project, you can't change which
external questionnaires are included in the registration when you invite the supplier to it. In this case, registration
always includes the external questionnaires selected for the original internal registration. If the supplier was
internally registered through a data import, you select the external questionnaires to include in the registration
when inviting them to participate in it.
If the requester selection during registration invitation feature is enabled in your site, you can also choose the
requester for invitations to fill out registration questionnaires. Choosing the requester allows you to identify
someone in your organization with whom the supplier is familiar as the sender of those invitations. The name
of the requester you choose shows in the "From" field  in registration invitations to the supplier. Depending on how
your organization has set up registration invitations, the requester's name can also show in other places in those
emails as well. For suppliers created from approved supplier requests, the default registration requester is the
owner of the supplier request project, which can be a person with whom the supplier already has a relationship. If
you don't choose a registration requester, registration invitations use whatever default sender is set up for them,
which can be a generic system alias, a custom alias, or you as the registration project owner.

1. On the Supplier Management  dashboard, locate the internally registered supplier you want to invite.
2. Choose the supplier's name to open their profile.
3. Perform one of the following actions:
•In the supplier's profile  summary, choose the registration status link in the Supplier Status  panel.
•In

be a generic system alias, a custom alias, or you as the registration project owner.

asking them to create an account or sign into an existing account on SAP Business Network and fill out and submit

If the status tracker for supplier management email notifications  feature is enabled in your site, the Registration
area also shows Email Status  and Email Status Date . The Email Status  lets you know if the registration invitation
email has been Delivered , Opened , or Bounced .
If you selected a registration requester, the invitation email notifications  to the supplier show the requester's name
in the "From" field.  Otherwise, the name in that field  is determined by how your organization has set up each
registration questionnaire.
If the supplier had a registration status of Registered  from an internal registration data import, inviting them to
participate in the registration creates a registration project for the first  time and resets their registration status to

Invited . Their registration proceeds through the workflow  your organization has defined  for new registrations. They
can only achieve Registered  status again through the final  approval of the external registration.
If the supplier had a registration status of Registration Denied  from an internal registration project, inviting them
to participate in the registration restarts it. They now have a registration status of Invited  and the workflow  that
your organization has defined  for a new registration restarts. In this case, the supplier doesn't see any previous
answers from internal recipients in external questionnaires, since the internal registration was denied.
If the supplier had a registration status of Registered  from an internal registration project, they remain in
Registered  status after you've invited them to participate in the registration, and the system treats all subsequent
activity in the registration as updates. In this case, the supplier contact sees the last approved internal answers
in external questionnaires and can update them as needed. The questionnaire version history retains all approved
versions previously submitted by internal recipients as well as approved updates from the supplier going forward.
Next Steps

questionnaires close after an amount of time defined  by the circumstances of the previous internal registration and
your site's questionnaire setup.

Reassigning External Questionnaires in a Completed
Registration to a Different  Recipient
Use these steps to reassign an external registration questionnaire to a different  recipient if the current recipient
isn't the most appropriate person to update it. In external registrations, only the supplier contact who is currently
assigned to external registration questionnaires can open and update them.
Prerequisites
T o reassign a different  recipient to external questionnaires in a completed registration:
•Y ou must be a member of the Supplier Registration Manager  or SM Ops Administrator  group.
•The registration must be in Registered  status.
•No external registration questionnaire updates must be pending approval (registration update status must be
Approved  or Denied ).
T o add or edit a supplier contact on the send questionnaires page, you must be a member of the Supplier
Registration Manager  or SM Ops Administrator  group.
Context
In new, external registrations, once the invited supplier contact opens at least one external questionnaire, no other
contact for the supplier can access the external questionnaires until the registration is complete. In new, internal
registrations, once an invited recipient opens at least one external questionnaire, only the assigned recipients and
the registration project owner can access the external questionnaires until the registration is complete.
After the registration is complete, however, you can reassign the external questionnaires in the registration to
different  recipients for updates. Reassigning external questionnaires for updates allows you to make sure that
the most appropriate recipient has a current invitation to update the questionnaires. Only the currently assigned
recipient or, in internal registrations, the project owner can update the questionnaires.
In external registrations, you can't reassign individual external questionnaires to different  supplier contacts.
Reassignments apply to all external registration questionnaires in the registration project for the supplier. In
internal registrations, you can reassign individual external questionnaires to different  recipients.
Y ou can't change which external questionnaires are included in the registration when you reassign a recipient. The
reassignment always includes the external questionnaires selected during the original registration

ternal registration questionnaires in the registration project for the supplier. In
internal registrations, you can reassign individual external questionnaires to different  recipients.

reassignment always includes the external questionnaires selected during the original registration invitation.

1. Locate the supplier for whom you want to reassign questionnaires on the Supplier Management  dashboard.
2. Choose the supplier's name to open their profile.

Results
The new recipients receive an email notification  for each external questionnaire in the registration, letting them
know of the reassignment.
Both the Supplier recipient  field  in the Registration  area of the supplier's 360 profile  and the Recipient  field  in the
individual external registration questionnaire details pages now to show the newly reassigned recipient.
As long as the newly assigned recipient hasn't submitted an update to any of the external questionnaires after

been applied to the registration, including the version number:
When you select a previous template version, the Registration  area updates to show you the previous, archived

you selected and who completed the completed tasks as of that template upgrade.
•Different  process flow  diagrams : The process flow  diagram shows the process flow  for the registration based
on the template version that you select, including tasks and task owners or approvers in that version.
•Registration status : An administrator in your organization can upgrade a registration to the latest template

Sourcing and Fulfillment  (Trading) Relationships with
Suppliers
A sourcing relationship with a supplier on SAP Business Network allows them to fill out SAP Ariba Supplier
Lifecycle and Performance questionnaires and participate in sourcing events. A fulfillment  or trading relationship
allows you to send them purchase orders and begin transacting with them. Y ou can establish both relationships at
the same time.
In your SAP Business Network buyer account, a supplier can have two different  SAP Business Network IDs (ANIDs):
a sourcing ANID that's assigned when you first  establish a sourcing relationship with the supplier and a transaction
ANID that's

ent  or trading relationship
allows you to send them purchase orders and begin transacting with them. Y ou can establish both relationships at
the same time.
In your SAP Business Network buyer account, a supplier can have two different  SAP Business Network IDs (ANIDs):
a sourcing ANID that's assigned when you first  establish a sourcing relationship with the supplier and a transaction
ANID that's assigned when you first  establish a fulfillment  relationship with them.
By default, you establish these relationships separately. Y ou establish the sourcing relationship when the supplier
responds to your first  invitation to participate in a sourcing event or fill out a supplier management questionnaire.
Managing Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Supplier Creation and Onboarding PUBLIC 149

Y ou establish the fulfillment  relationship when the supplier accepts a request to establish either a full or a
quick-enabled trading relationship that you've sent them in your SAP Business Network buyer account as part
of supplier onboarding for procurement. The supplier's acceptance can be automatic or manual, depending on
their settings. In cases where you don't have an existing sourcing or fulfillment  relationship with the supplier yet,
both types of invitation prompt the supplier to create a new SAP Business Network account. In some cases, an
invited supplier contact who isn't aware that the supplier has an existing SAP Business Network account can create
a new, duplicate account for the supplier instead of signing into the existing account. In this case, in your SAP
Business Network buyer account, the supplier's vendor ID is associated with different  transaction and sourcing
ANIDs pointing to the separate, duplicate supplier accounts. The only remedy for this situation is unlinking the
supplier's sourcing ANID, which results in the supplier losing of access to any content they responded to using the
account with the unlinked ANID.
An optional feature, SM-5144, allows you to flag suppliers for which you want to establish simultaneous sourcing
and fulfillment  relationships. Once you establish one type of relationship with the supplier, the other relationship is
established automatically and uses the same supplier ANID.
There are several options for flagging  suppliers to establish simultaneous sourcing and fulfillment  relationships:
•Flagging all suppliers : when you ask SAP Ariba Support to enable SM-5144, you have the additional option of
universally flagging  every supplier in your SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and Performance site.
•Flagging specific  suppliers : If you don't use the option for flagging  all suppliers universally, you can flag
specific  suppliers using either of the following methods:
•Setting the flag in registration mass invitations using the optional Fulfillment  column in the CSV data
files  that define  mass registration waves.
•Setting the flag manually for individual suppliers in their profiles.
When a supplier is flagged  and has an ERP vendor ID that doesn't start with VDR  in the SM source system,
establishing a sourcing relationship with them also automatically establishes the transaction relationship and vice
versa.

using the optional Fulfillment  column in the CSV data
files  that define  mass registration waves.
•Setting the flag manually for individual suppliers in their profiles.
When a supplier is flagged  and has an ERP vendor ID that doesn't start with VDR  in the SM source system,
establishing a sourcing relationship with them also automatically establishes the transaction relationship and vice
versa. For suppliers with ERP vendor IDs that start with VDR  in the SM source system, the flag is ignored. SAP Ariba
automatically assigns an ERP vendor ID that starts with VDR  and a source system of supplier account, depending
on the supplier's transaction volumes, whether their transactions involve SM to every supplier created from an
approved supplier request, but these IDs are often placeholders and are replaced by a "real" ERP vendor ID from an
integrated ERP system after a new supplier is synchronized to it, with an updated source system such as SAP . In
this case, once the ERP vendor ID is updated or the source system is updated to a non- SM value, or both, the flag
becomes active and the second relationship is established. A registration manager can also manually set the flag
after the ERP vendor ID or source system, or both, are updated.
 Note
There are several different  levels of SAP Business NetworkSAP Business Network for Supply Chain supplier
account, depending on the supplier's transaction volumes, whether their, and other factors. Some suppliers
deliberately maintain different  SAP Business Network accounts at different  levels for different  buyers. If the
supplier has a "real" ERP vendor ID (either a non- VDR  ID or a VDR  ID in a non-SM source system), automatic
flagging  can establish simultaneous fulfillment  and sourcing relationships using an ANID for an SAP Business
Network account that the supplier doesn't want to use for transactions with you. Setting flags  manually for
individual suppliers gives them more control over the account through which you establish these relationships
with them.

In this process:

3. A registration manager invites the supplier to register.

5. Questionnaire submission establishes the sourcing relationship and assigns the supplier account's ANID as the
supplier's sourcing ANID.

supplier's profile.

8. The integrated ERP system creates the new vendor record, generates an ID for it, and sends the ERP vendor ID

such as SAP .
9. A registration manager enables the fulfillment  relationship in the supplier's profile.
10. SAP Ariba establishes the fulfillment  relationship and assigns the same ANID to the supplier as the transaction
ANID.
11. The supplier shows in the vendor list in the buyer's SAP Business Network account and is enabled for
transactions.
12. The buyer sends the supplier a purchase order through SAP Business Network using the transaction ANID.

In this process:
1. The supplier is created through either replication from an integrated ERP system, migration from a supplier
organization, or data import in SM Administration . With all of these methods, the supplier is created with
"real" ERP vendor ID, which can be a non- VDR  ERP vendor ID or a VDR  ERP vendor ID in a non- SM source system
such as SAP .

2. A registration manager invites the supplier to register. The supplier is automatically flagged  for simultaneous
establishment of sourcing and fulfillment  relationships either universally at creation, in a mass registration
invitation wave, or manually before or just after being invited to register.
3. The supplier either creates a new SAP Business Network account or, if they have an existing account, logs into
it, where they fill out and submit the registration questionnaires.
4. Questionnaire submission establishes the sourcing and fulfillment  relationships at the same time and assigns
the supplier account's ANID as both sourcing ANID and transaction ANID for the supplier.
5. The supplier shows in the vendor list in the buyer's SAP Business Network account and is enabled for
transactions.

If your first  interaction with the supplier involves onboarding in SAP Business Network rather than a sourcing
or supplier management activity, once the supplier has accepted the fulfillment  (trading) relationship and been
flagged  for simultaneous fulfillment  and sourcing relationships, the supplier account's ANID is assigned as both
their sourcing and transaction ANID. If you've enabled the supplier through a trading relationship request (full
enablement) or a quick enablement invitation (quick or light enablement), that enablement assigns the supplier's
transaction ANID to their vendor key automatically. If you've requested the relationship after a search, the
supplier's vendor key must be added manually.

Manually Synchronizing a Supplier to Y our ERP
System
Use these steps to manually synchronize or resynchronize a supplier to an integrated SAP ERP system.
Synchronizing a new supplier adds a them to the ERP vendor master. Manually resynchronizing a supplier
reestablishes automatic synchronization after an error has occurred.
Prerequisites
T o manually synchronize a new supplier with the ERP system, you must be a member of the SM Manual ERP Sync ,
SM Ops Administrator , or SM ERP Administrator  group.
T o manually resynchronize a supplier with the ERP system, you must be a member of the SM ERP Administrator
group.
The supplier must meet either of the following conditions:
Managing Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Supplier Creation and Onboarding PUBLIC 155

•For synchronization, the supplier is new, your site is configured  to require initial manual synchronization
for new suppliers, and the supplier has the minimum registration or qualification  status where initial
synchronization is allowed.
•For resynchronization, there has been an error in the automatic synchronization of the supplier.
Context
Y our organization's ERP vendor master is the official  record of your company's suppliers.
Initial ERP synchronization adds suppliers that have been created in your site to the ERP vendor master. Y our
organization's ERP integration setup determines whether you always manually synchronize new suppliers to
the ERP system, or whether that initial synchronization is automatic once the new supplier achieves a specific
registration or qualification  status.
If your organization uses manual initial synchronization, you must manually synchronize the supplier to the ERP
system to establish the initial connection, after which updates are automatically synchronized.
If a synchronization error occurs, you must manually resynchronize the supplier with the ERP system to re-
establish the connection and resume automatic synchronization of updates.
 Note
If current supplier data includes validation errors, the supplier can't successfully synchronize to the ERP
system until those errors are corrected. Users with the appropriate permissions can correct any validation
errors introduced by answers to internal registration questionnaires. Members of the SM Ops Administrator
group can view error details and manage validation errors in SM Administration .
Procedure
1. Perform one of the following actions:
•In the supplier's profile  summary, choose ERP Data  in the Supplier Details  panel.
•In the classic supplier 360° profile,  choose ERP data  in the left-hand navigation bar.
The ERP data  detail area of the supplier's profile  opens.
2. Perform one of the following actions:
•T o synchronize a new supplier to the ERP system, choose   Sync Now .
•T o resynchronize a supplier to the ERP system after a synchronization error, choose   Resync Now .
Results
A message lets you know that the synchronization has started. Once it's finished,  any updates to the supplier in
either system are automatically synchronized between the two. Both the ERP data  detail area and these areas of
the supplier profile  show the current integration status

Now .
•T o resynchronize a supplier to the ERP system after a synchronization error, choose   Resync Now .

Supplier Qualifications  and Lifecycle Processes
Qualifications  vet supplier's suitability to provide specific  commodities for specific  regions and (if your organization
uses them) departments. They assign qualification  statuses for those combinations. Depending on your site's
setup, your organization can use similar workflows  for other processes.
If your organization qualifies  suppliers, you use one of two available qualification  methods:
•Supplier qualification  and disqualification  projects. In this case, there are no process status filters  in supplier
search, and you view and manage qualifications  only in the Qualifications  area of suppliers' 360° profiles.
•Process qualification  and discontinuation (disqualification)  projects. If your organization uses process
qualifications,  it can also use process projects to manage custom or miscellaneous lifecycle processes. In
this case, there are process status filters  in supplier search, and you can view or manage qualifications  in either
the Qualifications  or Processes  area of suppliers' 360° profiles.

About the Qualifications  Detail Area in the Supplier Profile
Y ou can view and manage supplier qualifications  in the Qualifications  detail area of the supplier profile.
Supplier qualifications  are only available in solutions that include SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and Performance.
By default, the Qualifications  detail area is only available after the supplier is registered. If your organization uses
process projects to manage supplier qualifications:
•Qualification  processes for unregistered suppliers are also available in the Processes  area.
•The Qualifications  area is available for suppliers who aren't registered

Y ou can do the following things in the Qualifications  detail area:
•Start a qualification  [page 173] , disqualification  [page 179] , or requalification  [page 182]  using qualification
and disqualification  projects.
•View a summary list of all the supplier's qualifications  and disqualifications.  Disqualifications  are nested under
their associated qualification.  The summary list includes end dates for the current statuses, such as the date a
qualification  expires or the date when a disqualification  becomes eligible for requalification.
•View qualification  or disqualification  details by choosing   (view). On the qualification  or disqualification
details page, you can:
•See expiration dates for qualifications  if your organization uses them.
•See all tasks associated with a qualification,  disqualification,  or requalification  in the Tasks  table.
•See details for external or internal qualification  questionnaires in the Questionnaires  table, including
questions and answers.
•Fill out and submit an internal questionnaire [page 177]  in a qualification  project.
•Complete any stand-alone T o Do tasks [page 259]  to which you've been assigned.
•Approve or deny [page 184]  a qualification,  disqualification,  or requalification  if you're in the approval flow.
•Reinviting [page 176]  the supplier to fill out the qualification  questionnaire if you've started a qualification  or
requalification  and they haven't responded yet.

The Qualifications  detail area only shows summary information for supplier qualifications  and disqualifications
that are based on a data import rather than a project, since project details such as questionnaires and tasks aren't
available.
A customer administrator in your organization can cancel an imported qualification  or disqualification  or an
in-progress qualification  project. Canceled qualifications  and disqualifications  no longer show in the Qualifications
detail area. If the supplier has an imported qualification  for which one or more disqualification  projects have been
created and a customer administrator cancels the imported qualification,  the canceled qualification  no longer
shows in the Qualifications  detail area, but the disqualifications  continue to show.
About the Qualifications  Area in Sites That Manage Qualifications  Using Process Projects
The following image shows an example of a Qualifications  area with process qualifications  for a supplier:
If your site previously used supplier qualification  and disqualification  projects and has transitioned to using
process qualification  and discontinuation projects, the Qualifications  detail area includes tabs for both types
of qualifications.  Any supplier qualification  and disqualification  projects show on the Legacy qualifications  tab.
Process qualifications  and disqualifications  show on the Process qualifications  tab. If your organization has only
managed supplier qualifications  in SAP Ariba using process projects, the Qualifications  detail area doesn't have
any tabs.

Y ou can do the following things in the Qualifications  detail area:
•Start [page 197]  or renew [page 216]  a qualification  process.
•View a summary list of all the supplier's qualification  processes.
If the supplier has more than one qualification  process of a specific  type, the summary list for that type shows
the processes in alphabetical order by category name. The summary list includes an effective  date for the
current qualification  status. Y ou can also search in this list or filter  it by process status, owner, decision-maker,
expiration date, and other characteristics.
•Disqualify [page 205]  a qualification  process that is in the approved or partially approved status.
•View qualification  process details. On the process details page you can:
•See all modular questionnaires included in the process qualification  and, if you're a recipient for an internal
questionnaire, fill it out and submit it [page 207] .
•Approve or deny [page 209]  modular questionnaires included in the qualification  process.
•Request an update or change the recipient [page 240]  of a modular questionnaire included in the
qualification  process.
•Set or change the status [page 220]  of a qualification  process, if you're a decision-maker for it.
•Cancel [page 212]  a qualification  process.
If your site previously used qualification  and disqualification  projects, you can also do the following things on the
Legacy qualifications  tab:
•View a summary list and details of the supplier's legacy qualifications  and disqualifications.
•Disqualify [page 179]  a legacy qualification,  which starts a legacy disqualification  project.
•Requalify a legacy qualification,  which starts a new qualification  process project [page 197] .
Enabling the process project feature disabled creation of new legacy qualification  projects in your site. All new
project-based qualifications  and requalifications  use process projects.
The Qualifications  detail area shows both qualification  processes that are based on process projects and
qualification  processes created from a data import. Y ou can only view details for qualification  processes based
on projects, since imported processes don't include questionnaires and tasks. Y ou can renew or cancel qualification
processes created from either projects or a data import.

About the Processes  Detail Area in the Supplier Profile
The Processes  detail area of a supplier's profile  is where you can create, manage, and view miscellaneous or
qualification  process projects and their questionnaires.
The Processes  detail area is only available if the process project feature (SM-16798) is enabled in your site.
If your site uses the supplier profile  summary (NGSM-2959), you can open the Processes  detail area from it by
choosing Processes  in the Quick Links  panel.
In the Processes  detail area, you can do the following things:
•Create any type of new miscellaneous or qualification  process [page 197]  for which you’re a process initiator.
•Search for any of the supplier's existing processes using the search bar.
•Filter the supplier's existing processes by status, owner, type, commodity, process initiator, or process
decision-maker by choosing Filter .
•View the details for any miscellaneous or qualification  process, including modular questionnaires used in the
process.

•Cancel a process or process renewal [page 212]  that is in progress.
•Renew a process [page 216]  to gather updated information.
•Set a status [page 220]  for a process if you're a decision-maker for it.
•Disqualify a qualification  process [page 205]  that is in the approved or partially approved status.
Y ou can fill out internal questionnaires [page 207]  that have been assigned to you and complete tasks for both

Supplier Qualification  and Disqualification  Status Flow
A supplier has an overall qualification  status, and each of their individual qualifications  has its own status.
A supplier can have multiple qualification  statuses for different  commodity, region, and department combinations.
The Qualifications  detail area of their profile  shows each of these individual statuses along with details of the
associated projects.
The supplier's overall qualification  status shows in:
•The Supplier Status  panel, in sites that use the supplier profile  summary (NGSM-2959).
•The Summary  area, in sites that use the classic supplier 360° profile.
The statuses of those individual qualifications  and disqualifications  determine the supplier's overall qualification
status.
The overall qualification  status for a supplier is based on the following priorities:
•If the supplier has at least one approved qualification  or requalification,  Qualified  is shown.
•If the supplier has no approved qualification  or requalification,  but has a disqualification,  the status of the
disqualification  is shown.
•If supplier has no approved qualification  or requalification  and no disqualification  in any stage of progress, the
highest

•If supplier has no approved qualification  or requalification  and no disqualification  in any stage of progress, the

•Otherwise, the supplier's status is Qualification  Not Started .

Transition from Legacy Supplier Qualification  Projects to
Qualification  Process Projects
If your site previously used legacy qualification  projects for supplier qualifications,  you can switch to using
qualification  process projects instead. Understanding what happens when you enable the process project feature in
your site and the current restrictions for qualification  processes is important to managing this transition.
When the Process Project Feature Starts Affecting  Qualification  Projects
Enabling the process project feature (SM-16798) in your site immediately disables creation of new projects from
the legacy supplier qualification  project template.
Changes to the Supplier Profile
If a supplier doesn't have a legacy qualification  project in any status, the Qualifications  detail area of their profile
only shows qualification  process controls and qualification  processes. The Qualifications  detail area is only visible
in the profiles  of registered suppliers.
If a supplier does have at least one legacy qualification  in any status, the Qualifications  detail area of their profile
includes two different  tabs:
•Legacy qualifications , which show any legacy qualification  and disqualification  projects.
•Process qualifications , which shows qualification  process controls and qualification  processes.
Managing Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Supplier Qualifications,  Preferred Category Status, and Other Lifecycle Processes PUBLIC 167

The following image shows an example of a Qualifications  detail area with these two tabs:
Transition for Existing Qualifications  and Disqualifications
Process qualification  doesn't disrupt qualification  activity that is in progress in legacy qualification  and
disqualification  projects, but for an entirely new qualification,  you can only use a qualification  process project.
During this transition:
•If a legacy qualification  or disqualification  is in progress (has a status of Qualification  Started , In
Qualification , Pending Qualification  Approval , Pending Qualification  Resubmit , In Disqualification , or
Pending Disqualification  Approval ):
•If it's based on a project, suppliers and internal users can complete the questionnaires and tasks in the
project.
•A process initiator can't create a qualification  process project based on the same combination of
commodities, regions, and departments.
•If a legacy qualification,  disqualification,  or requalification  is completed (has a status of Qualified , Disqualified ,
Restricted , or Expired ), a process initiator can create a qualification  process project for the same combination
of commodities, regions, and departments. Once a status decision is made for the process qualification,  the
legacy qualification  is marked as obsolete and:
168 PUBLICManaging Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Supplier Qualifications,  Preferred Category Status, and Other Lifecycle Processes

•The legacy qualification  remains in the supplier's profile  for information purposes only.
•Search results and API output reflect  the process qualification  rather than the legacy qualification.
•External questionnaires from a legacy qualification  project remain in the supplier's SAP Business Network
account but can't be edited.
The completed legacy qualification  is also marked obsolete if a customer administrator imports a process
status for the same combination of commodities, regions, and departments. Starting or importing a process
qualification  for a subset of the commodities, regions, and departments in a legacy qualification  has no effect
on the legacy qualification.
•A qualification  manager can still start a disqualification  (create a legacy disqualification  project) based on a
legacy qualification  (project-based or imported).
•Starting a requalification  based on a legacy qualification  or disqualification  (project-based or imported)
automatically opens the page for creating a new qualification  process project.
 Tip
Once the process project feature is enabled in your site, the legacy Supplier qualification  data  import is
disabled for most uses. However, you can still use it to update the statuses of imported legacy qualifications
to Expired . If a supplier has a legacy qualification  that was created from a data import, updating its status to
Expired  enables a process initiator to start a requalification  (renewal) and create a process qualification  for
the same combination of commodities, regions, and departments.
 Note
Migration of data from legacy qualification  projects to process qualification  projects isn't supported. When
a qualification  manager starts a requalification  from a legacy qualification  project, the resulting new process
qualification  project starts from scratch.
Transition for Supplier Qualification  Status
If a supplier has a legacy qualification  for a combination of commodities, regions, and departments, that
legacy qualification  continues to determine the supplier's qualification  status for that combination until there's
a qualification  process with an approved, conditionally approved, or denied status for the same combination. At
that point, the qualification  process becomes the source of the qualification  status.

combinations of commodities, regions, and departments.
These topics are applicable if your organization uses supplier qualification  and disqualification  projects to manage
supplier qualifications.  If your organization uses qualification  process projects, refer to Supplier Qualification  and

Supplier Qualification  and Requalification  Using Supplier
Qualification  Projects
After a supplier is registered, a qualification  manager can start qualifying the supplier for one or more specific
combinations of commodities, regions, and (optionally) departments by starting qualification  projects and sending
qualification  questionnaires. Approvers review the supplier's answers and approve or deny the qualifications.
 Note
This topic describes qualification  and requalification  using supplier qualification  projects. If your site uses
the process projects to manage supplier qualifications,  refer to Qualification  and Miscellaneous Supplier
Management Process Projects [page 188] .
Although a customer administrator can set qualification  statuses for suppliers using a data import operation in
both SAP Ariba Supplier Management solutions, supplier qualification  projects are only available in solutions that
include SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and Performance.
Supplier qualification  questionnaires typically ask for detailed information from the supplier that’s specific  to the
qualification's  commodities and regions. For example, qualification  questionnaires can ask suppliers about their
sourcing and sustainability processes; shipping, delivery, and return policies; commodity-specific  certifications;
compliance with policies; and other matters. New qualifications  are always based on commodities and regions that
a qualification  manager selects. They don't have to be for the commodities and regions for which the supplier is
registered. Qualifications  and requalifications  are also based on departments if they're enabled in your site.
When a supplier has either been

y-specific  certifications;
compliance with policies; and other matters. New qualifications  are always based on commodities and regions that
a qualification  manager selects. They don't have to be for the commodities and regions for which the supplier is
registered. Qualifications  and requalifications  are also based on departments if they're enabled in your site.
When a supplier has either been disqualified  or had a qualification  expire, they're eligible for requalification.
Qualifications  and requalifications  use the same workflow;  however, a requalification  is always based on the same
commodities, regions, and departments as the previous disqualification  or expired qualification.
The supplier qualification  project template determines:
•The questionnaire segments for various commodity, region, and department combinations, and which
segments are required.
•The approvers for the qualification  or requalification.  In sites that use buyer category assignments, approvers
are automatically assigned based on the commodities, regions, and departments of the qualification  or
requalification.
•The content of the questionnaire segments.
170 PUBLICManaging Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Supplier Qualifications,  Preferred Category Status, and Other Lifecycle Processes

•Whether other stakeholders in your organization must also approve the qualification  or requalification.
•Whether internal questionnaires or other tasks or documents are part of the qualification  or requalification  w.
•Whether the qualification  expires after a specific  date, and if so, whether there's a waiting period before the
supplier can be requalified.
The category or supplier managers assigned to the supplier start a new qualification  or a requalification,  send
the questionnaire, review the supplier's answers, request more information, approve or deny the qualification  or
requalification,  and monitor the approval flow  and status in the Qualification  detail area of the supplier's profile  on
the Supplier Management  tab of the dashboard. The supplier answers the qualification  questionnaire in their Ariba
Network for Suppliers profile.  The supplier's profile  information is automatically shared between SAP Business
Network and SAP Ariba Supplier Management solutions.
Notifications  let the supplier know that they've been invited to qualify for specific  commodities, regions, and
departments and provide a link to the questionnaire; let approvers know that they need to approve a supplier
qualification  or requalification;  let supplier or category managers know when one of their qualifications  has expired
or is eligible for requalification;  and let the supplier know that the qualification  or requalification  has been approved,
denied, or requires more information.
At any point after the supplier qualification  or requalification  has been approved, category or supplier managers
can:
•Start new qualifications  for additional combinations of commodities, regions, and departments.
•Evaluate the supplier for a preferred category status (if your company uses preferred supplier management
projects).
•Disqualify the supplier for commodities, regions, and departments for which it was previously qualified.
In solutions that include SAP Ariba Sourcing, event templates can specify that suppliers must have specific
qualification  statuses for the event's commodities and regions to either participate in or be awarded events.
Departments aren't considered for event eligibility.

Supplier Disqualifications  Using Supplier Disqualification  Projects
After a supplier is qualified  or preferred, a qualification  manager can ask to disqualify the supplier for previously
qualified  commodities, regions, and (optionally) departments by submitting a disqualification  form.
Although a customer administrator can set qualification  statuses for suppliers using a data import operation
in both SAP Ariba Supplier Management solutions, supplier qualification  and disqualification  projects are only
available in solutions that include SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and Performance.
Managing Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Supplier Qualifications,  Preferred Category Status, and Other Lifecycle Processes PUBLIC 171

 Note
Disqualification  projects work with qualifications  based on supplier qualification  projects. If your site uses
process projects to manage supplier qualifications,  you don't use disqualification  projects to manage
disqualification  statuses. For more information, refer to Qualification  and Miscellaneous Supplier Management
Process Projects [page 188] .
Disqualifications  are always based on a previous qualification.  However, the disqualified  commodities and regions
don't have to match exactly, and the disqualification  always applies to all commodity or region levels below the
ones approved for disqualification.  For example, a supplier can be qualified  for Europe and later disqualified  only
in France; that disqualification  applies to all departments and cities in France. Disqualifications  are also based on
departments if they're enabled in your site.
The disqualification  project template determines:
•What information qualification  managers add to the form.
•Whether the disqualification  requires approval, and if so, who the approvers are.
•Whether other stakeholders in your organization must also approve the disqualification.
•Whether the disqualification  is immediate upon approval, takes effect  on a specified  date without phase-out, or
takes effect  automatically after a phase-out period during which the supplier has Restricted  status.
Category or supplier managers start the disqualification,  approve or deny it, and monitor the approval flow  and
status in the Qualification  detail area of the supplier's profile  on the Supplier Management  dashboard.
If a disqualification  includes an approval flow,  notifications  let the approver know that the disqualification  requires
approval, let the requesting user know when the disqualification  has been approved or denied, and show the final
approver so that requesting users can have further discussions about denied disqualifications.
Once a disqualification  is approved, any preferred supplier status for the disqualified  commodities, regions, and
departments (including any preferred commodities, regions, or departments above or below the disqualified
commodities, regions, and departments in the same branch of the hierarchy) is automatically removed. The
rationale is that suppliers can't be considered "preferred" if they're disqualified,  for whatever reason and through
whatever means,

status for the disqualified  commodities, regions, and
departments (including any preferred commodities, regions, or departments above or below the disqualified
commodities, regions, and departments in the same branch of the hierarchy) is automatically removed. The
rationale is that suppliers can't be considered "preferred" if they're disqualified,  for whatever reason and through
whatever means, at any hierarchical level within the same combination. For example, if a supplier is preferred for
shoes and later disqualified  for apparel, the preferred status for shoes is automatically removed. Likewise, if a
supplier is preferred for apparel and later disqualified  for shoes, the preferred status for apparel is automatically
removed. In solutions that include SAP Ariba Sourcing, if event templates are restricted to qualified  participants,
or automatically invite preferred suppliers, disqualified  suppliers aren't event participants. However, disqualification
is otherwise just a status. There's no mechanism for preventing users from inviting disqualified  suppliers to
unrestricted sourcing events or conducting other activities with them.
Qualification  managers can requalify disqualifications.  If a qualification  is restricted, a qualification  manager can
only do so after the restricted period has passed and the supplier is disqualified.  If a disqualification  has a minimum
disqualification  period, they can only do so after that date has passed. They can also start new qualifications  for
categories, regions, and departments other than the ones for which the supplier was disqualified.
 Note
•If a supplier is requalified  for a process, the supplier can again be marked "preferred" for the same
combination of commodity, region, and department.
•If a supplier is qualified  for more than one process type for the same commodity, region, and department
combination, is preferred for the same combination, and is subsequently disqualified  for one of those
processes, then the "preferred" status is automatically invalidated.

Starting a New Qualification  Using a Supplier Qualification  Project
Use these steps to start qualifications  for different  combinations of commodities, regions, and departments if
your organization uses supplier qualification  projects. Questionnaire content in qualification  projects is tailored to
specific  combinations to measure whether or not a supplier meets your organization's standards and requirements
for them.
Prerequisites
T o start a new supplier qualification,  you must be a member of the Supplier Qualification  Manager  or SM Ops
Administrator  group.
Supplier qualifications  and disqualifications  are only available in sites that include SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and
Performance.
Context
This topic is only applicable if your organization manages qualifications  using supplier qualification  and
disqualification  projects. If your organization uses process qualification  projects, refer to Creating a Qualification  or
Miscellaneous Supplier Management Process Project [page 197] . If the process project feature is enabled in your
site, creation of new supplier qualification  projects is disabled, and you can create process qualification  projects
instead.
Supplier qualifications  are always based on a commodity and a region. If your organizations prequalification  form
includes a question for it, you can also choose a department for the qualification.  The commodities, regions, and
departments you select determine which external (supplier-facing) questionnaire segments are available for the
qualification,  which can be required or optional. This targeting allows your organization to evaluate the suitability
of a supplier based on specific  metrics, which can involve legal requirements, different  regulatory jurisdictions,
business criticality, and other factors.
The qualification  combines all the questionnaire segments that you select into a single questionnaire for
the supplier to fill out, and generates a single notification  with a link to that combined questionnaire. Y our
organization's qualification

pplier based on specific  metrics, which can involve legal requirements, different  regulatory jurisdictions,
business criticality, and other factors.
The qualification  combines all the questionnaire segments that you select into a single questionnaire for
the supplier to fill out, and generates a single notification  with a link to that combined questionnaire. Y our
organization's qualification  setup can also include one or more internal questionnaires. Internal questionnaires
Managing Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Supplier Qualifications,  Preferred Category Status, and Other Lifecycle Processes PUBLIC 173

aren't dependent on the qualification's  commodities, regions, and departments and are added automatically when
you start a qualification.
Since commodities, regions, and departments are hierarchical, with higher and lower levels, qualifying a supplier
for a commodity, region, or department qualifies  them for the lower levels of the hierarchy branch. For example,
qualifying a supplier for the U.S. qualifies  them for all 50 states.
The prequalification  form can include questions about expiration date and requalification  eligibility date. Whether
or not these questions are included, or are required if they're included, depends on your organization's qualification
setup. If you specify an expiration date for a qualification,  it automatically expires on that date. Otherwise, the
supplier remains qualified  until a qualification  manager starts a disqualification.  If you specify a requalification
eligibility date, once a supplier is disqualified  or a qualification  has expired, a qualification  manager can't start a
requalification  until that date is reached.
Y ou can start any number of qualifications  for the same supplier to qualify them for different  combinations of
commodities, regions, and departments. Y ou can't start a new qualification  for a combination that is already
covered by an existing qualification.
Procedure
1. Locate the supplier you want to qualify on the Supplier Management  dashboard.
2. Choose the supplier's name to open their profile.
3. Perform one of the following actions:
•In the supplier's profile  summary, choose the Qualifications  panel.
•In the classic supplier 360° profile,  choose Qualifications  in the left-hand navigation bar.
The Qualifications  detail area of the supplier's profile  opens.
4. Choose  (add).
5. Choose one or more commodities, regions, and (if your organization uses them) departments by performing
one of the following actions:
•Choose Search  and enter search terms in the field,  then select a value from the list of search results.
•Choose Browse  and use the arrows (  ) to navigate through the hierarchy, then select the values you want
to qualify the supplier for.
6. (Optional) If the prequalification  form includes a question about expiration date and you want the qualification
to expire, specify an expiration date.
7 . (Optional) If the prequalification  form includes a question about

lue from the list of search results.
•Choose Browse  and use the arrows (  ) to navigate through the hierarchy, then select the values you want
to qualify the supplier for.
6. (Optional) If the prequalification  form includes a question about expiration date and you want the qualification
to expire, specify an expiration date.
7 . (Optional) If the prequalification  form includes a question about requalification  eligibility date and you want to
ensure that the supplier can't be requalified  until a specific  date, specify that date.
8. Choose Next .
9. Choose the external (supplier-facing) questionnaire segments that you want to include in the qualification.  Y ou
can't uncheck required questionnaires.
T o see the content of a questionnaire, choose  (view).
10. Choose the supplier contact to whom to send the qualification.  If you want to send the questionnaire to a new
contact, you can add them here [page 54] .
11. (Optional) T o change any aspect of the new qualification,  such as commodities, regions, or dates, choose Edit ,
make your changes, and choose Next .

Results
If the supplier has never been qualified  before, they now have an overall qualification  status of Qualification
Started . If they had at least one previously approved qualification,  their overall qualification  status remains
Qualified . In either case, the status for the current qualification  is Qualification  Started . The supplier contact that
you chose receives an email notification  with a link that they can use to fill out the combined external questionnaire.
If your qualification  includes any internal questionnaires, they're now available to fill out as well.
In the supplier's profile,  the new qualification  shows on the Qualifications  detail area. T o view qualification  details,
including questionnaires, tasks, and process flow,  choose   (view).
Next Steps
External qualification  questionnaires close after an amount of time defined  in your site's questionnaire setup. After
a questionnaire has closed, the supplier contact can no longer edit it to fill it out. If a supplier hasn't responded, or
if the invitation has bounced, reinviting the supplier to qualify [page 128]  reopens any closed questionnaires and
allows you to choose a different  recipient if necessary. By default, questionnaires close 30 days after the invitation
is sent, but your organization can specify a different  amount of time in its questionnaire setup.

Reinviting a Supplier to Qualify or Requalify Using a Qualification
Project
Use these steps to remind a supplier to respond to an initial qualification  or requalification  invitation or to change
the invited supplier contact.
Prerequisites
T o reinvite a supplier to qualify or requalify using a qualification  project, you must be a member of the Supplier
Qualification  Manager  or SM Ops Administrator  group. If your organization manages qualifications  using process
projects instead, you can request updates for included modular questionnaires [page 240]  instead.
T o add or edit a supplier contact on the send questionnaires page, you must be a member of the Supplier
Registration Manager  or SM Ops Administrator  group. Members of the Supplier Qualification  Manager  group
can add but not edit supplier contacts on the send questionnaires page.
Y ou can only reinvite a supplier to qualify or requalify if the qualification  project is in Qualification  Started  status.
Once the supplier has opened and viewed an external questionnaire and the qualification  project has moved to In
Qualification  status, you can no longer reinvite them. If the supplier doesn't submit the questionnaire, you must
use another method to remind them.
Context
Reinviting a supplier is useful if:
•The previously invited contact is the right person to complete the qualification  or requalification  but they
need a reminder.  Reinviting the same contact sends them another invitation as a reminder.
•The previously invited contact isn't the right person to complete the qualification  or requalification.  Y ou
can choose a different  contact when reinviting the supplier.
•External questionnaires have closed before the invited contact responded.  External questionnaires close
after an amount of time defined  in your site's questionnaire setup. By default, that amount is 30 days, but
your organization can specify a different  amount of time in its questionnaire setup. After a questionnaire has
closed, the supplier contact can no longer edit it to fill it out. Reinviting the supplier reopens the external
questionnaires.
Y ou can't change which external questionnaires are included when you reinvite a supplier to qualify or requalify. The
qualification  always includes the external questionnaires selected during the original invitation.

1. Locate the supplier you want to reinvite on

questionnaires.

2. Choose the supplier's name to open their profile.

4. Locate the qualification  and choose   (more options) > Reinvite   .
5. (Optional) T o change the questionnaire recipient, choose a different  contact. If the supplier only has one

Filling Out an Internal Qualification  Questionnaire
Use these steps if your organization's qualification  projects include one or more internal questionnaires and you're
a questionnaire editor. Filling out internal questionnaires can be a required or optional step in supplier qualification.
Prerequisites
T o edit an internal qualification  questionnaire, you must be a member of the Supplier Qualification  Manager  or SM
Ops Administrator  group.
If the questionnaire is associated with a T o Do task, you must also be a task owner to edit the questionnaire while
completing the T o Do task.
Managing Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Supplier Qualifications,  Preferred Category Status, and Other Lifecycle Processes PUBLIC 177

Context
Internal supplier qualification  questionnaires typically collect supplemental information about a qualification  that
only people in your organization can provide, such as evaluations.
Internal questionnaires can be associated with T o Do tasks. If there's no T o Do task associated with an internal
questionnaire, or if it's associated with an optional T o Do task, filling  out the questionnaire is optional. If there's a
required T o Do task associated with an internal questionnaire and you're a task owner, you must submit the internal
questionnaire to complete the task.
Internal qualification  questionnaires that don't have a T o Do task are editable as long as they're not in approval until
the qualification  project is finally  approved or denied. An internal qualification  questionnaires with T o Do tasks is
editable as long as any T o Do task on the questionnaire is open, and is no longer editable when all of the T o Do
tasks on the questionnaire are complete. When the tasks on an internal qualification  questionnaire start depends
on how the qualification  project is set up. If a task doesn't have a predecessor, it starts when the qualification  starts.
Otherwise, it starts when the predecessor task is completed.
If the owner of a T o Do task on an internal questionnaire is a project group, all members of that project group who
have the appropriate permissions can edit the internal questionnaire. The first  member to submit the questionnaire
becomes the owner of the completed task..
 Note
Attachments in supplier management questionnaires can't include embedded documents.
The internal qualification  questionnaires mentioned in this topic are meant for legacy qualification  projects only.
If your site previously used legacy qualification  projects for supplier qualifications,  you can consider switching to
qualification  process projects instead. See Filling out an internal questionnaire for a supplier management process .

•T o fill out an internal qualification  questionnaire, navigate to the Qualifications  detail area of the profile,
then locate the qualification  and choose   (more options) > View    .
•If the questionnaire is associated with an active T o Do task and you're a task owner,

email notification.

Disqualifying a Supplier Using a Disqualification  Project
Use these steps to disqualify a previously qualified  supplier when they no longer meet your organization's
standards or requirements.
Prerequisites
T o disqualify a supplier, you must be a member of the Supplier Qualification  Manager  or SM Ops Administrator
group.
Supplier qualifications  and disqualifications  are only available in sites that include SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and
Performance.
Managing Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Supplier Qualifications,  Preferred Category Status, and Other Lifecycle Processes PUBLIC 179

If the process project feature (SM-16798) is enabled in your site, you can only start a disqualification  project for an
existing, legacy qualification  project. For process qualifications,  a process decision-maker can change its status to
denied [page 220]  instead.
Context
Supplier disqualifications  are always based on an existing qualification,  which can be the result of either an
approved qualification  project or a data import. The qualification  is for specific  commodities and regions and
(if your organization uses them) departments. Y ou can disqualify the supplier for the same combination or
commodities, regions, and departments in the original qualification,  or for subsets of the qualified  commodities,
regions, and departments. For example, if a supplier has a qualification  for clothing in Europe, you can disqualify
them for shoes in Spain. Disqualifications  show under the original qualification  in the Qualifications  area of the
supplier 360° profile.  If a supplier is already disqualified  for part of the original qualification,  and you can disqualify
the supplier for other commodities regions, and departments until all of the original qualification  is disqualified.
Since commodities, regions, and departments are hierarchical, with higher and lower levels, disqualifying a supplier
for a commodity, region, or department automatically disqualifies  them for the lower levels of the hierarchy branch.
For example, disqualifying a supplier for the U.S. disqualifies  them for all fifty  states.
Depending on how the disqualification  process is set up in your site, a disqualification  can take effect  immediately
upon approval, on a date that you specify in the disqualification  questionnaire, or after a phase-out period that you
specify in the questionnaire.
Procedure
1. Locate the supplier that you want to disqualify on the Supplier Management  dashboard.
2. Choose the supplier's name to open their profile.
3. Perform one of the following actions:
•In the supplier's profile  summary, choose the Qualifications  panel.
•In the classic supplier 360° profile,  choose Qualifications  in the left-hand navigation bar.
The Qualifications  detail area of the supplier's profile  opens.
4. Locate the qualification  that you want to disqualify either fully or partially and choose   (more options) >
Disqualify   .
If your organization now uses process

•In the supplier's profile  summary, choose the Qualifications  panel.
•In the classic supplier 360° profile,  choose Qualifications  in the left-hand navigation bar.
The Qualifications  detail area of the supplier's profile  opens.
4. Locate the qualification  that you want to disqualify either fully or partially and choose   (more options) >
Disqualify   .
If your organization now uses process qualifications,  existing legacy qualifications  for which you can start a
disqualification  project are located on the Legacy qualifications  tab.
5. Choose one or more commodities, regions, and (if your organization uses them) departments by performing
one of the following actions:
•Choose Search  and enter search terms in the field,  then select a value from the list of search results.
•Choose Browse  and use the arrows (  ) to navigate through the hierarchy, then select values to
include in the request. Y our choices are limited to the values includes in the qualification  on which this
disqualification  is based. Y ou can select the same values or a subset.
6. Enter any other information in the disqualification  questionnaire.
180 PUBLICManaging Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Supplier Qualifications,  Preferred Category Status, and Other Lifecycle Processes

7 . Choose Submit .
Results
If the supplier doesn't have any other qualifications,  after a full disqualification  is approved, they now have the
following overall qualification  status:
•Disqualified , if the disqualification  is immediate.
•Restricted , if your disqualification  process includes a phase-out period and you specified  a date in the
questionnaire. This status automatically updates to Disqualified  after the phase-out period has ended.
•Qualified , if you specified  a future date for the disqualification  in the questionnaire. This status automatically
updates to Disqualified  on that date.
If the supplier has at least one other valid qualification,  or the disqualification  is only partial, their overall
qualification  status remains Qualified  but the associated qualification  now shows the appropriate disqualification
status.
Once you've disqualified  a supplier for a combination of commodities, regions, and departments, they can no
longer participate in new sourcing events that require qualification  for participation. Disqualification  doesn't
remove suppliers from existing events. Disqualification  also removed any related preferred status level for the
disqualified  commodity, region, and department combination.

Requalifying a Supplier Using a Supplier Qualification  Project
Use these steps to requalify a supplier that has an expired or denied qualification,  or has been disqualified,  if your
organization uses supplier qualification  projects to manage supplier qualifications.
Prerequisites
T o requalify a supplier, you must be a member of the Supplier Qualification  Manager  or SM Ops Administrator
group.
Supplier qualifications  and disqualifications  are only available in sites that include SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and
Performance.
Context
Requalifications  are always based on one of the following previous qualifications:
•An imported disqualification  status. In this case, the requalification  creates a new qualification  project with the
commodities, regions, and departments associated with the status.
•An existing qualification  project that was denied or has expired. In this case, the requalification  relaunches the
original denied or expired qualification  project.
•A disqualification  project based on the entire set of commodities, regions, and departments of an existing
qualification  project. In this case, the requalification  relaunches the original qualification  project.
•A disqualification  project based on a subset of the commodities, regions, and departments of an existing
qualification  project. In this case, the requalification  creates a new qualification  project with the subset of
commodities, regions, and departments from the disqualification.
If the process project feature (SM-16798) is enabled in your site, starting a requalification  based on a legacy

who fill them out for the requalification,  including a different  supplier contact than the one who filled  out the
