Approving or Denying a Qualification,  Disqualification,  or
Requalification  Based on a Supplier Qualification  or Disqualification
Project
If you're in the approval flow  for a supplier qualification,  disqualification,  or requalification  based on a supplier
qualification  or disqualification  project, you can approve or deny it.
Prerequisites
Supplier qualifications  and disqualifications  are only available in sites that include SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and
Performance.
Context
This topic is applicable if your organization manages supplier qualifications  using supplier qualification  and
disqualification  projects. If your organization manages supplier qualifications  using process projects, approvers
for modular questionnaire projects included in a process qualification  approve or deny them [page 209] . Then
either a decision-maker sets the qualification  process's status [page 220]  or the status is automatically set based
on the statuses of included modular questionnaires.
Procedure
1. T o open the approval task details page with the questionnaire answers, perform one of the following actions:
•Navigate to the Qualifications  detail area of the supplier's profile,  then locate the qualification  and choose
  (more options) > View   . On the qualification  details page, locate the approval task and choose
  (take action).
•Choose the link in the approval notification  email.
2. Review the questionnaire answers.
 Tip
If an approver previously requested more information for this questionnaire, or if the questionnaire is for
a requalification  based on an original qualification  project, use the Latest Version  and Previous Version
columns to compare answers. These columns show the approval decisions for the versions. T o show only
the questions with updated answers, choose Updated . T o show all questions and answers, choose All.
.
3. In the upper right corner of the page, perform one of the following actions:
•T o approve the questionnaire, choose Approve , enter an optional comment, and choose Approve .
•T o deny the questionnaire, choose Deny , enter an explanatory comment, and choose Deny .
184 PUBLICManaging Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Supplier Qualifications,  Preferred Category Status, and Other Lifecycle Processes

•T o request additional information from the respondent, choose Request Additional Info , enter a comment
explaining what information you want, and choose Request Additional Info .
Results
For qualifications  and requalifications,  if you're the final  approver, the qualification  now has Qualified  or
Qualification  Rejected  status and receives an email notification  of that status. If not, the qualification  remains
in Pending Qualification  Approval  status until the final  approval or denial.
For disqualifications,  if you're the final  approver, the qualification  is now in Restricted  or Disqualified  status,
depending on how your organization's disqualification  process is set up. If not, the qualification  remains in
Qualified  status until the final  approval or denial. Suppliers in Restricted  status are automatically disqualified
at the date specified  on the disqualification  form. If the supplier has preferred status for the commodities, regions,
and departments specified  in the disqualification,  that status is automatically removed.
Any comments that you or other approvers added during questionnaire approval show on both the questionnaire
and approval task details pages, which you can see by viewing the questionnaire or task. Questionnaire details
pages show all comments for all tasks in the project, and you can filter  them to just show the comments for the
current questionnaire. T ask details pages show only the comments associated with the current task. Depending on
how your site's notifications  are set up, approval comments can also be included in approval or denial notifications
to suppliers.
If you requested additional information during approval of an external questionnaire and entered a comment, the
supplier sees the most recent approval comment when they revise their response to the questionnaire.
Next Steps
If you denied the qualification,  a qualification  manager can requalify them [page 182]  at a later date.
If your organization uses preferred supplier management to grant preferred category status to suppliers, after the
qualification  or requalification  for the specified  commodities, regions, and departments is finally  approved, you can
request a preferred category status [page 225]  for the supplier for those commodities, regions, and departments.
If you approved a disqualification  with a phase-out period, you can edit

Editing or Removing a Qualification  Restriction from a Supplier
If your organization's disqualification  projects include a phase-out period, a supplier qualification  has Restricted
status until the end of that period. Use these steps to edit or remove the restriction by changing the date on which
the supplier moves to Disqualified  status.
Prerequisites
T o edit or remove a qualification  restriction, you must be a member of the Supplier Qualification  Manager  or SM
Ops Administrator  group.
Supplier qualifications  and disqualifications  are only available in sites that include SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and
Performance. The supplier disqualification  form in your site must be set up to include a phase-out period for the
qualification.
Qualification  restrictions are only available in supplier disqualification  projects. If your organization uses process
projects to manage supplier qualifications,  a decision-maker can change a qualification  process's status [page
220]  instead.
Context
When a supplier qualification  is restricted, it's not disqualified  until the restricted period has ended. During that
time, you can't start a requalification  for it. Y ou can edit the disqualification  date for a restricted qualification  to
lengthen or shorten the restricted period or to remove the disqualification  date entirely so that the qualification
moves immediately to Disqualified  status and you can start a requalification.
Procedure
1. Locate the supplier from which you want to remove the qualification  restriction on the Supplier Management
dashboard.
 Tip
In supplier search results, you can filter  by the qualification  status Restricted .
2. Choose the supplier name to open their profile.
3. Perform one of the following actions:
•In the supplier's profile  summary, choose the Qualifications  panel.
•In the classic supplier 360° profile,  choose Qualifications  in the left-hand navigation bar.
The Qualifications  detail area of the supplier's profile  opens.
4. For the restricted qualification,  choose   (more options) > Edit    .
5. Edit or delete the disqualification  date.
186 PUBLICManaging Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Supplier Qualifications,  Preferred Category Status, and Other Lifecycle Processes

6. Choose Submit .
Results
If the disqualification  project has an approval flow,  once your edit of the restriction is finally  approved, the new
phase-out date takes effect.  If you removed the disqualification  date, the qualification  moved immediately to
Disqualified  status.

Making a Status Decision for a Supplier Management Process [page 220]
Qualification  and Miscellaneous Supplier Management Process
Projects
Y our organization can use supplier management process projects to collect information about and decide status
for supplier qualifications,  other business processes, or their discontinuation.
A supplier management process project is a framework that includes a collection of modular questionnaires to
gather information relevant to the process and a workflow  for deciding an outcome. A process project can be for:
•Qualification : If your organization uses process projects for supplier qualifications,  the modular questionnaires
can ask for specific  qualification-related  information for a particular combination of commodities, regions,
and departments. A qualification  process can include one or more general modular questionnaires that cover
your organization's universal qualification  criteria. It can also include specific  modular questionnaires related
to critical information or certifications  for specific  commodities, regions, and departments. Qualification
processes can include both internal and external questionnaires.
If your company uses a qualification  process and you're a process initiator for it, you can create and manage
those qualification  process projects in the Qualifications  or Processes  detail area of the supplier profile.
•Miscellaneous processes : If your organization uses process projects for any other types of supplier
management process, they can also include external or internal modular questionnaires relevant to that
specific  process. If your organization uses a miscellaneous process and you're a process initiator for it, you can
create and manage those process projects in the Processes  detail area of the supplier profile.
•Discontinuation : If your organization uses qualification  process projects, they can be disqualified  using a
discontinuation process. A discontinuation process can also include internal modular questionnaires to collect
information about the reason for discontinuing the qualification  process. Y ou can fully or partially discontinue
existing qualification  processes in the Qualifications  detail area of the supplier profile.
Data imported in your site determine whether a process is a qualification  process, discontinuation process, or
other (miscellaneous) process

nclude internal modular questionnaires to collect
information about the reason for discontinuing the qualification  process. Y ou can fully or partially discontinue
existing qualification  processes in the Qualifications  detail area of the supplier profile.
Data imported in your site determine whether a process is a qualification  process, discontinuation process, or
other (miscellaneous) process and the names of its statuses. The process project template determines:
•Who can start the process (process initiators) and set its final  status (decision-makers).
•The intake form that process initiators fill out when creating or renewing a process. This intake form can
include questions about the commodities, regions, and departments to which the process is applicable. A
question about at least one of these three elements is required. It can also include questions that set schedules
for the process's expiration and for expiration reminder notifications.
•The types of external or internal modular questionnaires used in the process.
Within a process, each modular questionnaire is defined  by its own template, which determines:
•The questionnaire's type, which determines the processes it's included in.
•Whether the questionnaire is internal or external.
•Whether or not the questionnaire is required in matching processes.
•The commodities, regions, and departments to which the questionnaire applies.
•The content of the questionnaire.
•The approval flow  for the questionnaire.
•Whether there are other tasks in the questionnaire's workflow.
•Whether or not the questionnaire project itself has an expiration date, and if so, which people in your
organization receive notifications  of pending and elapsed expirations in addition to the questionnaire owner.
188 PUBLICManaging Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Supplier Qualifications,  Preferred Category Status, and Other Lifecycle Processes

•Whether the expiration of a certificate  in a questionnaire triggers the questionnaire project's expiration as well.
•Whether or not recipients can update the questionnaire after it's first  approved, and if so, whether or not the
updates also require approval and the update approval flow.
•Whether the questionnaire automatically generates update requests to recipients each time it's included in a
new or renewed process.
Each modular questionnaire contains its own answers. Once a recipient has submitted a modular questionnaire for
the first  time, those answers are available in the questionnaire in any process that reuses it.
Users with the appropriate permissions create a process for a supplier in the relevant area of their supplier profile.
Process creation involves several steps:
1. The process initiator chooses what type of process that they want to create, then specifies  the commodities,
regions, and departments for the process and answers any other questions in the process intake form. If the
intake form includes questions about an expiration schedule, they can also specify when the process expires
and its expiration notification  schedule. The following image shows an example of this step:
2. The process initiator sees the modular questionnaires that are both included in the process and  match
the process commodities, regions, and departments. These questionnaires can be newly assigned, meaning
that they’ve never been filled  out before for this particular supplier. If they've already been filled  out and
approved for the supplier, the process initiator has the option to request an update, or the update request
Managing Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Supplier Qualifications,  Preferred Category Status, and Other Lifecycle Processes PUBLIC 189

can be automatic. Questionnaires can be required or optional. The process initiator can choose which optional
questionnaires to include in the process. The following image shows an example of this step:
190 PUBLICManaging Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Supplier Qualifications,  Preferred Category Status, and Other Lifecycle Processes

3. The process initiator chooses recipients for both external and internal questionnaires and finishes  creating the
process project. The following image shows an example of this step:
Notifications  let questionnaire recipients know that they need to fill out or update their assigned questionnaires
and inform the supplier primary contact that they've been invited to participate in the process.
Suppliers fill out or update external modular questionnaires and view any processes to which they've been
invited in their SAP Business Network account. Internal recipients fill out or update the internal modular supplier
management questionnaires to which they've been assigned in the Questionnaires  detail area of the supplier's
profile  or from the detail page for the process. T ask owners and approvers complete T o Do tasks and approve or
deny individual questionnaires in the Questionnaires  detail area or from the process details page as well.
While a process is in progress, the process project owner can cancel it if necessary.
After all approvals and other tasks for every questionnaire in the process are completed, a decision-maker for the
process decides its final  status in the Qualifications , or Processes  detail area of the supplier profile.  These final
statuses have names that are specific  to your organization's business process, but they correspond to an approved,
conditionally approved, or denied status.
Once a process is complete, a process initiator can renew it to collect updated information for a new status
decision. For qualification  processes, a renewal is a requalification.
Managing Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Supplier Qualifications,  Preferred Category Status, and Other Lifecycle Processes PUBLIC 191

 Note
Before starting a renewal, verify that the correct modular questionnaire is selected. Avoid canceling a renewal
unless necessary. If a new renewal is created immediately after cancelation, the intake form may not display or
accept values for Commodity , Region , or Department , which can prevent the process from starting. T o prevent
this from happening, wait briefly  between canceling a renewal and creating a new one. Ensure all required fields
are visible on the intake form.
Decision-makers can also update the final  status for a process at any time without a renewal. For example, if a
critical underlying questionnaire expires without an update, a decision-maker can change the final  status of the
process from approved to denied. Currently, no separate disqualification  process is supported, but changing the
status of a qualification  process to denied produces the same result. The primary supplier contact, process project
owner, and all the decision-makers receive notifications  when a decision-maker sets or updates the final  status of a
process.
After a qualification  process has been approved, category or supplier managers can evaluate the supplier for a
preferred category status level (if your organization uses preferred supplier levels) for the same combination of
commodities, regions, and departments or a subset. This option isn't available if the process is only conditionally
approved.
The process initiator of an approved or partially approved qualification  process can fully or partially discontinue the
qualification  process.
Administrators can also create processes using data imports. These processes aren't based on projects and don't
have associated questionnaires or workflows.  However, if the imported process has an eligible status, users with
the appropriate permissions can:
•Cancel it using the same data import.
•Cancel or renew it (creating a process project for it) in the supplier's profile.
Canceling a process created using a data import doesn't generate any notifications,  since there's no associated
project.

Discontinuation Processes
Discontinuation is associated with a separate supplier lifecycle stage Disqualification . It's a separate process
with its own workflow  and process statuses. Discontinuing existing qualification  processes is to disqualify these
processes.
Currently, a site can only have one discontinuation process type and one associated discontinuation process
project template. The discontinuation process type maps to all qualification  process types in the site.
Currently, discontinuation isn't supported for miscellaneous processes. Only qualification  processes can be
discontinued.
Y ou can create a full or partial discontinuation process, cancel an ongoing discontinuation process, renew a
discontinued supplier management process, or make a decision for a discontinuation process in the supplier 360º
profile.  The administrator in your organization can also perform all these operations through the data import task.
The qualification  process projects imported through the data import task can be partially or completely
discontinued in the user interface. The status is updated to show the discontinuation of the qualification  process.
 Note
After a data import task is successfully executed to create a discontinuation process with status
"discontinuation denied" for an existing supplier management process, no discontinuation process is created in
the supplier 360º profile.
After a data import task is successfully executed to update an existing discontinuation process to the
"discontinuation denied" status, the results are as follows:
•The existing discontinuation process is removed.
•For a full discontinuation, the original supplier management process is reverted, and the status is
"qualified"  or "conditionally qualified".

Workflow  for Creating a Qualification  or Other Supplier
Management Process Project
Process initiators create a new process by answering questions on an intake form and selecting questionnaires to
include and their recipients.
1. In a supplier's profile,  a process initiator creates a new process project by selecting a process type and filling
out and submitting the process intake form. The form can include questions about:
•The process's commodities, regions, and departments. A question about at least one of these three
elements is required.
•The process's expiration schedule and expiration reminder schedule, if the process can expire.
•Other aspects of the process that are part of your organization's business process.
Commodity, region, and department values are prepopulated in intake forms with entries from supplier request
or registration. If a supplier has both a request and a registration with this data, the registration data is used
to prepopulate the intake form. Therefore, the process initiator doesn't have to manually select these values
in intake forms while creating process qualifications.  However, if they're creating a process qualification  for a
different  matrix combination, they must use the dropdown menu to select the desired matrix values.
Managing Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Supplier Qualifications,  Preferred Category Status, and Other Lifecycle Processes PUBLIC 195

Commodity, region, and department values in intake forms are prepopulated based on the following inbuilt
rules:
•If both supplier request and supplier registration contain identical or different  matrix values, the intake
form is prepopulated with data from supplier registration.
 Note
If matrix values from supplier registration are available, they always override values from supplier
request. For example, if supplier registration contains only commodity values and supplier request
contains commodity, region, and department values, all the matrix values from registration (even
empty values) override all the values of supplier request.
•If a supplier request contains matrix values and supplier registration doesn't, the intake form is
prepopulated with data from the supplier request.
•If both supplier request and supplier registration don't contain any matrix values, the intake form is
prepopulated with default values provided in the template.
2. The system uses the following criteria to determine the list of modular questionnaires applicable to the process
project:
1. Questionnaire type: modular questionnaires with types referenced by the process project template's
external tasks are potentially applicable.
2. Commodity, region, and department: among the potentially applicable modular questionnaires with
matching types, the ones with commodities, regions, and departments that match the selections in the
process intake form are applicable to the new process project.
3. The process initiator selects the questionnaires to include in the process project. Their options in this step are
controlled by settings in the modular questionnaire templates, and can include:
•Adding optional questionnaires. Required questionnaires are automatically included.
•Requesting updates for any reused questionnaires that require an update for the current process. A setting
in the modular questionnaire template determines whether this option is available to the process initiator
in this step or whether the questionnaire automatically generates update requests.
4. The process initiator changes the default recipients of the questionnaires as needed and sends them.
The process initiator (project owner) is the default recipient of internal questionnaires. The supplier's primary
contact is the default recipient of external questionnaires.
5. The system:
•Creates the process

or
in this step or whether the questionnaire automatically generates update requests.
4. The process initiator changes the default recipients of the questionnaires as needed and sends them.
The process initiator (project owner) is the default recipient of internal questionnaires. The supplier's primary
contact is the default recipient of external questionnaires.
5. The system:
•Creates the process project.
•Creates any new modular questionnaires included in the process and invites their recipients to
submit them. New questionnaires can include questionnaires that are entirely new for the supplier or
questionnaires that require a new questionnaire project for each process.
•Sends update requests to recipients of any reused questionnaires for which the update request option is
either automatically or manually selected.
6. Internal recipients receive invitations to new internal questionnaires or requests to update reused internal
questionnaires and submit their responses in the supplier's profile.
7 . External recipients receive invitations to new external questionnaires or requests to update reused external
questionnaires. They create a new supplier account, create a new supplier user account, or sign into an existing
supplier user account as necessary on SAP Business Network and submit their responses.
8. Approvers for the modular questionnaire projects approve, deny, or request additional information for the
questionnaires or questionnaire updates. If they requested additional information, they then approve or deny
the edited questionnaires.
9. Completion of all included questionnaires starts the decision-making phase of the process project, which
occurs in one of the following two ways, depending on the project's configuration:
196 PUBLICManaging Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Supplier Qualifications,  Preferred Category Status, and Other Lifecycle Processes

•The system automatically sets a final  status for the process following rules based on the statuses of the
process's questionnaires. For example, a process can have a rule that automatically sets its final  status to
approved if all of its questionnaires are approved.
•A process decision-maker manually sets a final  status for the process after evaluating its questionnaires.
The decision-maker can optionally edit the intake form while setting the process status.
10. If the process is a qualification  process, the system publishes the supplier's status for consumption in other
areas such as sourcing events, contract workspaces, or guided buying. If the process has an expiration
schedule, that schedule starts.
11. (Optional) After the status is first  set, process decision-makers can change it, and can edit the intake form
when changing a status decision. For example, if a process is approved but an important questionnaire in the
process has expired without an update, a decision-maker can change the status to conditionally approved or
denied and change the expiration schedule.
12. (Optional) A process initiator renews the process [page 214] . Renewal can occur at any time once a process
has a final  status, but typical reasons include:
•The process is expiring or has expired.
•The process itself hasn't expired, but conditions in underlying questionnaires, such as expiration or denial
of update to a critical questionnaire, warrant a systematic re-evaluation rather than a simple status
change.
13. (Optional) A process initiator discontinues the process [page 205] .
Currently, only qualification  processes can be discontinued. The qualification  process to be discontinued must
be in the approved or conditionally approved status.
The process initiator can cancel an ongoing discontinuation process or renew a discontinued supplier
management process in the supplier's profile.  The decision-maker can make a decision for a discontinuation
process in the supplier's profile.  The administrator in the buyer organization can perform all these operations
through data import tasks.

Creating a Qualification  or Miscellaneous Supplier Management
Process Project
Use these steps to create a qualification  or miscellaneous process project to evaluate a supplier for a specific
combination of commodities, regions, and departments based on information in modular questionnaires.
Prerequisites
The process project feature (SM-16798) must be enabled in your site.
T o create a qualification  or miscellaneous process project, you must be a member of both the SM Modular
Questionnaire Manager  global user group and a project group with the Process Initiator  role in the process project
template.
If the supplier doesn't have a registration status of Registered , you can only create a
qualification  process for them if an administrator has enabled the self-service configuration  parameter
Application.SM.Process.AllowStartOfProcessQualificationForUnRegisteredSuppliers  
SM Admistration
Configuration  Parameters
  in your site. Y ou can always start a miscellaneous processes for a supplier regardless
of their registration status.
Managing Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Supplier Qualifications,  Preferred Category Status, and Other Lifecycle Processes PUBLIC 197

Y ou can't request an update for a questionnaire during process project creation if the questionnaire is in Pending
Submission  or Pending Approval  status.
T o add, edit, or delete a supplier contact in a supplier's profile  or when inviting a supplier to a questionnaire, you
must be a member of one of the following groups:
•Supplier Registration Manager
•SM Ops Administrator
•Customer Administrator
•Category Manager
•Commodity Manager
•Event Administrator
•Limited Event Administrator
•Sourcing Project Administrator
•Supplier Project Administrator
•Supplier/Customer Manager
•Supplier/Customer Agent
Members of the Supplier Qualification  Manager  group can add, but not edit or delete, supplier contacts in the
supplier's profile  and when inviting a supplier to a questionnaire.
Context
Depending on how your organization uses process projects, you can create a qualification  or miscellaneous
lifecycle process:
•Qualification  process projects are tied to the SAP Ariba qualification  lifecycle stage. Y ou create a qualification
process project in either the Qualifications  or Processes  detail area of the supplier profile.
•Miscellaneous process projects aren't tied to a specific  SAP Ariba lifecycle stage. Y our organization can
use them to define  processes that meet your organization's particular business requirements. Y ou create a
miscellaneous process project in the Processes  detail area of the supplier profile.
A process project is always based on a combination of commodities, regions, and departments. A supplier can only
have one process of each process type for each unique combination. Commodities, regions, and departments are
hierarchical, with higher and lower levels structured in parent-child relationships. Y ou can create multiple process
projects with the same process type for combinations that include parents or children of the commodities, regions,
and departments used in an existing process.
For example, if a supplier has an existing qualification  process for equipment in the United States for the
manufacturing department, you can't create another qualification  process project for that exact combination.
However, you can create a separate qualification  process project for equipment in Florida for the manufacturing
department (one level lower in the region hierarchy), or equipment in North America for the manufacturing
department (one level

rocess for equipment in the United States for the
manufacturing department, you can't create another qualification  process project for that exact combination.
However, you can create a separate qualification  process project for equipment in Florida for the manufacturing
department (one level lower in the region hierarchy), or equipment in North America for the manufacturing
department (one level higher in the region department), or laboratory equipment in Germany for the
manufacturing department (one level lower in the commodity hierarchy and a different  branch of the region
hierarchy).
The commodities, regions, and departments you specify in the process intake form determine which modular
questionnaires the process project uses to gather information from internal or external recipients.
If the type of process project you're creating can expire, the intake form includes questions that set an expiration
schedule for the process. If these questions are required, the process always expires at the frequency you set in
198 PUBLICManaging Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Supplier Qualifications,  Preferred Category Status, and Other Lifecycle Processes

the schedule. If they're optional, the process only expires if you set an expiration schedule. If your process intake
form includes questions about an expiration schedule, it can also include questions that set an expiration reminder
schedule. The expiration reminder schedule determines when the process automatically moves to expiring status
and sends notifications  to suppliers and internal process stakeholders.
The templates for the modular questionnaires included in a process project determine whether they can be reused
in multiple process projects for the same supplier, or whether each new process project requires a separate, new
questionnaire for the supplier. If the process includes reusable questionnaires that have already been assigned to
suppliers or internal recipients, you have the option of requesting updates to those questionnaires for your new
process.
Procedure
1. In the Qualifications  or Processes  detail area of the supplier's profile,  choose the add icon (  ).
2. On the Process type  menu, choose the type of process you want to create.
3. In the process intake form, enter the following information:
•Answers to any available questions about the commodities, regions, and departments to which the
process applies. This information determines which modular questionnaires the process project uses to
gather information from external and internal recipients. If the question is optional and you leave it blank,
the answer defaults to "All."
 Note
Matrix values (commodities, regions, departments, and material) in modular questionnaires are
prepopulated with entries from the intake form when process initiators create a qualification  or
miscellaneous supplier management process. This behavior also holds true when process initiators
create a qualification  or miscellaneous process for several suppliers at a time.
•An expiration schedule and an expiration reminder schedule, if available. Both schedules are defined  by
a set of two questions, one for frequency and one for offset,  which combine to create the schedule. For
example, an expiration frequency of months and an expiration offset  of 6 means that the process expires 6
months after its decision is made. An expiration reminder frequency of months and an expiration reminder
offset  of 5 means that the process generates expiration reminder notifications  and moves to expiring
status 5 months

quency and one for offset,  which combine to create the schedule. For
example, an expiration frequency of months and an expiration offset  of 6 means that the process expires 6
months after its decision is made. An expiration reminder frequency of months and an expiration reminder
offset  of 5 means that the process generates expiration reminder notifications  and moves to expiring
status 5 months after the decision and 1 month before the scheduled expiration.
•Any other questions included in the intake form.

Discontinuing a Qualification  Process
Use these steps to discontinue an existing qualification  process when a supplier no longer meets the standards or
requirements of your organization. Discontinuing a qualification  process is a disqualification.
Prerequisites
•Y ou must be a member of both the SM Modular Questionnaire Manager  global user group and the Process
Initiator  group of the existing qualification  process to be discontinued.
•The qualification  process to be discontinued must be in the approved or conditionally approved status. Y ou
can't discontinue a denied or in progress qualification  process.
Context
 Note
Discontinuation processes are designed to be internal processes that only involve people in your
organization. Choosing external modular questionnaires for a discontinuation process notifies  the supplier
of the discontinuation when they receive invitations or other notifications  for included external modular
questionnaires.
Y ou can fully or partially discontinue an existing qualification  process depending on the commodity, region, and
department combination you choose when starting a discontinuation process:
•T o fully discontinue a qualification  process, don't change the prefilled  combination of categories, regions, and
departments.
•T o partially discontinue a qualification  process, you can choose the subset combination of prefilled  categories,
regions, and departments.
When creating a discontinuation process, keep the following points in mind:
•Y ou can only create one discontinuation process with the same category, region, and department combination.
•After starting a partial discontinuation for an existing qualification  process, you can still fully discontinue the
existing qualification  process. After the full discontinuation, the partial discontinuation isn’t affected.
•For two existing qualification  processes with the parent-child relationship, if you start a discontinuation process
having the child relationship with both two processes, this

er starting a partial discontinuation for an existing qualification  process, you can still fully discontinue the
existing qualification  process. After the full discontinuation, the partial discontinuation isn’t affected.
•For two existing qualification  processes with the parent-child relationship, if you start a discontinuation process
having the child relationship with both two processes, this discontinuation process is nested under its parent,
not the grandparent, regardless of under which qualification  process you create the discontinuation process.

regions, and departments used in the discontinuation process.
If the intake form asks for an expiration schedule and expiration reminder schedule and you answer these
questions, the discontinuation process expires at the time you set. The process then automatically moves to
expiring status and sends notifications  to contacts before the discontinuation expires. After the discontinuation
process expires, the process status changes to expired. Expired discontinuation processes can be renewed.
Renewing a discontinuation process creates a new qualification  process with the same categories, regions, and
departments used in the discontinuation process.

Filling Out an Internal Questionnaire for a Supplier Management
Process
If you're the recipient of an internal questionnaire in a supplier management process, follow these steps to either fill
it out and submit it or specify a different  recipient so that the process can be completed.
Prerequisites
T o fill out an internal modular questionnaire, you must be its current recipient.
The process project feature (SM-16798) must be enabled in your site.
Managing Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Supplier Qualifications,  Preferred Category Status, and Other Lifecycle Processes PUBLIC 207

Context
Y ou can receive a request to fill out an internal questionnaire for a new process, or during the renewal of an existing
process. The process can be a qualification  process, in which case the renewal is a requalification,  or it can be a
miscellaneous process.
If you're the first  recipient for the questionnaire, you fill it out and submit it for the first  time.
If you're the recipient of an existing questionnaire for which there's an update request, either you're the original
recipient of the questionnaire or the process owner has reassigned the questionnaire to you for an update. In either
case, you edit any existing answers that require an update and resubmit the questionnaire. When updating an
existing questionnaire, you can see its version history.
If the feature for internal forms in modular questionnaire projects (SM-30222) is enabled in your site, modular
questionnaire projects can also include one or more internal forms. If you're the recipient of an internal
questionnaire, you fill out and submit the key questionnaire in the project. T o edit internal forms, see Filling out

•Navigate to the Questionnaires  detail area of the supplier's profile  and open the questionnaire.

3. Choose Save  to save your responses in draft mode, and return later to complete and submit them. Saved draft

By default, only the process owner can edit internal questionnaires and save them as drafts. However, the
process owner can request an update from a different  recipient and invite them to update the questionnaire.

the process.

Context

your organization. A supplier can have multiple processes that include the same questionnaire. The questionnaire's

Canceling a Qualification  or Other Process in the Supplier's Profile
Use these steps to cancel a qualification  or other supplier management process that's in progress so that you can
create a new process or process renewal for the same combination of commodities, regions, and departments.
Canceling an in-progress process removes or reverts it.
Prerequisites
In the supplier's profile,  you can only cancel a new process or process renewal that is in progress. Once a decision
has been made for the process or process renewal and it has an approved, conditionally approved, or denied status,
you can't cancel it. An administrator in your organization can cancel processes for which decisions have been made
using data import.
T o cancel a process associated with a project, you must be the project owner. For processes created through data
import, you can cancel them if you have the necessary permissions to view the supplier profile.
The process project feature (SM-16798) must be enabled in your site.
Context
A supplier can only have one process of each process type for a specific  combination of commodities, regions, and
departments. Canceling a new, in-progress process allows you to remove it from the supplier so that a process
initiator can create a new process of that type with the same combination. For example, if a template creator in
your organization has updated the process project template, you can cancel an in-progress process to create a new
one that uses the new version of the template. Or if a process project stalled out or was abandoned while it was in
progress, you can cancel it to start fresh with a new one.
Canceling a process renewal that is in progress reverts the process to the state (questionnaire and intake form
content, expiration schedule) and status it had before the renewal started, and can be used for the same purposes.
Processes can be based on a process project, or they can be created using a data import. If the process is based
on a project and is new, canceling it also cancels any modular questionnaires included in the process that meet
cancellation criteria. If the process is based on a project and has a renewal in progress, canceling the renewal also
cancels any modular questionnaires added during the current renewal that meet cancellation criteria.
Modular questionnaires are canceled during process cancellation under the following

If a process is based on a data import, there are no modular questionnaires associated with it.

Workflow  for Renewing a Qualification  or Other Supplier
Management Process
Process initiators renew a process by updating questions on its intake form, selecting additional questionnaires to
include, and deciding whether to request updates for existing questionnaires. For qualification  processes, a renewal
is a requalification.
1. In a supplier's profile,  a process initiator selects an existing process to renew.
2. The system loads the intake form from the currently published version of the process project template and
populates it with existing answers.
3. The process initiator edits the intake form as needed. They can't change the process's commodities, regions,
or departments during renewal, but can edit other answers, such as answers that set the expiration schedule
and expiration reminder schedule.
4. A renewed process always includes all previously added questionnaires. The system uses the following criteria
to determine the list of additional

The process initiator edits the intake form as needed. They can't change the process's commodities, regions,
or departments during renewal, but can edit other answers, such as answers that set the expiration schedule
and expiration reminder schedule.
4. A renewed process always includes all previously added questionnaires. The system uses the following criteria
to determine the list of additional modular questionnaires applicable to the renewed process project:
214 PUBLICManaging Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Supplier Qualifications,  Preferred Category Status, and Other Lifecycle Processes

1. Questionnaire type: modular questionnaires with types references by the process project template are
potentially applicable, including questionnaire types added to the process project template since the
process was first  created.
2. Commodity, region, and department: among the potentially applicable modular questionnaires with
matching types, the ones with commodities, regions, and departments that match the selections in the
process intake form are applicable to the process project.
5. The process initiator selects any additional questionnaires to include in the process project renewal. Their
options in this step are controlled by settings in the modular questionnaire templates, and can include:
•Adding optional questionnaires. Required questionnaires are automatically included.
•Requesting updates for any reused questionnaires that require an update for the process renewal. A
setting in the modular questionnaire template determines whether this option is available to the process
initiator in this step or whether the questionnaire automatically generates update requests.
6. The process initiator changes the recipients of the questionnaires as needed and sends them.
7 . The system:
•Creates any new modular questionnaires included in the process and invites their recipients to
submit them. New questionnaires can include questionnaires that are entirely new for the supplier or
questionnaires that require a new questionnaire project for each process.
•Sends update requests to recipients of any reused questionnaires for which that option is either
automatically or manually selected.
8. Internal recipients receive invitations to new internal questionnaires or requests to update reused internal
questionnaires and submit their responses in the supplier's 360° profile.
9. External recipients receive invitations to new external questionnaires or requests to update reused external
questionnaires. They create a new supplier account, create a new supplier user account, or sign into an existing
supplier user account as necessary on SAP Business Network and submit their responses.
10. Approvers for the modular questionnaire projects approve, deny, or request additional information for the
questionnaires or questionnaire updates. If they requested additional information, they then approve or deny
the edited questionnaires.
11. Completion of the final  included

unt, or sign into an existing
supplier user account as necessary on SAP Business Network and submit their responses.
10. Approvers for the modular questionnaire projects approve, deny, or request additional information for the
questionnaires or questionnaire updates. If they requested additional information, they then approve or deny
the edited questionnaires.
11. Completion of the final  included questionnaire that isn't already completed starts the decision-making phase of
the renewal, which occurs in one of the following two ways, depending on the project's configuration:
•The system automatically sets a final  status for the process renewal following rules based on the statuses
of the process's questionnaires. For example, a process can have a rule that automatically sets its final
status to approved if all of its questionnaires are approved.
•A process decision-maker manually sets a final  status for the process renewal after evaluating its
questionnaires. The decision-maker can optionally edit the intake form while setting the status.
12. If the process is a qualification  process, the system publishes the supplier's updated status for consumption in
other areas such as sourcing events, contract workspaces, or guided buying. If the process has an expiration
schedule, that schedule restarts from the beginning.

Renewing a Qualification  or Other Supplier Management Process
Follow these steps to renew a qualification  or other supplier management process. Renewing a process allows you
to gather updated information about it and potentially revise an earlier decision, including restarting an expired or
denied process. For qualification  processes, renewing means requalifying the supplier.
Prerequisites
T o renew a process, you must be a member of both the SM Modular Questionnaire Manager  global user group
and a project group with the Process Initiator  role in the associated process project template.
Y ou can't renew a process that's in progress. Y ou can only renew a process that has a status corresponding to a
decision (approved, conditionally approved, or denied) or that’s expiring or has expired.
T o request an update while the questionnaire is in Pending Submission  or Pending Approval  status, you must
enable the parameter Application.SM.AllowRequestUpdateOnPendingModularQuestionnaireStatuses . For more
information, see Allow request update on pending modular questionnaire statuses [page 285] .
The process project feature (SM-16798) must be enabled in your site.
T o add, edit, or delete a supplier contact in a supplier's profile  or when inviting a supplier to a questionnaire, you
must be a member of one of the following groups:
•Supplier Registration Manager
•SM Ops Administrator
•Customer Administrator
•Category Manager
•Commodity Manager
•Event Administrator
•Limited Event Administrator
•Sourcing Project Administrator
•Supplier Project Administrator
•Supplier/Customer Manager
•Supplier/Customer Agent
Members of the Supplier Qualification  Manager  group can add, but not edit or delete, supplier contacts in the
supplier's profile  and when inviting a supplier to a questionnaire.
Context
Process renewals always use exactly the same commodities, regions, and departments as the previous process. A
process renewal can be based on a previous process achieved through either of the following methods:
•An imported process status.  In this case, the renewal creates a new process project for the commodities,
regions, and departments associated with the imported status. Since imported processes don't have any
associated questionnaires, renewing the process means selecting the modular questionnaires to include for
216 PUBLICManaging Suppliers and Supplier

ed through either of the following methods:
•An imported process status.  In this case, the renewal creates a new process project for the commodities,
regions, and departments associated with the imported status. Since imported processes don't have any
associated questionnaires, renewing the process means selecting the modular questionnaires to include for

•A previous process project.  In this case, the renewal restarts the previous process project. The project already

recipients and request updates as part of the renewal. If there are optional questionnaires available for the
process, you can choose whether or not to include them in the renewal. Y ou can also set a new reminder and
expiration reminder schedule for the process, if they're available.
Since process renewals allow you to resend questionnaires, you can use them to collect updated information.
Decision-makers can use those updates to reassess the previous process decision, or they can leave the previous
decision in place.
The renewal always includes any questionnaires previously included in the process. Y ou can also add other
questionnaires that either were available but weren't previously included or that have been added since the
previous process was completed and match the process's criteria.

a set of two questions, one for frequency and 1 month for offset,  which combine to create the schedule.
For example, an expiration frequency of months and an expiration offset  of 6 means that the process
expires 6 months after its decision is made. An expiration reminder frequency of months and an expiration
reminder offset  of 5 means that the process generates expiration reminder notifications  and moves to
expiring status 5 months after the decision and one before the scheduled expiration.
•Any other questions included in the intake form.

Making a Status Decision for a Supplier Management Process
If you're a decision-maker for a process, use these steps to set or update its status to conditionally approved,
approved, or denied based on the most recent information in its questionnaires.
Prerequisites
T o make a status decision for a process project, you must be a member of a project group with the Decision Maker
role.
The process must be in a pending decision, approved, denied, or conditionally approved status.
The process project feature (SM-16798) must be enabled in your site.
Context
A process can be configured  so that its final  status is set automatically based on the statuses of its included
modular questionnaires. Otherwise, a decision-maker reviews the process and its questionnaires and manually sets
its status.
This status isn't permanent. A decision-maker can edit it at any time unless the process currently has a renewal in
progress or has expired. Changing a status decision is a more lightweight alternative to process renewal, which can
require updates to and re-evaluation of process questionnaires.
For example, if an important questionnaire in a qualification  process is expiring and the recipient hasn’t updated it
yet, a decision-maker for that process can update its status from an approved status to a conditionally approved
status. If the recipient allows the questionnaire to expire without updating it, the decision-maker can update
the process status again to change it from a conditionally approved status to a denied status. Comments allow
decision-makers to describe why they made a particular decision, and the decision history table on the process
details page shows all the status changes and the reasons behind them.
While you're making a status decision, most of the questions in the process intake form are editable and you can
change their answers. Saving your decision also saves any changes you made to the intake form. If the intake form
includes questions about the process's expiration schedule or expiration reminder schedule, you can edit those
schedules while making or updating a decision. For example, if you decide to conditionally approve the process
instead of fully approving it, you can shorten the expiration schedule so that the process comes up for renewal
sooner. The only questions that you can't edit on the intake form are the ones about the process's

cess's expiration schedule or expiration reminder schedule, you can edit those
schedules while making or updating a decision. For example, if you decide to conditionally approve the process
instead of fully approving it, you can shorten the expiration schedule so that the process comes up for renewal
sooner. The only questions that you can't edit on the intake form are the ones about the process's commodities,
regions, and departments. Those answers are set when the process is created and can't be changed.
Y our organization's process setup determines the names of the statuses in each process.
If the process has been set up to allow decision-making in an external system, the Make decision  button is locked
while that external decision-making is in progress. Once it's completed, you can once again set a status for the
process on the process details page.
Y ou can choose  View all decision makers  in the Process status  area of the details page to see the list of
decision-makers along with their email addresses, allowing you to contact the decision-maker when the decision is
delayed or pending. As a decision-maker you can use Make Decision  to make a status decision for the project and
use View all decision makers  to view the list of decision-makers for the process project.

Procedure
1. Perform one of the following actions:
•Choose the link in the email notification  informing you that the process is ready for a status decision.

choose the view icon (  ) to open its details page.
2. In the Process status  area, perform one of the following actions:

•T o update an existing status, choose Change .
If you choose Change , the application displays an information message. This message informs you that the
preferred status, if any, will be removed for the existing commodity, region, and department combination if
you set the process status to Conditionally Qualified  or Denied . However, the application doesn't display the
message when you attempt to change a status decision while renewing or discontinuing the process.
3. Choose the status that you want to set.
4. Enter a required comment about your decision.
5.Optional: Edit the process intake form to change answers as needed.
6. Choose Save .
Results
The process shows the currently set status. The primary contact , the process project owner, and the specific
decision-maker for the project receive notifications  informing them of the status change.
The Process status  area shows the decision history for the process, including the name of the decision-maker who
set the status and their comments. Currently, decision history doesn't include information about any changes to
the intake form made with the decisions.
If the process is a qualification  process, changing its status from approved to conditionally approved or denied
doesn't affect  any preferred category statuses associated with the previous approved status. An approved
discontinuation for the process does automatically remove the associated preferred category status.
Whenever you make a status decision, a new version of the process intake form is created. At a later point in time,

Excel. T o export a

discontinuation for the process does automatically remove the associated preferred category status.

Preferred Supplier Management
Preferred supplier management vets suppliers' suitability for a preferred category status for specific  commodities
for specific  regions and (if your organization uses them) departments. Y our organization can use up to five different
preferred category statuses.
Suppliers must be qualified  for a combination of commodities, regions, and departments before they can be
preferred.

Preferred Category Statuses Management Projects
After the supplier is qualified  for a specific  commodity, region, and (optionally) department combination, a
category or supplier manager can designate them as a preferred supplier for that combination.
Although a customer administrator can set preferred statuses for suppliers using a data import operation in both
SAP Ariba Supplier Management solutions, preferred supplier management projects are only available in solutions
that include SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and Performance.
Supplier preferred category status is always based on a combination of commodity and region. It can also be based
on departments if the department feature for supplier management projects is enabled in your site.
The preferred supplier management project template determines:
•What information users add to the preferred category status request form.
•Whether the preferred category status request requires approval, and if so, who the approvers are.
•Whether other stakeholders in your organization must also approve the status change.
Users request a preferred category status for a new commodity, region, and department combination, or a change
to an existing status, in the Preferred  detail area of the supplier's profile  on the Supplier Management  dashboard.
Up to five status levels are supported, including the preferred level. They also remove preferred status levels there.
 Note
•Y ou can't combine multiple qualified  combinations in the same preferred status request. For example, if a
supplier has separate qualifications  for Electronic reference material  and Printed media  in the Canada
region, you must create individual preferred category status requests for each category.
•If your site uses process qualifications,  you can request preferred category status for any valid process
qualification  or a subset.
If the preferred supplier management workflow  includes an approval flow,  notifications  let the approver know that
the preferred status change request requires approval; let the requesting user know when the preferred status
request has been approved or denied;s and show the final  approver so that requesting users can have further
discussions about denied preferred status requests.
In solutions that include SAP Ariba Sourcing, event templates can be set up to automatically invite the suppliers
that are preferred for the

ed status change request requires approval; let the requesting user know when the preferred status
request has been approved or denied;s and show the final  approver so that requesting users can have further
discussions about denied preferred status requests.
In solutions that include SAP Ariba Sourcing, event templates can be set up to automatically invite the suppliers
that are preferred for the event's commodities and regions. Department isn't considered for automatic event
invitation.

About the Preferred  Detail Area in the Supplier Profile
Preferred category statuses indicate the desirability of using a supplier for specific  combinations of commodities,
regions, and departments. Y ou view and manage supplier preferred category statuses in the Preferred  detail area
of the supplier profile.
Preferred category statuses are a set of labels that your organization uses to indicate the desirability of using a
supplier for a specific  combination of commodities, regions, and departments. Whether or not your organization
uses preferred supplier management depends on its processes.
Preferred supplier management projects are only available in solutions that include SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle
and Performance. The Preferred  detail area only shows in a supplier's profile  after the supplier is qualified  for at
least one commodity, region, and department.
Y ou can do the following things in the Preferred  detail area of the supplier profile:
•View a summary list of all of the supplier's preferred category statuses.
•View the preferred category request for a status by choosing   (view).
•Request a new or updated preferred category status [page 225]  for a supplier.
•Remove a preferred category status [page 228]  from a supplier.
In the preferred category request, you can approve or deny the task by choosing   (take action).
 Note
There is no advanced view for preferred supplier management projects.

Requesting a New or Updated Preferred Category Status
for a Supplier
Y ou can request a new preferred category status for a supplier to indicate the desirability of doing business with
them for a specific  combination of commodities, regions, and departments, or request a status change based on
changing circumstances.
Prerequisites
T o request a new or updated preferred category status for a supplier, you must be a member of the Preferred
Supplier Manager  or SM Ops Administrator  groups.
Preferred supplier management projects are only available in solutions that include SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle
and Performance.
Y ou can only request a new preferred category status for a combination of commodities, regions, and departments
for which the supplier is already fully qualified.  Y ou can't request a preferred category status based on less than full
qualification:
•If your organization manages supplier qualifications  using supplier qualification  and disqualification  projects,
you can't request a preferred category status based on a partial qualification  that has one or more subordinate
disqualifications.  For example, if a supplier is qualified  for clothing in the United States for the marketing
department but disqualified  for shirts in Florida, their qualification  is partial and you can't request a preferred
category status based on it.
•If your organization manages supplier qualifications  using qualification  process projects, you can't request
a preferred category status based on an approved qualification  process that has 1 or more subordinate
qualification  processes that are denied. Y ou also can't request a preferred category status based on a
qualification  process that's conditionally approved.
Context
Preferred category statuses encourage the procurement and sourcing agents in your organization to do business
with certain suppliers for specific  commodities, regions, and departments. Depending on how your site is set up,
suppliers with the highest preferred category status can appear at the top of guided buying search results or be
invited

ication  process that's conditionally approved.
Context
Preferred category statuses encourage the procurement and sourcing agents in your organization to do business
with certain suppliers for specific  commodities, regions, and departments. Depending on how your site is set up,
suppliers with the highest preferred category status can appear at the top of guided buying search results or be
invited automatically to participate in sourcing events.

Procedure

preferred category status.
2. Choose the supplier's name to open their profile.
3. Perform one of the following actions:
•In the supplier's profile  summary, choose Preferred  in the Quick Links  panel.
•In the classic supplier 360° profile,  choose Preferred  in the left-hand navigation bar.
The Preferred  detail area of the supplier's profile  opens.
4. Perform one of the following actions:
•T o request a new preferred category status, choose   (add).
•T o request a change to an existing preferred category status, locate the status and choose  (more
options) > Edit   .
5. If you're requesting a new preferred category status, select one or more commodities, regions, and (if your
organization uses them) departments by performing one of the following actions:
•Choose Search  and enter search terms in the field,  then select a value from the list of search results.
•Choose Browse  and use the arrows (   ) to navigate through the hierarchy, then select values to include in
the request.
Y our choices are limited to the values for which the supplier is already fully qualified.
6. Choose the status level to request.
7 . Enter any other required information in the form.
8. Choose Submit .
Results
The request, with its current status, shows in the Preferred  detail area. If there's an approval task for it, that task
starts now.

Removing a Preferred Category Status from a Supplier
If a supplier is no longer preferred for a specific  combination of commodities, regions, and departments, you can
remove their preferred status by deleting it.
Prerequisites
T o remove a supplier preferred category status, you must be a member of the Preferred Supplier Manager  or SM
Ops Administrator  groups.
Preferred supplier management projects are only available in solutions that include SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle
and Performance.
Y ou can't remove a preferred category status if its request is still in approval. Y ou can only remove approved
category statuses.
Context
Deleting a preferred category status never requires approval.
If your organization manages supplier qualifications  using qualification  and disqualification  projects, when a
supplier is disqualified  for a combination of commodities, regions, and departments, any preferred statuses
associated with the previous qualification  are removed from the supplier automatically.
228 PUBLICManaging Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Supplier Qualifications,  Preferred Category Status, and Other Lifecycle Processes

If your organization manages supplier qualifications  using process projects, changing the status of a qualification
process from approved to conditionally approved or denied doesn't affect  any preferred statuses associated
with the previous approved status. A discontinuation of the qualification  process does automatically remove any
associated preferred category statuses from the supplier.
When a preferred category status is automatically removed from a supplier because of a disqualification,  the
Preferred  detail area of their supplier profile  shows the disqualification  status instead.
Procedure
1. Locate the supplier for which you want to remove a preferred category status on the Supplier Management
dashboard.
2. Choose the supplier's name to open their profile.
3. Perform one of the following actions:
•In the supplier's profile  summary, choose Preferred  in the Quick Links  panel.
•In the classic supplier 360° profile,  choose Preferred  in the left-hand navigation bar.
The Preferred  detail area of the supplier's profile  opens.
4. Locate the status and choose   (more options) > Delete    .
5. Choose Delete .
Results
After you delete a preferred category status, the supplier is no longer eligible for sourcing events that use preferred
status as a criteria. Y ou can request a new preferred category status for the same supplier and combination of
commodities, regions, and departments at any time.

How Supplier Performance Management (SPM)
Projects Fit into the Supplier Management Workflow
T o measure supplier performance, your organization can use classic Supplier Performance Management (SPM)
projects.
The features associated with SPM projects include:
•Scorecards for grading suppliers. Scorecards can include data from surveys responses, report data, and data
added directly to the scorecard.
•Surveys for periodic evaluation of suppliers by internal and external stakeholders.
•Performance criteria translated into key performance indicators (KPIs) that map between SPM scorecards and
surveys. Y ou can store KPIs in the Sourcing Library  for use across multiple projects.
•Analytical reporting tools.
SPM projects are standalone projects that use the classic project interface. They don't show on the
Supplier Management  dashboard and aren't linked in any way to supplier request, registration, qualification,
disqualification,  preferred supplier management, or process projects.
For information on running supplier performance management reviews using SPM projects, see Running Supplier
Performance Management Reviews .

Modular Questionnaire and Supplier Certificate
Management
Modular questionnaires are projects that maintain discrete sets of related information, such as certificates,
risk assessments, or policy, compliance, audit, or other information about suppliers or engagement risk. They
can function independently or serve as components in a larger process such as a process qualification  or an
engagement risk assessment.
Modular questionnaires are the recommended way to collect supplier certificates.  They include some special
features for certificate  management, including features for managing expiring and expired certificates.

Y ou view and manage modular questionnaires for a supplier in the Questionnaires  detail area of the supplier's

Questionnaires  tab.

If a modular questionnaire project only includes a questionnaire, viewing it directly opens the questionnaire details
page with questions, answers, and version history. If the process project feature (SM-16798) is enabled in your site,
questionnaire details for the project or key questionnaire also include a summary of the process projects in which

If a modular questionnaire project also includes internal forms, viewing it opens the questionnaire project details

The questionnaire project details page includes:

Supplier Certificate  Management
Supplier management questionnaires can collect certificate  information from suppliers. Y ou can view a supplier's
certificates  and their expiration status in the Certificates  area of the supplier profile.
If your site uses the supplier profile  summary (NGSM-2959), it includes a Certificates  panel that shows the
number of Valid , Expiring , and Expired  certificates  for the supplier that your organization has collected. The
Certificates  area shows more certificate  details.
Any internal or external supplier management questionnaire can include certificate  questions. The Certificates
detail area of a supplier's profile  shows all certificates  associated with a supplier that were collected in approved
questionnaires or questionnaire updates. Each certificate  has a summary with the certificate  name, expiration date,
and a status of Valid , Expiring , or Expired .
234 PUBLICManaging Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Modular Questionnaire and Supplier Certificate  Management

T o be included in the Certificates  detail area of a supplier's profile,  a certificate  must be collected using either of
the following methods:
•Internal or external questionnaires using Certificate  questions mapped to the vendor.certificate  field  in
the supplier database, including request, registration, and qualification  questionnaires.
•Modular questionnaires using Certificate  questions or certificate  sections with mapped certificate  questions.
In either case, the questionnaire must be approved. Certificates  that were added in denied questionnaires or
questionnaire updates aren't included in the Certificates  area.
If your site uses the supplier profile  summary (NGSM-2729) and the supplier is public (has a linked SAP Business
Network account), a separate Business Network Certificates  panel shows the certificates  the supplier has added
to their SAP Business Network profile.  Choosing that panel opens the Certifications  area of the supplier's
SAP Business Network profile.  The number of certificates  shown in the Certificates  panel (collected by your
organization in approved questionnaires) and in the Business Network Certificates  panel (self-reported by the
supplier on SAP Business Network) can be the same in some cases, or can differ.  See About the Supplier Profile
Summary [page 14]  for details.
In the Certificates  detail area, you can view certificate  details by performing the following actions:
•Search for a certificate  by entering the certificate  type in the search bar. Y ou can enter all or part of the type to
start the search.
•Filter certificates  by any combination of type, status, effective  date, whether or not the certificate  has expired,
and the commodities, regions, and departments to which it applies. After selecting one or more filter  values in
the filter  popup, choose Apply  to apply them to the Certificates  area. T o clear filters,  in the filter  popup, choose
Clear all , then choose Apply .
•Sort certificates  by last updated, recently expired, or oldest expired.
•Choose any certificate  summary card to open complete certificate  details, including links for downloading the
certificate  attachment and opening the questionnaire where the certificate  was collected. Certificate  details
Managing Suppliers and Supplier Lifecycles
Modular Questionnaire and Supplier Certificate  Management PUBLIC 235

can include associated commodities, regions, and departments. However, that information is only valid for
certificates  collected in modular questionnaires, which create a direct relationship between the commodity,
region, and department of the modular questionnaire project and its associated certificates.
 Note
•SAP Ariba doesn't validate the details in certificate  answers (effective  date, expiration date, and so
on) against associated certificate  attachments. Approvers are responsible for comparing the certificate
attachment to the details in the certificate  answer to make sure that they match before approving the
associated questionnaire. Approvers can request additional information from the recipient to resolve any
discrepancies.
•If a questionnaire recipient submits a certificate  with a past expiration date, the certificate's  status can
show as Expiration in Progress  for a short time before changing to Expired .
Certificates  collected in modular questionnaires include some special features. A template creator in your site
sets up these certificate-related  modular questionnaires, which can include both certificate  and other types of
questions, usually for a specific  subject. Some certificate-related  modular questionnaires can ask for multiple
related certificates,  while other questionnaires can ask for one critical certificate.  A modular questionnaire
project can be applicable to all commodities, regions, and departments, or only to a specific  combination, and
the certificates  collected in it inherit that commodity, region, and department information from the modular
questionnaire. In addition to the expiration dates of individual certificates,  the modular questionnaire project where
they're collected can also expire.
Managing supplier certificates  in modular questionnaires involves the following tasks:
•Sending suppliers certificate  questionnaires : you can ask suppliers to provide information about their
certificates  by inviting them to fill out the relevant modular questionnaires. Depending on your site's solution
package, these certificate-related  modular questionnaires can be standalone questionnaires, engagement risk
assessments, or included in a process project.
•Approving or denying certificate  questionnaires : If you’re an approver, you must review supplier answers and
approve or deny their certificate  questionnaires

to fill out the relevant modular questionnaires. Depending on your site's solution
package, these certificate-related  modular questionnaires can be standalone questionnaires, engagement risk
assessments, or included in a process project.
•Approving or denying certificate  questionnaires : If you’re an approver, you must review supplier answers and
approve or deny their certificate  questionnaires [page 243]  or questionnaire updates.
•Monitoring certificate  status : In addition to viewing certificates  in the Certificates  detail area of supplier
profiles,  you can monitor certificates  collected in modular questionnaires by:
•Receiving email notifications : depending on how a modular questionnaire is set up, if you're a supplier's
primary supplier manager or an owner of the questionnaire project, you can receive email notifications  for
upcoming and lapsed expirations. These notifications  can be for a specific  certificate  or for the modular
questionnaire project as a whole.
•Monitoring questionnaire status : you can view the status of modular questionnaire projects associated
with the supplier, as well as the questionnaires themselves, in the Questionnaires  detail area of the
supplier's profile.  A modular questionnaire project status of Expiring  or Expired  can mean that the
questionnaire project itself has an expiration schedule. Certificate  questions in modular questionnaires can
also be set up so that a certificate  expiration changes the questionnaire's status to Expiring  or Expired .
Certificate  expirations themselves are always based on the certificate  expiration date and aren't affected
by the status of the modular questionnaire project in which they're collected.
